steps.m: to point to or indicate something

steps.n: Root letters combined with fatha and shadda to make a word that asks 'which one?'
Added the calling particle يٰٓ to make it into a form of address
Added ها to make it specifically address a group
Basic vowel marks (fatha) added above each letter to make the root readable as a simple noun

steps.m: people (those who are social by nature)
steps.n: Root means 'being social' - changed to mean 'people' since humans are social by nature
Added ال (the) at the start to mean 'the people' instead of just 'people'

steps.m: the people (as the ones being addressed)
steps.n: Added damma to show this is who is being addressed in يٰٓأَيُّهَا (O you)
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Added اِتَّ at the beginning to show doing the action to oneself. The و changes to ت and combines with the added ت using shadda
Added وا to show multiple people (they) did the action
Added ا to help pronounce the word at the start
Adding fatha marks creates the basic past tense verb meaning 'he did something'

steps.m: your (all of you) Lord (the one who nurtures and sustains you)
steps.n: The root رَبَبَ became رَبّ (Lord/nurturer), then كُمُ was added to mean 'your (plural)'. The ُ shows this word is doing the action.
This is a relative pronoun that connects two parts of a sentence. The shadda on ل is due to sun letter assimilation with ذ.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. This word is describing Allah (referred to by ٱلَّذِى - 'the One who') in this ayah.
Added كُم at the end to show who was created. This is addressing all people (ٱلنَّاسُ) mentioned at the start of the ayah.
Basic form of the preposition that shows where something comes from or what it's made of
The shadda (ّ) is added because this word comes before another word that starts with ن (نَّفْسٍ). When مِن comes before words starting with ن, the ن sounds merge together for easier pronunciation.
Root meaning 'breath' becomes a noun meaning 'soul' by adding vowel marks
Adding the two dots below (kasratan) shows this word is indefinite and in the genitive case
The word is already in its final form and adding a shadda would change the meaning incorrectly
Changes from basic root to active participle form, meaning 'one'
Added ة to make it feminine
Changed to a special alif (looks like a small line above) and added two dots below to show it comes after 'from' (min)

steps.m: to create something from nothing, to make, to shape
he created (connected to خَلَقَكُم in previous phrase)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. This continues the story from the previous phrase where Allah created mankind (خَلَقَكُم)
Adding وَ (and) at the start connects this to the previous statement about creation, showing this is a continuation of Allah's creative acts
Basic preposition meaning 'from'. The sukun (ْ) is added to show where the word stops.
Added ها at the end to show this is happening to or coming from the soul (نَّفْسٍ) mentioned earlier in the verse.
Added vowel marks to make the root into a noun meaning one who is paired/joined. This word is being acted on by the verb خَلَقَ (created) in this ayah.
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb خَلَقَ (created)
Added هَا at the end to show this spouse belongs to her (referring back to نَّفْسٍ/soul mentioned earlier)

steps.m: he spread/scattered (connected to خَلَقَ - he created)
steps.n: Connected the letters with fatha vowels and added shadda to show emphasis. This verb continues the action from خَلَقَ (he created) in describing what Allah did.

steps.m: and he spread/scattered (connected to خَلَقَ - he created)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) to connect this action with the previous one (خَلَقَ - he created), showing these are related actions
Basic preposition meaning 'from'
Added هُ to show who/what is being referred to
Added مَا to show it refers to exactly two things/people. In this ayah, it refers to the first soul (نَّفْسٍ) and its mate (زَوْجَهَا) mentioned earlier.

steps.m: man, person (one who walks/stands on foot)

steps.n: Changes the singular word into a broken plural form to show multiple men. This is one of the most common plural patterns in Arabic.
Shows this word is receiving the action of scattering/spreading (وَبَثَّ) in the verse
Added vowels and ي to make an adjective meaning 'many' or 'much'
Added ًا to make it describe how the action was done. This word describes how Allah spread forth men (رِجَالًا) from Adam and Eve.
Changed from basic root to irregular plural form for 'women' by adding alif and hamza at the end
Added ً (tanween) to show this word is receiving the action of بَثَّ (spread forth)

steps.m: and women (as objects spread forth with رِجَالًا)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) to connect this with رِجَالًا (men) as things that Allah spread forth
Command form with وا at the end to tell multiple people to be mindful/protect themselves

steps.m: and protect yourselves, and be conscious (plural command)
steps.n: Adding conjunction 'and' to connect with previous phrase
Added ال to make it specific and changed to the unique form for Allah. This is a special change that only happens for this word.

steps.m: Allah (as the object being warned about/feared in اتَّقُوا)
steps.n: The word 'Allah' gets a stronger L sound (shadda) and an 'ah' ending because it's what we're being told to be mindful of

steps.m: possessor of, one who has (masculine singular)
steps.n: Base demonstrative word indicating possession or attribution

steps.m: the one who (connects خَلَقَكُم with رَبَّكُمُ)
steps.n: Adding ال with shadda changes this from a possessive word to a relative pronoun that connects رَبَّكُمُ (your Lord) with the action خَلَقَكُم (created you)
The ي is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create basic past tense verb
Added alif after first root letter to show intensive or repeated action
Added ت at beginning to show mutual action between people
Changed from past tense 'he asked' to present tense 'you all ask' by adding ون ending, and lengthened the middle alif
Added kasra to the letter ب to make it readable
Added هِ at the end to show this refers to Allah mentioned earlier in the ayah
Added small alif (ۦ) after هِ to help with pronunciation

steps.m: to show mercy and maintain family ties

steps.n: Adding vowels َ and ِ turns the root into a complete verb
Changes from verb 'showed mercy' to plural noun meaning 'mercies'
Added the definite article to specify these particular family relationships

steps.m: and the wombs/blood relations (connected to ٱللَّهَ)
steps.n: Added conjunction to connect this word to Allah in the phrase 'fear Allah and the wombs/family ties'
Basic form of the word that shows condition or possibility
Adding shadda changes this from a word meaning 'if' to one that adds emphasis to what follows. Here it emphasizes that Allah was watching over them.
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word in Arabic that always refers to The One True God.

steps.m: Allah (as the object being feared in ٱتَّقُوا۟)
steps.n: Allah is the one being feared - the fatha at the end shows this. The shadda makes the L sound stronger.
Added short 'a' sounds (fatha) to each letter to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he existed'

steps.m: he/it was (watching over in this ayah)
steps.n: The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas in a past tense verb. This is a common change with weak letters.
Basic preposition meaning 'upon' or 'on'
The ى changes to يْ when connecting to a pronoun
Added كُمْ at the end to show this applies to multiple people being addressed

steps.m: to watch over carefully or guard

steps.n: Added ي between second and third root letters to show someone who does this action as their role or characteristic. The pattern shows this is a strong or constant quality.

steps.m: a watchful guardian (describing how Allah was عَلَيْكُمْ/over you)
steps.n: Added tanween with alif (ًا) to show this word describes Allah's role in the sentence. It comes after كَانَ (was) and describes how Allah watches over people.
The long alif makes the meaning stronger and more direct
Changed from 'he gave' to 'you all give!' by dropping the ending and changing vowels to make it a command for a group
Added وا to make it a command for multiple people
Added و at the beginning to connect it with what comes before

steps.m: to lack a father while being young

steps.n: Changes from verb 'to lack a father' to noun 'orphan' by adding ي
Changed the word to show many orphans instead of just one

steps.m: the orphans (who are mentioned in وَءَاتُوا۟ - and give)
steps.n: Added ال to show these are specific orphans being talked about. This word is the object (receiver) of the verb وَءَاتُوا۟ (and give) in this ayah.
Root letters م-و-ل combine to make مال (maal), which means wealth/money
Changes the word to its broken plural form to show multiple properties or amounts of wealth
Added fatha to show this word is being acted upon by the verb تَأْكُلُوا (eat/consume)
Added pronoun suffix to show the wealth belongs to the orphans mentioned as الْيَتٰمَىٰ earlier in the verse
This is a negative particle that prevents something from happening. Here it stops the action of exchanging bad for good things that belong to orphans.

steps.m: and not (connects to previous command وَءَاتُوا۟ - give)
steps.n: Adding و at the beginning connects this prohibition to the previous command to give orphans their property. The و shows these are related instructions about dealing with orphans' wealth.

steps.m: to exchange or substitute one thing for another

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added ت at beginning and doubled middle letter to show doing action to oneself
Added تَ prefix for 'you' and changed final vowel to ُ for present tense

steps.m: you (all) exchange/take in substitution (الْخَبِيثَ for الطَّيِّبِ)
steps.n: Added وا to show multiple people are doing the action. In this ayah, it's warning against exchanging bad things for good things when dealing with orphans' property.
Removed تَ prefix from تَتَبَدَّلُوا to make تَبَدَّلُوا, both mean 'you all exchange'
Added vowels (fatha-damma-fatha) to make root into past tense verb meaning 'became bad'
Changed from a verb (action) to a describing word by adding ي in the middle
Added ال to make it specific
Added fatha at end to show this is the object being acted upon in the sentence
Added vowels to connect the letters and make it an adjective
The shadda intensifies the meaning to emphasize complete purity
Adding ال makes it specific

steps.m: with the very pure/good thing (object of تَتَبَدَّلُوا - exchange)
steps.n: The word is affected by two things: 1) the preposition بِ meaning 'with', and 2) it's the object being exchanged in تَتَبَدَّلُوا (do not exchange)
This is the basic negative particle that stops or prevents something. Here it's telling people not to do the action تَتَبَدَّلُوا (exchange/substitute).
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it, like saying 'and' in English
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show past tense
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Changed from 'he' to 'you' by replacing يَ with تَ

steps.m: you all eat/are eating (to a group)
steps.n: Added plural marker وا to show multiple people are being addressed
Root letters م-و-ل became مَال by adding the vowel marks - a common pattern for nouns meaning 'wealth'
Changed to broken plural form to show multiple properties or amounts of wealth
Changed alif to alif maqsurah for proper spelling

steps.m: their wealth (referring to the orphans' wealth mentioned in the ayah)
steps.n: Added pronoun suffix to show possession by the orphans mentioned earlier in the verse

This is a basic preposition that shows direction or destination. In this ayah, it connects أَمْوٰلَهُمْ (their wealth) to أَمْوٰلِكُمْ (your wealth), showing the transfer of wealth from one to another.
Added an alif maksura (ى with two dots) at the end to show this is the standard written form
Added a small alif (ٓ) above the final letter to help with pronunciation
Root letters م-و-ل became مَال by changing و to ا (alif) to make the basic word for wealth
Changed from single wealth to plural by adding أ at start and وا in middle - like changing 'money' to 'monies'
Added genitive case ending (like saying 'of wealth')
Added كُمْ at the end to show possession by multiple people
Adding shadda changes this from 'if' to a word that adds emphasis, like saying 'indeed' or 'certainly' in English
Added هُ at the end to show who/what is being talked about. This connects to the word كَانَ (was) in this ayah.
Added a small و mark (ۥ) after هُ to help with pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to be or exist in a certain state

steps.n: Added short 'a' vowels (fathas) to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he was'
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is a common change in hollow verbs

steps.m: to commit a serious sin or wrong

steps.n: Changed the action into a noun by adding damma, making it refer to the sin itself rather than doing it
Added tanween alif to show this is the object being described as 'great' in the phrase 'it was a great sin'
Added the letter ي between the second and third root letters with vowel marks to make it into a word describing something that has this quality
Added ًا at the end to show this word is describing the noun حُوبًا (sin) in the ayah

steps.m: if (introduces a condition about fearing unfairness with orphans)
steps.n: This is a conditional word that introduces the possibility of fearing unfairness when dealing with orphan girls in marriage. It connects to خِفْتُمْ (you feared) which comes after it.

steps.m: and if (connects the condition about orphans to previous guidance)
steps.n: The و connects this new condition about orphans to the previous guidance about marriage and fairness

steps.m: to fear or be afraid of something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
The middle weak letter و changes to ي and then drops when adding endings. The first vowel changes to kasra.
Added تُ to show who did the action
Changed from 'I feared' to 'you all feared' by adding مْ at the end
Starting with the two separate words that will combine. أَنْ means 'that' and لا means 'not'

steps.m: that not (subject of خِفْتُمْ - you feared)
steps.n: The ن from أَنْ combines with the ل from لا, creating a doubled ل (shown by the ّ mark). This is just a pronunciation change, not a meaning change.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
The ت at the start means 'you' and shows the action is happening in the present

steps.m: you all deal justly (in relation to orphans)
steps.n: Changed from singular 'you' to plural 'you all' - talking about everyone dealing fairly with orphans
This is a basic preposition that shows location or being inside something. Here it connects 'orphans' (يَتٰمَىٰ) to the main sentence about being fair.

steps.m: to lose a parent as a child
an orphan (someone who lost a parent)
steps.n: Changes from describing what happened to describing the person it happened to
Changes the word to show there are many orphans, not just one
Adds 'the' to show we're talking about specific orphans
Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he married' to 'you marry!' (command form)
Changed from singular to plural command
Added فَ to show this is a result or consequence
Basic relative pronoun that connects to the verb مَلَكَتْ (possessed/owned) to mean 'what was possessed'
Added short 'a' vowels (fatha) to make it mean 'he became good'
Changed to past tense verb with middle alif, common for hollow verbs
Starting with the basic preposition that shows direction or purpose
Added the pronoun 'you all' (كم) to show who the action is for. This connects to فَٱنكِحُوا۟ (then marry) to show who should do the marrying.
This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or what it belongs to
The shadda (ّ) doubles the م sound because this word comes after another word ending in ن (خِفْتُمْ). This is just for easier pronunciation.
The fatha (َ) at the end connects this word to the next word النِّسَآءِ (the women), showing that we're talking about "from the women"
Root letters show connection to femininity and women
Irregular plural form of 'woman' - doesn't follow normal plural rules
Added ال to make it specific and shadda on ن because it comes after ال
The word gets a kasra (ee sound) at the end because it comes after مِنَ (meaning 'of/from'). The alif is written longer but pronounced the same.

steps.m: to make into two or double
steps.n: Root relates to doubling or making something two
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make a basic past tense verb
Added مَ at the start and changed the ending to ى to make a word meaning 'in pairs/twos'

steps.m: in twos, paired (describing how to marry wives)
steps.n: Added the long alif (ٰ) to show this is the final form. In this ayah, it describes how men can marry up to four wives, but they should marry them two at a time.
Root relates to the number three
Forms a special pattern showing how to group things. This word is part of the options for marriage being described.
Adds conjunction prefix و meaning 'and' to connect this option with previous options

steps.m: four or to divide into four parts
steps.n: Root relates to the number four
The vowel marks change the root into a specific pattern that means 'one fourth'
Adding alif changes the meaning from 'one fourth' to 'in groups of four'
Added 'and' to connect this with the previous numbers mentioned (two and three)
Basic conditional particle that shows something depends on something else happening

steps.m: then if (connects this condition to what was mentioned before)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to connect this condition with the previous statement about marrying women. It shows this is a follow-up condition.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to 'I' form by adding ت at the end. The middle letter و got dropped and we added the ِ (i) sound to keep it easy to say - this happens with similar words that have و in the middle.
Added م to the end of خِفْتُ to make it second person plural masculine (you all). The ت becomes تُ with damma.
Starting with the first part أَنْ which means 'that' and is used to connect parts of a sentence
Added لَا which means 'not' to negate what comes after

The ن from أَنْ combines with the ل from لَا to make a doubled ل (shown by ّ). This is just a pronunciation change, not a meaning change. This word connects to تُقْسِطُوا (you do justice) to mean 'that you do not do justice'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from past tense 'he' to present tense 'you' by adding تَ at the start and changing vowels
Added plural masculine subject marker وا to indicate multiple male addressees
Root relates to being one or alone
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a past tense verb
Changed pattern to show someone/something that is characterized by oneness
Added feminine marker to specify one feminine thing
Added فَ to show this is a result or consequence
Added tanween to show this is the object of the verb 'marry' mentioned earlier in the verse
Basic conjunction particle that gives a choice between two options. Here it connects 'then one' with 'what your right hands possess', showing these are alternative options.
Relative pronoun referring to 'whatever' of the women that are permissible to marry. In this ayah, it connects to النِّسَآءِ (the women) through طَابَ (are good/lawful).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create past tense verb meaning 'he possessed'

steps.m: she/it possessed (describes ما before this word)
steps.n: Added feminine marker ت to show the subject is feminine. Here it refers back to ما (what/whatever) which takes feminine agreement

steps.m: to be on the right side or blessed
steps.n: Root relates to right hand and being blessed
Root meaning 'right/blessed' becomes the noun 'right hand'
Forms broken plural pattern أَفْعَال to show multiple right hands
Changes to show this word is doing the action (adds ُ at end and stretches the middle alif)
Adds كُمْ to show possession by multiple people being addressed
Basic demonstrative pronoun for pointing to something nearby
Adding the alif above (ٰ) changes it from pointing to something near to something far
Adding لِ makes the pointing stronger, like saying 'that one there'
Adding كَ shows we're talking about something masculine and makes it more specific

steps.m: to be close or near to something
steps.n: Root relates to nearness and closeness
Added fatha vowels between letters and changed و to ا at the end to make 'he' form of the verb
Added أَ at start and changed the end to ى to make it mean 'more' or 'nearer' - like how we add '-er' in English to make 'near' into 'nearer'
Added an alif mark (ٰ) and a small alif (ٓ) to help with pronunciation. The meaning relates to this being a more likely way to prevent unfairness, as mentioned in the surrounding ayah.
Starting with the basic particle that introduces dependent clauses. In this ayah, it introduces what you fear might not happen - being fair.
Added the negative particle لَا to show what you fear won't happen

steps.m: that not (you will be fair to يَتٰمَىٰ)
steps.n: The ن and ل merge together, making the ل stronger (shown by ّ). This is a common way to make particles easier to say together. This introduces what is feared - that you won't be fair to the orphans.

steps.m: to be dependent on others/to have many dependents

steps.n: The root letters ع-و-ل became عَالَ by adding vowels and changing the middle letter و to ا, making it past tense 'he became dependent'
Changed to present tense with ت prefix for 'you'. The middle ا changes to ُ in present tense of hollow verbs.

steps.m: you all become dependent/have many dependents
steps.n: Added وا to make it plural 'you all' and added the small و mark (۟) that shows this is the end of the word
Changed the meaning to be causative - making someone give something
Added damma (ُ) to show this is in the nominative case
Added وا to make it plural command
Added و at beginning to mean 'and'
Forms a special plural pattern for groups of women
Makes the noun definite and adds emphasis with shadda
Adds fatha to show this is the object receiving the action of giving (وَءَاتُوا۟) in this ayah
The vowel pattern shows something becoming a natural characteristic
Adding ات makes it a plural noun referring to multiple gifts
The kasra at the end shows this word is being described or possessed by something

steps.m: their (feminine) dowries (referring to the women mentioned as النِّسَآءَ)
steps.n: Added the feminine plural possessive pronoun to show these are the women's dowries
Added kasra under first letter and sukun on middle letter to create noun form
Added ة to show one specific instance of giving

steps.m: a gift freely given (object of آتُوا)
steps.n: Added tanwin to show this is the object being given. This word describes the marriage gifts (صَدُقٰتِهِنَّ) that should be given to women.
This is a connecting particle that shows one thing follows another

steps.m: and if (connecting to طِبْنَ - they become pleased)
steps.n: Added إِن which makes this a conditional phrase. This combination introduces a condition: 'and if they become pleased...'
Changed the first vowel to kasra to show this happened to something rather than something doing it

Added نَ at the end to show multiple females did this. The weak letter ي is dropped when we add نَ. This word relates to النِّسَآءَ (the women) from earlier in the verse who are pleased with something.
Starting with the basic preposition that shows purpose or direction
Added كُمْ to show this is directed to multiple people being addressed

This is a preposition that shows movement away from something or what something is about. In this ayah, it connects the verb طِبْنَ (they become pleased) with شَىْءٍ (something), showing what they are pleased about giving up.
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to form the basic noun

steps.m: a thing (possessed by/connected to something else)
steps.n: Added the genitive case ending to show this word is connected to what comes after it in the ayah
Basic preposition meaning 'from'
Added هُ at the end to show what the 'from' refers to. The هُ refers back to شَىْءٍ (something) mentioned earlier in the verse.
The shadda (ّ) is added because this word comes after a word ending in ن or م, causing the م to be doubled in pronunciation. This is just a pronunciation change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to make a noun meaning 'self' or 'soul'

steps.m: willingly (object of طِبْنَ - they became pleased with)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to make it an object (something being acted upon). Shows someone doing something willingly/by their own choice. Connected to طِبْنَ (they became pleased with) in the ayah.
Changed from basic root 'eat' to a command meaning 'eat!' by dropping first letter and adding vowels
Adding plural masculine marker to make command for group
Adding coordinating conjunction to connect with previous command
Adding pronoun suffix to show what is being eaten

steps.m: pleasant (describes the food mentioned in فَكُلُوهُ)
steps.n: Added ي between second and third root letters to make it describe something that brings happiness. This word describes how the food (مِنْهُ) should be eaten.

steps.m: pleasantly/with enjoyment (describes how to eat the food in فَكُلُوهُ)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this describes how the action should be done. The long ي (ٓ) and hamza (ٔ) are just spelling variations that don't change the meaning.
Changes the hamza (ء) to make it easier to read, and adds two small diagonal lines (tanween) to show it describes HOW to eat
Added vowels and ي to turn the root into an adjective that describes something as pleasant
Added tanween (double fatha) to show this describes something in a sentence

steps.m: in a very wholesome and pleasant way (describing how to eat/consume)
steps.n: Changed from a describing word to an adverb (tells how to do something), paired with هَنِيٓـًٔا to say 'eat/enjoy in a wholesome way'
This is a negative particle that stops something from happening. Here it's telling people not to do something (not to give)
The و connects this command with previous instructions. It's like making a list of things to do and not do.

steps.m: to give, to bring, to come

steps.n: The تُ at the start shows this is about 'you' doing the action
Added plural marker وا to show multiple people are being addressed. The word السُّفَهَآءَ (the foolish ones) receives this action.

steps.m: to lack wisdom or act foolishly

steps.n: Added short vowels (fat-ha and kasra) to make it mean 'he became foolish' in the past tense
Changes to plural form for people who are foolish, like English '-ish' people
Added ال to make it definite. The س gets a shadda because it's a sun letter.

steps.m: the foolish ones (object of تُؤْتُوا - do not give)
steps.n: Added accusative case ending because this word is the object of the verb تُؤْتُوا (do not give)
The root letters م-و-ل combine to make مال (māl), where the و changes to ا - this is a common pattern for words about wealth/money
Added أ at the beginning and returned the original و to make it plural, following a common pattern for property words

steps.m: your wealth (referring to what Allah made as support for you)
steps.n: Added كُمُ to show the wealth belongs to those being addressed. The alif changes to a long alif (ٰ) when connecting to the suffix.
This is the basic form of the feminine relative pronoun, used to describe or give more information about feminine things
Added ال with shadda to make it more specific. In this ayah, it's describing 'your wealth' (أَمْوٰلَكُمُ) which Allah made as a means of support.

steps.m: he made (subject of Allah in this ayah)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. This word refers to Allah making/setting something in this verse.

steps.m: a deity, one who is worshipped
steps.n: Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' by adding vowel patterns
This is the unique name for The One True God in Islam. The ال makes it definite (like 'the'), the shadda emphasizes the ل, and the ُ shows this is the subject of جَعَلَ (made/placed) in this ayah.
This is a preposition showing direction or purpose, connecting to 'your wealth' (أَمْوٰلَكُمُ) in this ayah

steps.m: for you all (referring to those being addressed about أَمْوٰلَكُمُ)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show this applies to multiple people being spoken to. This connects to 'your wealth' (أَمْوٰلَكُمُ) in the ayah, showing who owns the wealth.
Connected the root letters with basic vowels to form a past tense verb
Changed the middle letter و to ي and added ا after it to make a noun meaning the result of supporting

steps.m: a means of support (object of جَعَلَ)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is indefinite and is being acted upon. This word is the object of جَعَلَ (made/appointed) - Allah made wealth as a means of support.
Changed from root letters to past tense 'he' form by adding the vowels a-a-a
Changed from past tense to command form by changing vowels
Adding و at the end makes it a command for a group (like saying 'all of you provide!' instead of just 'provide!')
Adding imperative prefix ٱ to form command
Adding attached object pronoun هم meaning 'them' (masculine plural)

steps.m: and provide for them! (all of you)
steps.n: Adding conjunction prefix وَ to connect this command with previous phrase

steps.m: in (preposition showing location inside أَمْوٰلَكُمُ/your wealth)
steps.n: Basic preposition showing something is inside or within something else. Here it refers to being inside your wealth.

steps.m: in it (referring back to أَمْوٰلَكُمُ/your wealth)
steps.n: هَا refers back to أَمْوٰلَكُمُ (your wealth) which was mentioned earlier in the ayah

steps.m: to clothe, dress, or cover someone

steps.n: Changed root letters into a command by adding vowel marks - now means 'you clothe!'
Added imperative prefix alif to form command
Added هُمْ at the end to show who receives the action - 'them' refers back to السُّفَهَاء (the foolish ones) mentioned earlier in the verse
Adds conjunction meaning 'and'

steps.m: to speak, say, or tell something

steps.n: Changed from basic verb 'to say' into a command telling one person to 'say!'
Makes the command apply to multiple people instead of just one

steps.m: and all of you say! (connected to وَٱرْزُقُوهُمْ and وَٱكْسُوهُمْ)
steps.n: Adds 'and' to connect this command with the previous commands in the verse about providing for and clothing them
This is a particle that shows direction or purpose. Here it connects to قِيٰمًا (a means of support) to show what something is for.

steps.m: to/for them (referring to السُّفَهَآءَ - the foolish ones)
steps.n: Added هُمْ to show this refers back to السُّفَهَآءَ (the foolish ones) mentioned earlier in the ayah
Changed the root letters for 'speak' into a noun meaning 'speech' by adding vowels

steps.m: a kind word (to them) (object of وَقُولُوا)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the object being said. This word describes what kind of speech should be given to them, as commanded by وَقُولُوا (and say).

steps.m: to know, recognize, or be familiar with

steps.n: Added vowel marks to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Changed to passive participle form showing something that is known or recognized
Added emphasis by doubling the م
Added tanween to show this describes how to speak (modifies قَوْلًا - speech)
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added ٱ at start and ت after first letter to make it mean 'to test oneself' rather than just 'to test'
Added وا at the end to show this is addressed to multiple people
Added وَ at start to connect this command to what came before

steps.m: to lack something essential (especially a father)
orphan (one who has lost their father)
steps.n: Added vowels (fatha and kasra) to the root letters to make a noun meaning 'one who has lost their father'
Changed the word to show it means many orphans
Added ال to show these are specific orphans being talked about. This word is what is being tested/examined in the command وَٱبْتَلُوا۟ (test/examine).
This is a particle that shows when something ends. The fatha and shadda make it a complete word.
The alif maksura (ىٰ) and small alif (ٓ) are added to extend the sound at the end. This word connects to إِذَا (when) to show 'until when' something should happen.
This is a time word that connects two events. In this ayah, it connects 'reaching marriageable age' with what should happen after.
The ا at the end shows this is about something that will definitely happen in the future, not just might happen. In this ayah, it's about when orphans definitely reach marriageable age.

steps.m: to reach, arrive at, or attain something
he reached (before حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا - until when)
steps.n: Added fatha marks to connect the letters. This shows one person did something in the past. In this ayah, it comes after 'until when' to show when something happens.

steps.m: they reached (referring to الْيَتٰمَىٰ - the orphans)
steps.n: The suffix وا changes 'he' to 'they' (referring to the orphans mentioned earlier in the ayah). The ُ (damma) before وا is needed for proper pronunciation.
Added a small mark (۟) above the وا to show it's pronounced fully. This doesn't change the meaning.
Added vowel marks and alif to convert the verb into a noun meaning the concept of marriage
Adding ال ('the') makes it refer to a specific marriage instead of marriage in general

steps.m: the marriage (object of بَلَغُوا - they reached)
steps.n: Adding fatha at the end shows this word is the object of the verb بَلَغُوا (they reached) in this ayah
This is a combination of two particles: فَ (and/so) and إِنْ (if)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Added an alif at the start to make it mean 'to find/perceive something' instead of just 'notice'
Added تُ at the end to change from 'he' to 'I' in past tense
Changed the ending from تُ (meaning 'I') to تُم (meaning 'you all') to show multiple people doing the action
This is a preposition showing source or origin
Added shadda for emphasis and pronunciation when connecting with previous word

steps.m: from them (referring to the orphans mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show this refers to 'them' (the orphans mentioned in the ayah)

steps.m: to guide correctly, to lead to the right way
good judgment (as the object being discovered)
steps.n: The root 'guide correctly' becomes 'good judgment' as a noun. The ًا ending shows it's the thing being found (connects to 'you perceived in them')
Connected the root letters with fatha to make past tense verb
Changed from 'he handed over' to a command meaning 'you hand over!' by adding alif at start and changing the ending
Added وا to make the command for multiple people
Added فَ at beginning to show this follows from previous statement
Basic preposition showing direction or recipient. In this ayah, it shows who should receive their wealth.
The ى changes to ي before adding pronouns

steps.m: to them (recipients of أَمْوٰلَهُمْ - their wealth)
steps.n: Added هِمْ to show 'them' (masculine plural). This refers to the orphans who should receive their wealth when they reach maturity.
Root letters م-و-ل became مَال by changing و to ا (alif) to make the basic noun for 'wealth'
Changed from single wealth to plural by adding أ at start and و in middle - like changing 'money' to 'monies'
Added هُمْ at the end to show the wealth belongs to them (referring to the orphans mentioned in the ayah)
This is the basic negation particle that stops something from happening. Here it's stopping the action of eating the orphans' wealth.
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it. This connects the prohibition about eating orphans' wealth with the previous command about testing them.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding تَ at start and changing vowels. The sukun (ْ) on the first root letter is needed for pronunciation.
Added و and ا at the end to show multiple people doing the action

steps.m: you all eat it (referring to the orphans' wealth)
steps.n: Adding هَآ (with long alif) to show 'it' refers to the orphans' wealth mentioned earlier in the verse
Basic meaning of going beyond proper bounds
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels
Changed to a noun form showing the action itself
Added an alif at the end to change it from a noun (excess) to an adverb (in an excessive way)
Basic meaning of rushing or doing something quickly
Changed the word from an action (rush) to a thing (rushing)
Added ًا to show this word describes how something is done

steps.m: and in a rushing way (describes how they might wrongly consume orphans' wealth)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) at the beginning to connect this to the previous warning about wrongful consumption of orphans' wealth
This is a connecting word that introduces what comes next. Here it connects to the idea of 'they grow up' (يَكْبَرُوا۟), showing what the previous statement is trying to prevent.
Basic meaning of growing or becoming large
Added vowels to make basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added وا to show multiple people doing the action. The context is about orphans growing up to maturity.
Compound of و (and) + من (who/whoever)
Basic meaning of existing or being
Added short vowels (fat-ha) to make it past tense 'he was'. This is a step before final spelling changes.
The middle weak letter و changes to ا because it has a fatha before and after it. This is a common change in hollow verbs. In this ayah, it describes someone's state of being poor.
Basic meaning of being wealthy or self-sufficient
Changed from root letters to an adjective by adding the vowels 'a' and 'i'
Added shadda to intensify the meaning
Added two dots at the end (tanween) to show this word is receiving the action, like saying 'was wealthy' instead of just 'wealthy'

steps.m: to be pure, to refrain from wrong
steps.n: Basic meaning of being pure or abstaining
Added vowels to create basic Form I verb
Added Form X pattern which gives meaning of seeking/requesting the action
Present tense prefix indicates subject is 'he'
Adds coordinating conjunction meaning 'then/so'

steps.m: then let him seek to abstain
steps.n: Adds command particle meaning 'let/should'

steps.m: whoever, the one who (subject of كَانَ)
steps.n: Base relative pronoun that introduces a conditional clause. Here it refers to any guardian of orphans' property who might be in either situation (rich or poor).
The و connects this clause about poor guardians to the previous clause about rich guardians, showing both situations are being addressed
Added fathas to make the basic past tense form meaning 'he was'

The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a hollow verb (has a weak middle letter). This is a common change in Arabic that doesn't affect the meaning. In this ayah, it describes someone who is rich.
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters and show the basic meaning of being poor
Added ي between second and third root letters to show this is a strong or lasting quality

steps.m: a poor person (object of كَانَ - was)
steps.n: Added ًا (tanween fatha) at the end to show this is the object/predicate of كَانَ (was) - meaning someone who is poor
Connected the root letters to form the basic command
Added يَ at the start to show 'he' and changed the pattern to show ongoing action (present tense)
Added لْ at beginning to make it a suggestion/command and changed final vowel to sukun
Added فَ at beginning to connect this command with what came before it

steps.m: to know/recognize what is good and right
what is known to be good and proper
steps.n: Changes the concept of 'knowing what is good' into 'something that is known to be good/proper'

steps.m: the right way, the proper manner
steps.n: Adding ال makes it definite - referring to a specific known thing or custom

steps.m: in a proper/reasonable manner (related to فَلْيَأْكُلْ - let him eat)
steps.n: Adding بِ shows how the action (eating) should be done - in a proper way. This connects to the verb فَلْيَأْكُلْ (let him eat) in the ayah.

steps.m: in a proper/reasonable manner (describing how فَلْيَأْكُلْ - let him eat - should be done)
steps.n: The letter ب (meaning 'in/with') at the start makes the word end with kasra (ِ). This describes how the eating should be done - in a proper way.
This is a conditional word that shows something happens at a specific time. In this ayah, it's talking about when you give the orphans their wealth.

steps.m: then when (connects to دَفَعْتُمْ - you give)
steps.n: The فَ shows this happens after something else. Here it connects to when you give the orphans their wealth.

steps.m: to push, hand over, or pay
steps.n: Basic past tense form of the verb
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make the basic past tense form
Added تُمْ at the end to show that many people (all male or mixed group) did this action in the past
Basic preposition showing direction or recipient. This word has no root since it's a particle.
Changed ى to يْ to prepare for adding the pronoun. This is a spelling change that doesn't affect meaning.

steps.m: to them (recipients of أَمْوٰلَهُمْ - their wealth)
steps.n: Added هِمْ to show 'them' (masculine plural). In this ayah, it shows who should receive their wealth back.
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters
Changes to broken plural form to show multiple instances of wealth
Added هُمْ (hum) at the end to mean 'their' - like adding 's to show ownership
This is the basic concept of witnessing or testifying to something
Adding vowels makes it a past tense verb meaning 'he witnessed'
Added أ at the beginning to make the verb causative - meaning to make someone else do the witnessing
Added ُ at the end to show this is present tense indicative mood
Added وا at the end to change from 'I witness' to 'you all witness!' - makes it a command for a group
Added فَ to show this action follows from what came before
This is a basic preposition showing position, responsibility, or obligation. It's one of the most common Arabic prepositions.
Changed ى to يْ to prepare for attaching the pronoun. This is a common change when adding pronouns to words ending in ى.

steps.m: upon them (referring to the orphans' property mentioned in أَمْوٰلَهُمْ)
steps.n: Added هِمْ which means 'them' (masculine plural). In this ayah, it refers back to the orphans whose property is being discussed.
Changed from basic root to past tense by adding fathas, with ى at the end showing it's past tense

steps.m: and he was sufficient (connected to فَأَشْهِدُوا۟)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this word with the previous command (فَأَشْهِدُوا۟ - then take witnesses). The small alif (ٰ) shows how to pronounce the ى correctly.
Connected the root letters together to form the basic word
Added shadda on ل to make this the specific name for the one true God
Added fatha on the shadda to help with pronunciation
Added بِ at the beginning to show 'by/with' and ِ at the end to connect it with the next word

steps.m: to count, reckon, take account of

steps.n: Added ي between second and third root letters to show someone who does this action professionally or thoroughly
Added ًا to show this describes what Allah is sufficient as (object of كَفَىٰ - sufficient is)
The vowels change the root into a noun meaning 'man'
Changes singular 'man' into plural 'men' using broken plural pattern
Adds 'the' to specify these particular men
Adds لِ with kasra at start to show 'for/to' and kasra at end to connect with next word
Adds emphasis by doubling the ل with a shadda
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed from verb 'establish' to noun meaning 'share/portion' by adding ي and changing middle vowel to kasra
Added tanween dammah to show this is any share/portion (not a specific one)
Combined the two words مِن (from) and مَا (what) together

steps.m: from what (of تَرَكَ ٱلْوٰلِدَانِ - what the parents left)
steps.n: The ن merges into the م, shown by the shadda (ّ). This is a common pattern in Arabic when ن is followed by certain letters like م.

steps.m: he left (what الْوٰلِدَانِ وَٱلْأَقْرَبُونَ/the parents and relatives left behind)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this is about something that happened in the past. In this ayah, it's talking about inheritance - what parents and relatives left behind.

steps.m: to give birth or produce offspring

steps.n: Changes the basic meaning into a word for someone who does this action. This pattern is common for changing actions into the people who do them.
Adds ان at the end to show we're talking about exactly two parents
Adds ٱلْ at the beginning to point to specific parents being discussed

steps.m: the two parents (who left something behind)
steps.n: Changes وَا to وٰ (same sound, just written differently). The ِ at the end shows this word is connected to تَرَكَ (left behind) in the sentence.
This pattern shows something naturally becoming close or near
Changes from 'close' to 'closer/nearest' by adding أ at start and changing middle vowels - like English adding '-er' or '-est'
Adds 'and' to connect this word to what came before it
Makes the word plural to refer to multiple close relatives
Adds 'the' (ال) to make it specific close relatives
Root meaning 'female' becomes plural word 'women' by following special pattern نِفَاء
Added emphasis on the ن with shadda
Changed regular alif to long alif
Added kasra at the end to show this word is affected by لِ
Added لِل which combines لِ (for) and ال (the)
Added و at beginning to mean 'and'

steps.m: a share/portion that is set aside
steps.n: Changes from verb (to set) into noun (a share/portion)
Added tanween damma to show this is the subject of the sentence
Connected the two words مِن (from) and مَا (what) together

steps.m: from what (of what تَرَكَ ٱلْوٰلِدَانِ the parents left)
steps.n: When مِن comes before a word starting with م, the ن changes into another م with a shadda. This makes it easier to pronounce.
Adding fathas creates the past tense verb. This word shows what the parents (الْوٰلِدَانِ) left behind as inheritance.

steps.m: to give birth or be born
steps.n: Basic root meaning showing the concept of birth or coming into existence
Changes the action into the person who does it, like changing 'to give birth' into 'one who gives birth'
Adding ان shows we're talking about exactly two parents
Adding ال makes it specific and ِ shows it's being acted upon in the sentence. This refers to the parents who left inheritance mentioned in the verse.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Added أ at beginning and changed vowels to make comparative form
Added ون to make masculine plural form referring to multiple close relatives
Added ال to make the word definite (specific)
Added و at beginning to connect this word to the previous one
Combined the two words مِن (from) and مَا (what)
The ن changes to م and gets a shadda because it's easier to say. This word connects to تَرَكَ (left/inherited) to show what was left behind.
Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb
The middle letter has a shadda (doubled) to make it stronger. This word tells us that 'whatever' (مِمَّا) became small or few in amount.
Added sukun to show the end of the first word part

steps.m: from him/it (referring to نَصِيبٌ - a share)
steps.n: Added هُ at the end to show who/what is being referred to. Here it refers back to نَصِيبٌ (a share) mentioned earlier in the ayah.

This is a connecting word that gives a choice between two options. Here it connects 'little' (قَلَّ) and 'much' (كَثُرَ) to show that whether the inheritance is small or large, there is still a share.
The vowel pattern َُ shows something naturally grew or increased by itself

steps.m: to assign or establish something in its place

steps.n: Changes from verb (to assign) to noun (a share/portion)

steps.m: a share (as the object being discussed in the ayah)
steps.n: Added ًا to show this is the object being discussed in relation to what men and women inherit

steps.m: to make something required or necessary

steps.n: Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed to show something that was made required, rather than the person who made it required

steps.m: a required portion (describing نَصِيبًا - a share)
steps.n: Added shadda on م and tanween to show this describes the share (نَصِيبًا) mentioned in the ayah
This is a conditional particle that means 'when' something happens
Added و at the beginning to connect this with what came before. This word introduces a condition about 'when' something happens, specifically when حَضَرَ (arrives/comes) happens.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create past tense verb. This word is connected to القِسْمَةَ (the distribution) in this ayah, showing who attended the distribution.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb

steps.m: a division, an act of distributing
steps.n: Changed from 'he divided' to a noun meaning 'one time of dividing something up'
Added ال to make it specific
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object of the verb حَضَرَ (attended/was present)
This is a special plural noun that means 'owners of' or 'those who have'. It's like saying 'people of' in English.
Added small alif marks (۟) to show this is plural. In this ayah, it's followed by 'the relatives', showing who the owners/possessors are.
Changes the verb into a noun meaning the state of being close or related to others

steps.m: the relatives (who are being discussed in the inheritance division)
steps.n: Makes it specific to the relatives being discussed in this context of inheritance distribution

steps.m: to lack a father while being young

steps.n: Forms a noun describing someone affected by the loss of their father
Changes the word to show many orphans using a special plural pattern
Makes the word specific by adding 'the'
Adds 'and' to connect this word with what comes before it

steps.m: to be in need, to be still/settled
one who is in constant need
steps.n: Added مِ at start and ي in middle to make a noun meaning 'someone who is poor/needy'
Makes broken plural form to indicate multiple poor people
Adds nominative case ending to complete the word
Adds 'wa' (and) at start + 'al' (the) to make 'and the poor people'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed from 'he provided' to a command by removing first vowel and changing middle vowel to 'u'
Added وا to make the command for multiple people
Added helping alif to start the command

steps.m: provide for them! (command to many people)
steps.n: Added هُم to show who should receive the provision

steps.m: so provide for them! (command to many people)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this action follows from what came before
This is a preposition showing where something comes from
Added shadda on م to help with pronunciation when connecting to previous word

steps.m: from/of it (connected to فَٱرْزُقُوهُم - provide for them)
steps.n: Added هُ to show what we're taking from. This connects to the command 'provide for them' in the ayah.
Command form made by adding damma (u sound) to first letter and sukoon (stop) at end
Adding وا changes it from commanding one person to commanding many people
Added وَ (and) at the beginning to connect this command with the previous phrase فَٱرْزُقُوهُم (give them provision)
This is a particle that shows direction or purpose. Here it connects to قَوْلًا (saying) to show who the saying is for.
Adding هُمْ shows this is directed to multiple people. This word connects to قَوْلًا (saying) to show who should receive the kind words.
Changed from basic root meaning 'speak' into a noun meaning 'speech' by adding vowels

steps.m: a kind word (object of قُولُوا - say!)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this is receiving the action of the command 'say!' (قُولُوا). The context shows this means specifically 'kind words' to orphans and needy people.

steps.m: to know or recognize something well

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Added مَ at start and changed vowels to make it mean 'something that is known/recognized as good' instead of the action of knowing
Added emphasis mark (shadda) on م because it comes after a word ending in a similar sound
Added ًا at the end to show this describes how to speak to them (referring to قَوْلًا - 'statement' in the ayah)
Basic past tense verb showing the feeling of fear
Added ي at start to show present tense, and changed ending due to weak letter
Added لْ at start and removed ى at end to make it mean 'let him fear' instead of 'he fears'
Added و at start to connect with previous statement
This is the basic form meaning 'who' that refers to one person or thing
Adding ين makes it refer to many people instead of just one
Adding ال makes it point to specific people - changes 'who' to 'those who'
Added final vowel marks and shadda for proper pronunciation. This word is the subject of تَرَكُوا (they left) in this ayah.
This is a basic conditional word that introduces a hypothetical situation - it's like saying 'what if' or imagining something that didn't actually happen
Added vowel marks to show how to pronounce it correctly: 'law'
Connected the letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past
Added وا at the end to show a group of people did this action
This is the basic form of the preposition that shows where something starts or comes from
Added vowel marks to show how to pronounce it correctly. This word connects to خَلْفِهِمْ (behind them) to tell us where something is from.

steps.m: to be behind or after something

steps.n: Added fatha and sukun to make the basic noun meaning 'behind'
Added kasra to show this word is owned by or connected to something
Added هِمْ at the end to show who it belongs to - 'them'
Root 'scatter' changed to 'scattered ones' by adding damma (u sound) and doubling the middle letter
Added ي and a vowel mark to show these are descendants/offspring that came from something
Added ة to make it into a noun meaning 'descendants' (because they are spread out from their ancestors)
Added tanween (ً) to show this word is being acted upon by something else in the sentence
Root letters got vowels to make a past tense verb meaning 'became weak'
Changes to broken plural form showing a group of weak things/people
Added a mark at the end to show this describes 'offspring' - like adding 'a' or 'an' in English

steps.m: to fear or be afraid of something

steps.n: Added vowels and changed و to ا because it's in the middle of the word. This is a common change in Arabic for words with و in the middle.

steps.m: they feared (referring to those who might leave behind weak offspring)
steps.n: Added وا to show multiple people did the action. This connects to the earlier part of the verse talking about those who might leave behind weak offspring.
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation of the preposition
Changed the final ى to يْ to prepare for attaching the pronoun
Added هِمْ at the end to show who is being acted upon (them)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Added يَ at start to show present tense, and تَّ in middle to show doing action to oneself
Added فَ (fa) to connect this action with what came before it
Added لْ to make it a command/suggestion - 'let him' or 'he should'
Changed to plural by adding وا at the end and changing the vowel to ُ
Changed to the unique form used only for Allah, with ال merged into the word and the hamza removed

steps.m: Allah (as the object of فَلْيَتَّقُوا۟ - they should fear)
steps.n: The word 'Allah' gets a mark (fatha) at the end because it receives the action of 'they should fear'
Added vowels to connect the letters and make a past tense verb. The middle و changes to ا in this form.
Changes from past tense (he said) to present tense (he says) by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowel
