Added هُ to show who the preposition is referring to
Added نَّ to change the pronoun from singular masculine to plural feminine
Added damma and sukun to make it mean 'a quarter'
Added 'ال' to make it specific, doubled the ر with shadda, and added final damma to show it's the subject of فَلَكُمُ (then for you is)
This word combines two parts: مِن meaning 'from' and مَا meaning 'what'

When مِن comes before a word starting with م, the ن changes to match the م and doubles it (shown by the ّ mark). This makes it easier to pronounce. This word connects to تَرَكْنَ (they left) in the verse.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make past tense verb. In this ayah, it refers specifically to inheritance left behind.
Changes from 3rd person (he) to 2nd person (you) by adding تَ suffix
Added م suffix with damma on ت to make plural 'you all', with final sukun

steps.m: if (introduces condition about لَّمْ يَكُن 'not having')
steps.n: This is a conditional particle that introduces a condition about not having children. It works with the following word لَّمْ to express 'if there is not'. In this ayah, it's part of explaining inheritance rules based on different conditions.
This is a negative word that makes present tense verbs talk about things that did not happen in the past. In this ayah, it changes يَكُن (is) to mean 'was not'.
The shadda (ّ) makes us say the ل twice because it comes after the word إِن, but the meaning stays the same. It still means 'did not'.
The و is a weak letter that affects how the word is formed
Added ي at the start to show who is doing the action (he) and when (present/future)
The ُ (damma) shows this is about a state of being rather than an action done to something else
Removed the و and final vowel because it comes after لَّمْ (did not). This changes it to past tense and makes it mean 'did not exist/was not'
This is a preposition meaning 'for' or 'to' that connects actions or things to people
Added كُمْ which shows this applies to multiple people being spoken to
The shadda on ل adds emphasis to show this is definitely or surely for them

steps.m: to give birth, to bear children
steps.n: This root relates to birth and offspring
Changed from root meaning 'birth' to simple noun meaning 'child' by adding vowel marks
Added double damma to show this is an indefinite subject. This word is the subject of يَكُن (there is) in this ayah.
This is a conditional particle that introduces 'if' statements. Here it introduces the condition 'if there was' (كَانَ)
فَ at the start shows this is a result or sequence following from the previous statement. It introduces a new condition that follows from the earlier one.
Added fathas to make it past tense. This is the theoretical form before the weak letter changes.

steps.m: he was/existed (past tense, in فَإِن condition)
steps.n: The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas in a past tense verb. This word appears in a conditional phrase starting with فَإِن (and if).
This is a preposition that shows something belongs to someone or is for their benefit

steps.m: for you all (plural masculine, indicating the inheritors)
steps.n: Added كُمْ which means 'you all' when talking to multiple people. In this verse, it refers to the male inheritors being addressed about inheritance shares.
Added vowels to make the noun form meaning 'child' or 'offspring'
Added tanween damma to show this is an indefinite noun. This word is the subject of يَكُن (there is) in this ayah.
Added fatha to show this is a preposition meaning 'for' or 'to'
Added هُ to show who the preposition is for
Added نَّ to change it from singular masculine to plural feminine
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from something before it
Changed pattern to make it a noun
Added damma vowels to make it mean 'one eighth'
Added shadda for emphasis
Added ال at beginning to make it definite and ُ at end to show it's the subject
Starting with the two separate words that will combine: مِن meaning 'from' and مَا meaning 'what'
When مِن comes before a word starting with م, the ن changes to match the م and gets a shadda. This makes it easier to pronounce. This word connects to تَرَكْتُم (you left/inherited) in the ayah.
Basic past tense form of the verb
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb meaning 'he left behind'
Added تَ and changed middle vowel to sukun to show one male person did this in the past
Changed the ending to تُم to show multiple men did this action (in context of inheritance laws)
Basic preposition meaning 'from' - shows where something starts or comes from
The 'n' sound gets doubled (gets a shadda) because it comes after a connecting letter
Added tanween baa (ۢ) because this word comes before another word starting with ب (بَعْدِ)
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to create the basic noun form meaning 'after'
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to what follows it (وَصِيَّةٍ meaning 'will/testament'). Together they mean 'after a will/testament'.

steps.m: to instruct or direct through a will
steps.n: Root meaning relates to giving instructions or making a will
Changes from verb 'to instruct' into noun meaning 'one who carries out instructions'

steps.m: a specific act of making a will
steps.n: Added ة to change from 'person who guards' to 'act of leaving instructions'

steps.m: a will/testament (being acted upon in the context of inheritance)
steps.n: Added two dots under the last letter to show this is receiving the action (like saying 'a will' vs 'by/with a will')

steps.m: to advise, to make a will
steps.n: Root meaning relates to giving instructions or making a will
Changed root letters into past tense verb 'he advised' by adding vowel marks
Changed from 'he advised' to 'you advise' by adding تُ prefix
Changed from 'she advises' to 'you all are advised' by adding plural ending -ūna
Starting with the basic feminine pronoun that refers back to the will/bequest (وَصِيَّةٍ) mentioned in the verse

steps.m: with/by it (the will/bequest they instruct with)
steps.n: Added the preposition بِ to show this is the means/instrument of instruction. The آ at the end is just a written form of ا that appears at the end of words in the Quran.

steps.m: or (connecting دَيْنٍ as an alternative to وَصِيَّةٍ)
steps.n: This is a basic particle that connects two options. In this ayah, it connects 'debt' (دَيْنٍ) as an alternative to 'bequest' (وَصِيَّةٍ), showing that the inheritance rules apply after either of these are handled.
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make it a noun referring to what is borrowed
Added tanween (two kasras) at the end to show this is any debt, not a specific one. This word comes after أَوْ (or) in the ayah.
This is a conditional particle that introduces a condition, like saying 'if something happens' in English

steps.m: and if (introduces a condition in كَانَ)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this condition with the previous statement. This word introduces a condition that is completed by the verb كَانَ (was) that follows it.
Added fathas to make the basic past tense form before the weak letter change
The middle weak letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas in the past tense. This word is part of a condition about having children (وَلَدٌ).
This is the basic concept of the root, showing movement on foot
The vowels change the root meaning from 'walking' to mean 'a man' - someone who walks/moves around

steps.m: a man (subject of يُورَثُ - is inherited from)
steps.n: Adding tanween (double damma) shows this is indefinite (like saying 'a man' instead of 'the man'). This man is being inherited from in the verse.
Changes to passive voice showing someone receives inheritance rather than inheriting themselves

steps.m: he is inherited from (subject of كَانَ)
steps.n: Adds ُ at the end to show this is the main doer in the sentence (after كَانَ)

steps.m: to be exhausted, to be without descendants

steps.n: Doubling the middle letter makes the meaning stronger

steps.m: state of having no parents or children
steps.n: Added long 'aa' sound and 'ah' ending to make a noun meaning 'having no family'

steps.m: as one who has no parents or children (object of يُورَثُ - is inherited from)
steps.n: Added -an ending to show this person (who has no parents/children) is being inherited from
This is the basic form of the coordinating conjunction that connects two alternative options
Added kasra at the end because the next word starts with a connecting hamza. This helps with pronunciation when reading the words together.
Adding alif and sukun makes it a command form
Adding ة makes it specifically refer to a female person

steps.m: a woman (subject of يُورَثُ - is inherited from)
steps.n: Adding tanween shows this is indefinite (any woman) and is the subject being inherited from in this verse
Added هُ to show who the word 'for' refers to
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it
Added pronunciation helpers (ۥٓ) that don't change the meaning
Root letters get short vowels (fat-ha) to make the basic word for 'brother'
The weak letter و drops at the end of the word when it's by itself (not connected to anything else)

steps.m: a brother (subject of يُورَثُ - is inherited from)
steps.n: Added double damma (tanween) to show this is the subject being inherited from in this verse

steps.m: or (connecting دَيْنٍ as an alternative to وَصِيَّةٍ)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that shows there are two equal options - either a will (وَصِيَّةٍ) or a debt (دَيْنٍ). Both must be paid before inheritance is distributed.
Changes the word pattern to make it feminine by adding damma, sukun and taa
Adds two small dashes on top to show it means 'a sister' instead of just 'sister'

steps.m: to be total, complete, or whole

steps.n: Doubling the middle letter makes the meaning stronger and more complete
Added kasra (ِ) at the end to show this connects to what comes after it
Added ل with kasra (لِ) at the beginning to indicate 'for/belonging to', which is a common prefix for showing possession or purpose

steps.m: then for every/each (in the context of inheritance shares)
steps.n: Added فَ at beginning to show this is a result or consequence of what came before
Changes from basic root to active participle form, meaning 'one'
Changed alif to alif with overbar (ٰ) and added tanwin kasra to show this word belongs to كُلِّ (each) in the ayah
This is the basic preposition meaning 'from'
Adding shadda emphasizes the word, making the 'm' sound stronger
Adding sukun on the ن shows where the word pauses before attachments
Adding هُمَا at the end shows this refers to exactly two people or things
The root letters represent the concept of being one-sixth
Adding the vowel marks ُ (damma) turns the root into the specific fraction 'one sixth'
Adding ال makes it specific, like saying 'the one-sixth share' instead of just 'one-sixth'
This is a conditional particle that introduces a condition. In this ayah, it introduces a condition about inheritance shares.
Added فَ at the beginning to show this condition follows from the previous statement about inheritance shares
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak letter between two fathas
Added وا at the end to show multiple people (they) did the action
Added vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Adds 'a' at start to make it mean 'more' or 'greater' than something else

steps.m: from (showing the starting point of وَصِيَّةٍ)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts from or comes after. In this ayah, it shows the inheritance comes after (from) the will and debts are taken care of.
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun for pointing to something nearby
The alif is replaced with a long alif (ٰ) to show distance
Added ل with kasra (لِ) which is part of the demonstrative structure
Added كَ at the end which completes the demonstrative pronoun structure
This is a basic pronoun meaning 'they' or 'them' (for a group of males or mixed gender)
Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result or consequence. This word connects to شُرَكَآءُ (partners) in the next part of the verse.
Added ي between second and third letters to show someone who does the action of sharing
Changes to broken plural form for partners/associates
Added ُ at the end to show these partners are doing the action
Basic preposition showing location or position
Added ى at the end to complete the word. This preposition connects to the word الثُّلُثِ (the third) to show 'in the third'

steps.m: three, to divide into three parts

steps.n: Adding the vowel marks ُ (damma) turns this into the word for 'one-third'
Added ال to specify this particular one-third share being discussed in the inheritance law
Shows this is the one-third portion being discussed in the inheritance distribution. The ِ ending shows it follows فِى (in)
Basic preposition showing where something starts or comes from. Here it connects to the word بَعْدِ (after) to show the starting point of when inheritance should be calculated.
Added a small ن mark (called a 'sukun') to show the word ends here. This is just for pronunciation and doesn't change the meaning.
Connected the letters with vowel marks to create a word meaning 'after' - this shows how something comes with distance/separation in time or space
The ِ at the end shows this word is owned by or connected to وَصِيَّةٍ (will/testament) - like saying 'after the will' in English
Changed from basic root to noun form by adding vowels and doubling the ي to mean 'the person who does the action'
Added ة to change from 'person who instructs' to 'the instruction itself (testament)'
Added two dots under the last letter to show it comes after another word (after بَعْدِ meaning 'after')

steps.m: to advise, instruct, or make a will

steps.n: Connected root letters with fatha vowels to create past tense verb
Changed to passive voice by adding يُ at start - now means 'being advised' instead of 'advising'

steps.m: it is instructed (about وَصِيَّةٍ - a will/bequest)
steps.n: Added alif khanjariya (ىٰ) to indicate the long vowel sound at the end
Basic preposition showing how something is done. Here it connects to وَصِيَّةٍ (will) to show what is being instructed.

steps.m: with it (referring to وَصِيَّةٍ - the will)
steps.n: Added هَا which refers back to وَصِيَّةٍ (the will) mentioned earlier in the verse

steps.m: with it (referring to وَصِيَّةٍ - the will)
steps.n: The alif is elongated for pronunciation at the end of the word. This doesn't change the meaning.
This is a basic connecting word that gives equal options. In this ayah, it connects 'will' with 'debt' to show that inheritance is divided after either of these are paid.
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to make it a noun. The ي with sukun (ْ) makes a long 'ay' sound.
Added tanween kasra (ٍ) at the end to show this is any debt in general. This word comes after أَوْ (or) in the ayah, listing it as one of the things that must be taken care of before inheritance.

steps.m: to change or be different from

steps.n: Root letters were joined together with a fatha and sukoon to make a word meaning 'other than'
Added fatḥa at the end to show this word describes مُضَآرٍّ (harmful), indicating the bequest should be without being harmful
Changes from verb (to harm) to noun (harmer)
Added مُ at the beginning to show intense/repeated harming action

steps.m: one who mutually harms/causes harm back and forth
steps.n: Adding آ shows the harm goes back and forth between people
Added two dots under the last letter to show it's describing how something is done (like an adjective)

steps.m: to instruct, direct, or make a will

steps.n: Adding vowels changes this into a noun meaning someone who has the quality of protecting/guarding
Added shadda and round ta at the end to change from 'one who guards' to 'a written will'
Added tanween to show this is the object being acted upon in the sentence
This is the basic preposition showing source, origin, or a part of something
Added fatha at the end to help connect smoothly to the next word
The shadda emphasizes the connection and makes the word flow better with what comes after it
Added ال to specify this is THE one true God, and the hamza was dropped because this is a special word. The shadda shows emphasis.

steps.m: of Allah (connected to وَصِيَّةً 'commandment' in this ayah)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show this connects to the word وَصِيَّةً (commandment) before it. The alif at the start changed to a wavy alif which is common in the Quran.
Connected the root letters to form the basic word for deity/god
Added a double L sound (shadda) to change from 'any god' to 'Allah' - the One True God's special name
Added damma at the end to show Allah is the subject doing something in this sentence
Added و with fatha at the beginning to mean 'and'. The alif gets a wavy top (ٱ) when it comes after و
Added ي after first root letter and vowels to show this describes someone who has complete knowledge. When used for Allah, it means His knowledge is perfect and complete.
The double damma (ٌ) at the end shows this is a main word describing Allah directly, not just part of another word

steps.m: very patient and gentle (as a quality)
steps.n: Added ي between second and third root letters to show this is a strong quality someone has
The added double-dots (tanween) at the end shows this is describing Allah as most patient and gentle
This is the basic form of the demonstrative pronoun used to point to something. It's feminine, unlike ذلك which is masculine.

steps.m: that (pointing to something far) (refers to حُدُودُ - limits)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show distance - this indicates we're pointing to something far away or previously mentioned. In this ayah, it's referring to the limits/rules (حُدُودُ) that were mentioned before.

steps.m: to prevent, restrict, or set boundaries

steps.n: Doubling the middle letter adds emphasis to the meaning of restriction/boundary
Changed vowels to ُ and added و to make it plural, showing there are multiple limits or rules
Added ُ at the end to show this is the subject being pointed to by تِلْكَ (those)
Changed to the unique name for the One True God by adding ال and merging letters
Added ِ to show possession, changed ا to ٱ for pronunciation, and added shadda for emphasis. This word shows Allah owns or sets the limits mentioned before.
This is a conditional word that means 'whoever' or 'anyone who'. In this ayah, it refers to the person who obeys Allah and His messenger.
The وَ adds 'and' to connect this with the previous statement about Allah's limits (حُدُودُ ٱللَّهِ). The full meaning is: 'These are Allah's limits, and whoever obeys Allah and His messenger...'
The و changes to ا in the past tense form
Added يُ at start to make it present tense, and changed the middle letter to make it easier to say
Added kasra at the end to show it's in jussive mood (after certain words like لَمْ)
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word that became the proper name for God in Arabic.

steps.m: Allah (as the object being obeyed in يُطِعِ)
steps.n: Added shadda to show emphasis on ل, changed initial alif to wasl form, and added fatha at end to show Allah is the object being obeyed in the phrase 'whoever obeys Allah'
Changed the word into a noun for someone who does the action as their role. The و after second letter makes it intensive, showing this is their main purpose.
Added َ (fatha) and هُ (his) to show this is both possessed by someone and is being acted upon. This word is what is being obeyed in the phrase 'whoever obeys Allah and His messenger'.

steps.m: and His messenger (object of يُطِعِ - obeys)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) to connect this to ٱللَّهَ (Allah) as the two things being obeyed. The small ۥ is just a pronunciation guide.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Changed from 'entered' to 'makes enter' by adding يُ at start - now means causing someone else to enter
Added sukoon to show this is part of a conditional statement (after مَن 'whoever')

steps.m: He makes him enter (referring to Allah making the person enter)
steps.n: Added هُ to show who is being made to enter (referring back to مَن 'whoever')
Changes from present tense verb 'enters' to add 'him' at the end (ه shows who is being entered)

steps.m: to hide or protect by covering

steps.n: The doubled ن shows the covering is complete and thorough

steps.m: a garden (because it's covered by trees)
steps.n: Adding ة makes it a noun for a covered place - a garden is covered by trees which provide shade and protection

steps.m: gardens (object of يُدْخِلْهُ - He will admit him into)
steps.n: The ات makes it plural (many gardens). This is what someone is being admitted into, as mentioned in يُدْخِلْهُ (He will admit him)
The ٍ shows these are some gardens (not specific ones) that have rivers flowing beneath them, as mentioned in the next part of the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
تَ at start shows this is happening now (present tense)

This is a preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects the verb تَجْرِى (flow) with تَحْتِهَا (beneath it), showing that the rivers flow from beneath the gardens.
Becomes an adverb showing location
Adding vowel marks to show it's a location word
Added kasra to show this word connects to what comes after it
Added هَا to show this refers to the gardens (جَنَّاتٍ) mentioned earlier in the verse
Basic past tense verb form with fatha vowels
Changed from 'flowing' action to name of thing that flows (river)
Forms broken plural pattern أَفْعَال indicating abundance/multiplicity
Adding double dammah makes it indefinite (like saying 'a/some rivers')
Added 'the' (ال) at the start to make it specific rivers, and changed the ending to match its position in the sentence
Added damma to show this word is the subject of the verb تَجْرِى (flows/flowing) in this ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changes from verb (he lasted) to noun (one who lasts)
Makes the word plural to show multiple people lasting forever

steps.m: ones who last forever (plural, object of يُدْخِلْهُ)
steps.n: Adds a fatha at the end to show this describes who is being put into Paradise. The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) above it.
This shows something is inside or within something else
Added هَا which means 'it' and refers back to the gardens (جَنَّاتٍ) mentioned earlier in the ayah. This shows who or what is being stayed in.
The alif with bar (ٰ) changes 'this' to 'that', showing something farther away
The لِكَ part is added to show we're pointing to something far away

steps.m: and that (pointing to Allah's limits mentioned before)
steps.n: The وَ connects this to the previous statement about Allah's limits (حُدُودُ ٱللَّهِ)
Changes verb 'to succeed' into the noun 'success'
Added ال to make it definite ('the success' instead of just 'success') and ُ to show it's the subject being pointed to by ذٰلِكَ (that)
Changed the verb into an adjective by adding ي between second and third root letters
Added ال to make it definite and ُ to show it's describing Allah in this ayah
This is a combination of two particles: و (and) + من (who/whoever)

steps.m: to disobey or go against authority

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: he disobeys (after مَن in a condition)
steps.n: The ي at the end is dropped because it comes after مَن (whoever) in a conditional sentence
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity'
Changed from the general word for 'god' to the special name 'Allah' by adding ال (al-) and joining letters together. The doubled L (shown by shadda) makes it stronger.

steps.m: Allah (as the object of يَعْصِ - disobeys)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show Allah is being acted upon by the verb 'disobeys'. Also changed the initial alif to a wasla since it comes after another word.
The و makes it mean 'someone who does this action a lot'
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb يَعْصِ (disobeys)
Added هُ at the end to show it means 'His messenger', referring to Allah's messenger

steps.m: and His messenger (being acted upon by يَعْصِ)

Added vowels and doubled middle letter to show emphasis
Added تَ to show doing action to oneself or beyond limits
Added يَ to show present tense and male subject
Added و to connect this word to previous phrase. This word describes crossing Allah's limits (حُدُودَهُۥ) mentioned right after it.

Added fatha to show this word is receiving the action of crossing/transgressing (يَتَعَدَّ)
Added هُ to show these are Allah's limits being referred to
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Added hamza with fatha at beginning to show causative meaning
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Changed final vowel to sukun for jussive mood
Added object pronoun هُ (him) to show who is being made to enter

steps.m: eternally/forever (describing how they will be in نَارًا/fire)
steps.n: The ًا ending makes this word describe how the action happens - they will be in the fire eternally. The alif (ا) is written as a long alif (ٰ) in this word.

steps.m: in it (referring to نَارًا - the fire)
steps.n: Added ها to show we're talking about the fire (نَارًا) mentioned earlier in the verse
Starting with the basic preposition that means 'for' or 'to'
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it

steps.m: and for him (connected to عَذَابٌ - punishment)
steps.n: Added هُ at the end to show this is 'for him'. The ۥ mark shows this is a long vowel. This phrase connects to عَذَابٌ (punishment) in the ayah, showing who will receive the punishment.

Adding the double dammah makes this an indefinite noun - meaning 'a punishment' instead of 'the punishment'

steps.m: to be easy, light, or despised

steps.n: Added short vowels (fatha-kasra-fatha) to make 'he was despised' in the past tense
Added مُ at start to show 'one who' and changed middle vowels to make it mean 'the doer' instead of 'was done to'. The و drops because it's between two vowels.

Added tanween to show this is describing the punishment (عَذَابٌ) mentioned in the ayah

steps.m: and those who (feminine plural) (subject of يَأْتِينَ)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it in the Quran.
Connected the root letters together
Added يَ at start to make it mean 'he' in present tense, and added dots/marks to show how to say it
Added نَ at the end to show multiple feminine subjects

steps.m: to go beyond proper limits, to be shamefully bad
one who/that which is shamefully bad

Added ة to make it refer to one specific instance of the shameful action

steps.m: the shameful act (object of يَأْتِينَ - they commit)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ to show we're talking about a specific shameful act. Added َ at the end because it's the object of the verb 'they commit'

steps.m: from (connects نِسَآئِكُمْ 'your women' to the sentence)
steps.n: This is a preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects 'your women' (نِسَآئِكُمْ) to the rest of the sentence, showing which women are being talked about.
This makes it a broken plural form meaning 'women'
The word 'women' gets 'your' (plural you) added at the end, with some letter changes to make it flow better
Basic past tense verb form meaning 'he did the action'
Added است at the beginning to show asking for witnesses
Changed from past tense 'he sought' to a command meaning 'you seek!' by changing the vowels at the end
Added وا at the end to change from 'seek witnesses' to 'you all seek witnesses' - like telling a group to do something
Added فَ at beginning to show this follows from previous statement
This is a basic preposition that shows something is on top of or against something else
The ى changes to يْ before adding pronouns

steps.m: upon/against them (feminine plural) (referring to نِّسَآئِكُمْ - your women)
steps.n: Added هِنَّ which shows this applies to a group of females. In this ayah, it refers back to the women mentioned earlier (نِّسَآئِكُمْ)
Added vowels to connect the root letters
Making the word into a noun form
Makes it into a feminine number word by adding أ and ة

steps.m: four (as the object being called for in فَٱسْتَشْهِدُوا۟)
steps.n: Added tanween fatHa to show this is the object being requested in the command 'call for four (witnesses)'
This is a basic preposition meaning 'from' that shows where something starts or comes from
Added emphasis (shadda) to the م because it comes after another similar sound
Added كُمْ at the end to show this means 'from you all' (masculine plural)
This is a conditional particle that introduces a condition. In this ayah, it introduces what should happen if the witnesses testify.
The فَ shows this is connected to the previous command to bring witnesses. It means 'then if they testify...'

steps.m: to witness/testify to something seen or known

steps.n: Adding vowels makes this a past tense verb meaning 'he witnessed'
Added وا with damma before it to show multiple people did this action
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Changed to a command by adding أ at the start and changing the middle vowel to 'i' - tells one person to hold/keep something
Added وا to make it a command for a group
Added فَ to show this action follows from what was mentioned before
هن shows we are talking about multiple women
This is a preposition that shows location or containment. In this ayah, it connects to ٱلْبُيُوتِ (the houses) to show where something happens.
This is a broken plural form, changing the internal structure of the word to show multiple houses

steps.m: the houses (in genitive case after فِي)
steps.n: Two changes happen here: 1) ٱلْ makes it specific houses 2) The ِ ending shows it comes after the word فِي (in/inside)
This is the basic form of the word that shows when something reaches its limit or end point
Adding the shadda makes the meaning stronger and more definite

steps.m: until (connects to يَتَوَفَّىٰهُنَّ - takes their souls)
steps.n: The alif maksura (ىٰ) at the end shows this word connects what comes before it to what happens after it. Here it connects keeping women in houses until death takes them.
Doubling the middle letter makes the meaning stronger - showing that something is taken or collected very thoroughly
Adding تَ at the start shows the subject does the action to himself or for his own benefit
Adding يَ shows this is happening in the present/future
The ى at the end is written with an alif maqsura (ىٰ) when it's at the end of a word
هُنَّ is added to show the verb is acting on multiple feminine objects
Added vowel marks to turn the root into a noun meaning the event or state of dying
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific death. This word is the subject doing the action of claiming/taking in the verse.
Adding ُ (u) at the end shows this word is doing the action (subject)
This is a connecting word that joins two possible outcomes: either 'death takes them' or 'Allah makes for them a way'. It shows these are alternative possibilities.
Root letters got fatha marks to make 'he' past tense verb
Added يَ at beginning to show present tense, with ُ at end to show basic present form
Changed the ending vowel to fatha to show subjunctive mood
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
Added the U mark at the end to show this is the subject
This is a preposition that shows direction or purpose. In this ayah, it connects to the verb يَجْعَلَ (make/establish) to show what is being made or established.
Adds a masculine singular pronoun to show who or what something is for
Changes the pronoun from masculine singular to feminine plural to match that we're talking about a group of women

steps.m: to make a path or way

steps.n: Adding ي and vowels changes it from verb to noun meaning 'path'
Added tanween fatHa to show this is the object that Allah makes/sets in the phrase 'or Allah makes for them a way'
Added ان at the end to show we're talking about exactly two people or things
Added ال at the beginning to make it more specific and change it from just pointing to something to describing who does something

steps.m: and the two who (subject of يَأْتِيَٰنِهَا)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to mean 'and'. Added ِ at the end to show this is the subject doing the action in يَأْتِيَٰنِهَا (they both commit it). The ٱ and ّ are just pronunciation helpers.
Added present tense prefix يَ and vowel marks to show ongoing action
Added ان to show this is happening in the present tense with two people
Added هَا to show who is being approached
This is a preposition that shows something coming from somewhere or someone
Added the kasra vowel mark under the م to show how it's pronounced

steps.m: from you all (who do يَأْتِيَٰنِهَا/commit it)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show this is talking about a group of people. This refers back to those who commit the act mentioned in يَأْتِيَٰنِهَا.
Changes from verb 'to harm' into the noun 'harm' by adding ى
Makes the verb causative - meaning 'to make something harmful'
Changes to passive voice - someone/something receives the harm
Changed to 'you all' form and added هُمَا ('them both') at the end to show two people being harmed

steps.m: then you all harm them both (connected to يَأْتِيَٰنِهَا)
steps.n: Added فَ at beginning to connect this command with the previous statement about those who commit the act (يَأْتِيَٰنِهَا)

steps.m: if (introduces the condition 'if they both repent')
steps.n: This is a conditional particle that introduces a condition - in this case, it starts 'if they both repent...'

steps.m: then if (connects to previous statement about punishing them)
steps.n: The فَ shows this condition follows after the previous statement about punishment. It connects the two ideas - first punish them, then if they repent...

steps.m: to return back from wrong to right

steps.n: Added fathas to make it past tense. The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a special kind of verb.
Adding ا at the end shows that two people did the action together. This connects to the earlier word وَٱلَّذَانِ (those two who) in the verse.
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters into a past tense verb
Adding أ makes it transitive/causative - 'to make good' instead of 'to be good'. This is a verb, so it doesn't have case endings like nouns do.
Adding alif at the end shows two people did the action together (referring back to وَٱلَّذَانِ 'the two who' from earlier in the verse)

steps.m: and they both reformed/corrected (something) (connected to تَابَا - 'they both repented')
steps.n: و connects this to the previous verb تَابَا (they both repented), showing these actions happened together
Connected the root letters with fatha to create basic past tense verb
Adding 'أ' at the start changes the meaning from 'turn' to 'turn away' - like turning your back on something
Changes to present tense by adding vowel marks - the ُ at the end shows 'I' am doing it
Adding وا changes it from 'I turn away' to 'you all turn away'
فَ connects this command to what came before it, meaning 'so' or 'then'
This is a preposition that means 'away from'. The fatha and sukun help with pronunciation.

steps.m: away from them both (referring to the two people mentioned in يَأْتِيَٰنِهَا)
steps.n: Added هُمَا which means 'them both' - this refers back to the two people mentioned earlier in the verse who are doing the action.
The alif is written with a special mark (آ) at the end to show it's being stretched in pronunciation, but this doesn't change the meaning.
Starting with the basic particle that shows emphasis
Adding the shadda and kasra makes the emphasis stronger. This word emphasizes the importance of what follows it - in this case, it's emphasizing that Allah (اللَّهَ) has these qualities.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding specific vowel patterns
Added ال (the) and merged it with the word, doubled the ل with shadda, and changed the first letter to show this is the proper name for the one true God
Added fatha at the end because Allah is the object of إِنَّ (indeed/verily) in this ayah
Connected the root letters together with fatha marks to make them pronounceable
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas. This is a common change with weak middle letters. The meaning also changes to past tense.

steps.m: to turn back or return (from wrong to right)

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb

steps.m: one who constantly turns back in forgiveness
steps.n: The doubled و and added ا make it mean someone who does this action repeatedly or intensely

steps.m: one who constantly turns back in forgiveness (describing كَانَ)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this word describes how Allah was (كَانَ) in this verse
Adding vowels makes this a proper verb meaning 'to be merciful'
Changed from an action (being merciful) to describing someone who is very merciful
Adding shadda makes the meaning even stronger

steps.m: extremely merciful (describing Allah in this ayah)
steps.n: Adding the -an ending (tanween with alif) shows this is describing something in the sentence - here it's describing Allah
This is an emphasis word that makes what comes after it stronger, like saying 'truly' or 'certainly' in English
Adding ما changes this from a word that just adds emphasis to one that means 'only' or 'just'. It restricts what comes after it, like saying 'The only thing that...' in English

steps.m: to turn back or return (especially to Allah in repentance)

steps.n: Changed from basic root letters to a noun meaning 'repentance' by adding vowels
Added ة to make it a specific instance of repentance
Added ال to make it definite and doubled the ت as a result
Added ُ to show this is the subject of إِنَّمَا (indeed/verily), and changed اَل to ٱل for proper pronunciation
This is a basic preposition that shows something is on top of or upon something else. In this ayah, it connects 'repentance' to 'Allah', showing that accepting repentance is upon Allah.
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the ل, making it a unique name for The One True God

steps.m: of Allah (the doer of يَتُوبُ - accepts repentance)
steps.n: Added ِ at the end to show possession or connection, and changed initial alif to wasl form. In this ayah, Allah is the one who accepts repentance from those who turn back to Him.
This is a relative pronoun that connects parts of a sentence, like saying 'the one who' in English
Changed from singular to plural by adding ين at the end
Added لِ at the beginning to show this is 'for' or 'to' those people. This connects to the verb يَتُوبُ (accepts repentance) later in the verse.
Basic meaning of doing or performing work
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show past tense meaning
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added ون at the end to show multiple people doing the action
Root meaning relates to something being bad or evil
Changed from a verb meaning 'to harm' into a noun meaning 'harm/evil' by adding a ُ (u sound) on first letter
Added ال to point to specific harm/evil. The ل combines with س making it stronger (shown by ّ)
Added َ at the end to show this is what's being done in يَعْمَلُونَ (they do). The ٓ shows the و is stretched when reading.
Basic meaning of lacking knowledge
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Added alif and ة to make it into a noun describing the state of being ignorant

steps.m: with/by ignorance (describes how السُّوٓءَ/evil was done)
steps.n: Added بِ at start to mean 'with/by' and tanwin kasra at end to show it's indefinite. This word explains how the evil mentioned earlier (السُّوٓءَ) was done - out of ignorance rather than intentionally.

This is a connecting word that shows one thing happens after another. The shadda (double letter mark) makes the 'm' sound stronger to emphasize the sequence. In this ayah, it connects to the word يَتُوبُونَ (they repent) to show when the repentance happens.
Basic meaning of returning or turning back
Root letters ت-و-ب combined with vowels to make a noun meaning 'the act of repenting'
Added يَ at the start to show 'he' is doing the action now (present tense)

steps.m: they return in repentance (subject of يَعْمَلُونَ)
steps.n: Added ونَ to show multiple people doing the action. This word is connected to يَعْمَلُونَ (they do) in the ayah, referring to the same group of people.
Added kasra (ِ) under م to show this is a preposition. In this ayah, it connects to the word قَرِيبٍ (near/soon) to mean 'from soon' or 'soon after'.
Basic meaning of nearness or proximity

steps.m: soon (in relation to يَتُوبُونَ - they repent)
steps.n: The root 'to be near' becomes an adjective 'soon/near' by adding vowels. The -in ending shows it describes when the repentance happens.
Same word but used to mean 'near' instead of 'soon' - connected to another word through ownership
This is the basic form of the demonstrative pronoun used to point to things that are far away
Added ائك to point to something far away more strongly
Added proper vowel marks and extended alif for proper pronunciation

steps.m: so those (connecting to what was mentioned before)
steps.n: Added فَ to connect this to the previous statement, showing a result or consequence
Basic meaning of returning or turning back
Added vowel marks to make it a present tense verb meaning 'I/you/he repent'
Added يَ at the beginning to make it mean 'he repents' instead of just 'repentance'. Shows it's happening now or in the future. Connected to يَعْمَلُونَ in the ayah.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' by adding vowel marks
Added 'the' to make it specific/definite
Added damma to show this word is the subject

steps.m: upon (ready to connect to a pronoun)
steps.n: The ى changes to يْ when connecting to pronouns like هِمْ
Added هِمْ to show this applies to a group of people (them)
Basic meaning of being or existing
Changed from basic root to past tense 'he was' by adding vowel marks and turning و into ا

steps.m: and he was (connects to عَلِيمًا حَكِيمًا - All-Knowing, All-Wise)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this word with what comes after it in the ayah, showing that Allah was and continues to be All-Knowing and All-Wise
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
Added the U mark at the end to show this is the subject
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed pattern to show this is a permanent, intense quality of Allah
The ًا ending shows this word is describing Allah in this sentence
Basic meaning of wisdom and judgment
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a past tense verb
Changed the verb into an adjective describing someone who has wisdom
Added double fatha to show this is being used as an object in the sentence
Adding vowels to make it a usable past tense verb
Added ت with kasra at the end to show this is about a feminine subject (she)
Added و at the beginning to connect this with what came before, like saying 'and'
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to make it pronounceable

steps.m: one act of returning from wrong (repentance)

Added ال to make it specific and doubled the ت because it comes after ال
Changed اَل to ٱل for easier pronunciation. Added ُ to show this is the subject of لَيْسَتِ (is not)

Adding ال and changing ذا to ذي turns it from 'this' into 'the one who' - like changing from pointing at something to describing someone
Adding ين at the end makes it plural - now it points to many people instead of just one
Adding لِ at the beginning means 'for' or 'to'. This word connects to يَعْمَلُونَ (they do) in the ayah to show who is being talked about.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. This describes 'those who' (الَّذِينَ) in the verse.
The و changes to ي and gets doubled to show something that regularly causes harm
Adding ات makes it refer to multiple harmful actions. This word is being acted upon in the phrase 'they do bad deeds'
Adding ال makes it refer to specific bad deeds that were mentioned

steps.m: of the harmful actions/of the bad deeds
steps.n: The ِ shows this word is being described by another word that comes after it
Connected the base letters with fatha to make them pronounceable
Added shadda to strengthen the meaning of the limit
Added a special 'ى' at the end to show something continues until a certain point
Added small alif (ٓ) to connect with the word إِذَا that comes after it, showing these words work together to mean 'until when'

This is a time particle that connects two events. Here it connects death approaching (حَضَرَ) with what happens at that moment. The ا at the end makes it specifically about future or conditional time.

steps.m: he came/arrived (to أَحَدَهُمُ ٱلْمَوْتُ - one of them, death)
steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past. In this ayah, this word is describing death coming to someone.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form a noun meaning a single unit or individual
Added هُم to show this refers to 'one of them' (referring to those who do evil deeds mentioned earlier in the verse)
Added final damma because this word is the subject of the verb حَضَرَ (approached/came to)
Adding the fatha mark changes this from a basic root into a noun meaning 'death'
Added ُ (damma) at the end to show 'death' is doing the action (subject)
Adds 'al' (ال) at the start to make it 'the death' instead of just 'death'
Added vowel marks (fathas) to the root letters to make it mean 'he said' in the past
The middle letter و changes to ا because it has a fatha and comes between two fathas
This is the basic emphatic particle that strengthens what comes after it
Adding the double mark (shadda) and fatha makes this word stronger - like saying 'definitely!' instead of just 'yes'

steps.m: indeed I (subject of قَالَ - he said)
steps.n: Adding ى at the end shows this is about me. This word is what's being said in قَالَ (he said).

steps.m: to return back from wrong to right

steps.n: The weak letter و drops out when we make the command form. Added damma (ُ) on first letter and sukun (ْ) on second letter to make the command form.

steps.m: I repented (speaking in قَالَ he said)
steps.n: Added تُ at the end to show 'I' did this action in the past. This word appears after قَالَ (he said) in the ayah, showing someone is speaking about their own repentance.
Adding ال makes it refer to the specific present moment
The أ and و combine to form آ, which is common in Arabic when these letters come together
Adding ال makes it specifically refer to 'the' present moment rather than any present moment
Adding the fatha at the end shows this word is being used as a time expression
This is a negative particle that negates what comes after it
Added و at the beginning to connect this negation with what came before it
Starting with the basic demonstrative particle that points to something specific
Added ين to make it refer to multiple people
Added ال at the beginning to make it definite and specific
Added final vowel mark and standardized spelling with hamza wasl and shadda. This word is the subject that يَعْمَلُونَ (they do/work) describes in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form the basic noun
Added يَ at the start to make it mean 'he' and show it's happening now, changed inside vowels to make it a doing word instead of a thing
Added ونَ to show multiple people doing the action
Added damma and sukun to show proper pronunciation of the pronoun
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it. This word connects to كُفَّارٌ (disbelievers) which comes right after it in the ayah.

steps.m: to reject or cover up truth

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Changed to show someone who does a lot of disbelieving by doubling the middle letter and adding ٌ at the end
Changed from singular (one disbeliever) to plural (many disbelievers) by changing the 'a' sound to 'u' sound at the start
Base demonstrative pronoun for plural
Added distance marker to show the things being pointed to are far away
Added diacritical marks to show proper pronunciation: damma on أ, sukun on و, alif with madda, kasra on ئ, and fatha on ك
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Added أَ at beginning and sukun on middle letter to show causing something to happen
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action. In this ayah, this word is followed by لَهُمْ عَذَابًا (for them a punishment), showing what was prepared.
Added fatha to show the vowel sound
Added هُمْ to show who the action is for (them)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Long ا after second root letter makes this a noun meaning the result of punishment. This word is the object (what is being prepared) in the phrase 'We have prepared for them a punishment'.
The ًا ending shows this is the object of أَعْتَدْنَا (We have prepared)
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form basic past tense verb
Form فَعِيل for intensive adjectives describing qualities, especially painful or intense qualities
This word describes عَذَابًا (punishment) in the ayah, showing what kind of punishment it is

steps.m: to point to or indicate something

steps.n: The middle letter is doubled to make the questioning stronger
Added يا with extended alif (ٰ) to call attention, like saying 'hey!' to someone
Added short vowels (fatha and damma) to make it a proper calling word meaning 'O you'
Added ها to make it a proper calling word
This is the basic form meaning 'who'. It's used to point to someone or something we're about to describe.
Adding ين at the end makes it refer to many people instead of just one person
Adding ال at the beginning makes it point to specific people - in this case, it's pointing to the believers who are being addressed
Added the shadda on ل and fatha at the end to show proper pronunciation. The hamza at the start is written differently but pronounced the same.
Adding alif makes it mean 'to actively put trust in something'
Added وا at the end to show multiple people did this action
Added a sukun mark to help with pronunciation when connecting to next word
This is a basic word that makes things negative. In this ayah, it's saying something is not allowed or lawful. It's like adding 'not' in English to make a positive statement negative.
Added vowels (َ) to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he/it was lawful'
When two same letters come together at the end, they combine with a shadda

steps.m: it becomes lawful (in relation to لَا before it meaning 'not')
steps.n: Changed from past to present tense by adding يَ at start. The word means 'it becomes lawful' but with لَا before it means 'it is not lawful'
Added fatha to show the vowel sound on the letter lam
Added ك with damma to show it refers to multiple people being addressed
Added م with sukun to complete the plural pronoun. This word is the indirect object of يَحِلُّ (it is lawful/permissible) in this ayah.
Basic particle that connects ideas. Here it introduces what is not permissible - inheriting women by force.

steps.m: to receive something from someone who has passed away

steps.n: Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb meaning 'to inherit'
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing final vowel to ُ
Changed from 'he' to 'you' by replacing يَ with تَ
Added وا to show multiple people are being addressed
Forms irregular plural for 'women' - this is a special pattern used for groups of people
Added 'the' and made it the object of the verb 'inherit' (تَرِثُوا) in this ayah. The ا is lengthened to آ for emphasis.
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to create a noun meaning the state or feeling of dislike
Added tanween alif (ًا) at the end to make it describe how an action is done. In this ayah, it describes how inheriting women is being done - by force/unwillingly.
This is a basic negative particle that negates what comes after it
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense with ت prefix for 'you' and adjusted vowels
Added و to show multiple people (all male or mixed group) are doing the action
Added هُنَّ to show the action is being done to multiple women (referring to wives in this verse)

steps.m: to go away or depart (taking something)

steps.n: Added vowel marks to make it 'he went away' in the past tense
Changes to present tense verb form. The ت at the beginning shows 'you' are doing the action.
Adds وا at the end to show many people (all male or mixed group) are doing the action together

steps.m: so that you (all) may go away (taking something)
steps.n: Adds لِ at the beginning to show this is the purpose or intended result of the previous action (not allowing you to inherit women against their will)

steps.m: to divide or separate into parts

steps.n: Added vowel marks to connect letters and make it a noun meaning 'some' or 'part'

steps.m: with some (of what you gave them)
steps.n: Added بِ at beginning to show 'with' and kasra at end because it's connected to what comes after it in the sentence

steps.m: what (referring to what you gave them)
steps.n: This is a relative pronoun that connects to ءَاتَيْتُمُوهُنَّ (you gave them). The آ is just a spelling variation of ا.

steps.m: what (referring to what you gave them)
steps.n: The alif is written as آ instead of ا. This is just a spelling variation that doesn't change the meaning or pronunciation.
Adding long alif makes it stronger - means 'give' instead of just 'come'
Added ي with sukun as the final root letter
Added تُم to show past tense for 'you all'
Added و to prepare for next form, but meaning stays same
Added هُنَّ to show the feminine plural object (them)
Added shadda to emphasize the meaning. This is a particle that introduces exceptions or conditions.

steps.m: except (in this ayah, introduces the exception to the prohibition mentioned before)
steps.n: Added the long alif at the end. This is a common spelling variation in the Quran that shows where to pause when reading. In this ayah, it introduces the only case where the previous prohibition doesn't apply.

This is a connecting word that joins two parts of the sentence together. Here it connects 'it is not permissible for you' with 'to inherit women'. Think of it like glue that holds the meaning together.
Added يَ at the beginning to show it's happening now or in the future. The ْ (sukun) helps with pronunciation.
Added نَ at the end to show that multiple females are doing the action. This word is part of the exception phrase 'except that' (إِلَّآ أَن) in the verse.

steps.m: to exceed proper bounds, be shamefully immoral

steps.n: Adding vowels turns the root into a past tense verb meaning 'became immoral'
Changed the verb into a noun for a specific instance of immoral behavior by adding alif and ة
Added the preposition بِ to show this is something being done with/by means of

steps.m: with an immoral act (object of يَأْتِينَ)
steps.n: Added tanween kasra (ٍ) to show this is indefinite and affected by the preposition بِ. The alif is written as a long alif (ٰ) which is just a writing variation.
Added vowels and doubled middle letter to show active meaning
Added مُ prefix to show who does the action
Added ة to make it feminine

steps.m: and you all live closely with

Added feminine plural pronoun suffix meaning 'them (feminine)'

steps.m: to know or recognize something as good

Changed from 'he knew' to 'something that is known/recognized as good' - like changing from action to describing what the action created
Added 'with' (بِ) at start and made it definite with ال, plus kasra at end to show connection

steps.m: then if (connects to كَرِهْتُمُوهُنَّ - you dislike them)
steps.n: This combines two particles: فَ (then/so) and إِن (if) to show that one thing follows another conditionally. Here it connects to the next word كَرِهْتُمُوهُنَّ (you dislike them) to mean 'then if you dislike them'.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show past tense
Changed from 'he' to 'I' by adding تُ at the end
Changed from 'I' to 'you all' by adding -تُمُو ending

This root carries the basic meaning of possibility or hope
Added fatha vowels and changed final ي to ى which is common in words ending with ي
Added فَ at beginning to show this follows from what came before
Added alif khanjariya (small alif) and elongation mark for pronunciation

Added vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense with 'you' as subject
Added وا to show multiple people are doing the action
Connected the root letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter to form the basic noun meaning 'thing'

steps.n: Added tanween alif (ًا) at the end to show this is the object being disliked in the phrase. The hamza is written smaller (ٔ) when it has tanween.

steps.n: Converting from root letters to past tense verb meaning 'he made/placed'
Changes from past tense to present tense because it comes after 'perhaps that' (عَسَىٰٓ أَن) which needs a present tense verb

steps.m: one who is worshipped, a deity

Changed to the unique name of The One True God by adding ال and merging letters. The ُ shows this is the subject of يَجْعَلَ (places/puts) in this ayah.
This is a basic preposition that shows something is inside or within something else
Added ه which means 'it' to show what is being referred to. In this ayah, it refers back to شَيْـًٔا (something) that was mentioned earlier.
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to make a noun meaning something that has this quality
Added tanween alif to show this word is receiving the action of Allah placing (يَجْعَلَ) much good in it
Added vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Changed the vowel and added ي to make it into an adjective meaning 'much' or 'many'
Added ًا at the end to make it describe how something is done. In this ayah, it describes how much good (خَيْرًا) Allah puts into something.
This is a combination of two particles: و (and) + إن (if)
This is the basic meaning showing what someone wants or seeks
Changed the root letters into a past tense verb by adding the vowel 'aa' in the middle
Adding أَ at the start makes this a transitive verb - something you do to something else
Adding تُمْ at the end shows this is 'you all' (plural) in the past tense
The مُ was already there in both words - this is just a spelling change where دْتُ becomes دتُّ with a shadda. The meaning stays the same.

Added fatha at end to match sentence grammar

steps.m: to pair or join things together

Base root meaning
Adding fatha vowels between root letters

steps.m: and if (connects to خِفْتُمْ - you fear)

steps.m: to fear or be afraid of something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed the vowel to make it a command form
Added تُ to show who did the action
Changed from 'I feared' to 'you all feared' by adding مْ at the end to show it's talking to a group

steps.m: to split or separate into different sides

steps.n: The ا shows the separation is happening between two sides
Added fatha at the end because this is what is being feared in the sentence (the object of خِفْتُمْ - you feared)
Adding vowels changes this from a root into a usable word
Added kasra at the end to show it connects to what comes after it

steps.m: between them both (referring to the two spouses)

Connected the root letters with fatha to make past tense verb
Changed from 'he sent' to 'send!' by making it a command - just like in English we drop 'he' and use the basic verb form
Added فَ to show this command follows from previous statement
Changed to plural command by adding وا and adjusting the alif to ٱ
Adding vowel marks creates the basic past tense verb form

steps.m: an arbitrator (object of فَٱبْعَثُوا - send forth)
steps.n: Changed from 'he judged' (verb) to 'a judge' (noun) and added tanween to show it's the object being sent
Basic preposition showing where something comes from or starts
The م gets a shadda (double letter) because it comes right after a word ending in ـًا (an), making the sound flow better
Added sukun to show the 'n' sound stops clearly

steps.m: to belong to a family, to be settled

Added kasra to show this word is connected to what comes after it
Added هِۦ to show this is 'his family'. The small ۦ after هِ is just a pronunciation mark.
Changed the root into a noun for a person who judges

steps.m: and a judge (object of فَٱبْعَثُوا۟/send forth)

This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or what group it belongs to. Here it connects the word حَكَمًا (arbitrator) with أَهْلِهِۦ (his family), showing the arbitrator should be chosen from his family.
Added a shadda (ّ) on the م because it comes after a word ending in a vowel sound. This helps connect the words smoothly when reading.
Forms the basic noun meaning family/relatives
Shows this word belongs to or is connected to something else
Added feminine possessive pronoun suffix هَا with elongated alif (آ) meaning 'her'. The word follows مِنْ (from) in the ayah.

Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb
Added يُ at start to make it present tense. The و changed to ي because it's between two vowels. Shows something happening now rather than in the past.
Added ا at the end to show two people doing the action instead of one

steps.m: to make right, to fix, to repair

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create basic past tense verb
Added إِ at start and ا after second root letter to create noun meaning 'the act of doing something'

steps.m: reconciliation (as the goal they both seek)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the object (what is being sought) in relation to يُرِيدَآ (they both want)

steps.m: to harmonize, to make things fit together

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb

Changed from past to present tense by adding يُ at start, showing 'he' is doing the action now instead of before
Added kasra at end to show this is part of a conditional phrase
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding vowel marks
Added 'al' to make it specific/definite - 'the god' instead of just 'a god'
Added damma to show this word is the subject of the sentence

steps.m: to separate or be between things

steps.n: Adding vowels changes this from a root into a usable word meaning 'between'

steps.m: between them both (referring to the husband and wife)

Changed و to ا because it's a hollow verb (has weak middle letter)

steps.n: The pattern فَعِيل shows someone who has this quality in the most complete way. When used for Allah, it indicates His perfect knowledge of all things.

steps.m: All-Aware (as a description of اللَّه after كَانَ)

Changed from 'he worshipped' to a command by adding 'u' at start and putting sukoon at end

Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. The shadda shows the ل is doubled.

steps.m: Allah (as the object of وَٱعْبُدُوا۟ - worship)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show Allah is the object being worshipped in the command 'worship Allah'

steps.m: to make something a partner or equal with something else

steps.m: you all make a partner (plural command: don't associate!)

Added a small alif (ۦ) after هِ which is just a pronunciation helper that doesn't change the meaning
Added fatha on ش and sukoon on ي to make it mean 'thing'. This word is what's being acted on by the verb تُشْرِكُوا (associate/join in worship) - meaning 'don't associate anything with Him'.

Made the word definite by adding 'the'

The بِ means 'to' or 'for', showing direction or connection

steps.m: and to the owner of (connected to إِحْسٰنًا - showing kindness)

Adds و to connect this word to the previous items in the list of those to whom kindness should be shown

Added ا after first letter to make the broken plural form - this is a common way to make plurals in Arabic
Changed the ending to show this word is connected to what comes before it in the sentence

steps.m: the poor people (connected to إِحْسٰنًا - showing kindness to)
steps.n: Makes it specific which poor people - the ones we should show kindness to
Adds conjunction 'and'

steps.m: a companion (object of فَسَآءَ - how evil)

steps.m: a portion (object of أُوتُوا - they were given)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the object being given in the phrase 'they were given a portion'
This is the basic form of the preposition that shows where something comes from or what something is made of
The n sound blends into the next letter, shown by the shadda mark
The fatha at the end connects this word to the next word (ٱلْكِتٰبِ) showing it means 'from the Book'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed the pattern to show the result of writing - the written thing (book)
Added ال to make it specific
Added kasra at the end because it comes after مِنَ (from/of) in مِّنَ ٱلْكِتٰبِ (from/of the book)

steps.m: to exchange, to buy and sell

steps.n: Adding ت in middle makes it Form VIII, showing someone doing action for themselves
Added plural ending ون to show multiple people doing the action. This word connects to يُرِيدُونَ (they want) in the ayah, showing the same group is doing both actions.

steps.m: to wander off the right path

steps.n: Connected the letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
Added alif and ة to make it into a noun describing the action itself
Added ال to point to a specific instance of wandering off, and doubled the ض because it comes after ال

steps.m: the act of wandering off (as object of يَشْتَرُونَ - they purchase)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this is what's being purchased in the phrase 'they purchase misguidance'
Added يُ at start and changed middle letter to ي to make 'he wants' in present tense
Adds a 'u' sound at the end to show this is the main verb in the sentence

steps.m: they want/desire (referring to those who were given a portion)
steps.n: Changed from singular (he) to plural (they) by adding ون. This refers back to الَّذِينَ (those who) from earlier in the verse.

steps.m: and they want/desire (connecting to يَشْتَرُونَ - they purchase)
steps.n: Added وَ to connect this action to the previous verb يَشْتَرُونَ (they purchase), showing these actions happen together

This particle connects يُرِيدُونَ (they want) to تَضِلُّوا۟ (you go astray), showing what they want to happen - they want you to go astray. It's like the word 'that' in English when you say 'they want that you go astray'.
Adding fathas makes it a past tense verb meaning 'he went astray'
Changed from past to present by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowel to ِ (kasra). The ل is doubled with shaddah.
Changed from 'he' to 'you' by replacing يَ with تَ

steps.m: you all lose the way (after وَيُرِيدُونَ أَن - and they want that)
steps.n: Added وا to show multiple people are being addressed. This verb comes after 'they want that' in the ayah.
Adding ي and vowels creates the common pattern for 'path'
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the س with shadda

steps.m: the path (object of يَشْتَرُونَ - they purchase)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being purchased in the phrase 'they purchase the path'
Connected the root letters to form the basic word for deity
Added shadda on ل to make this the specific name for the one true God
Added fatha on the shadda to help with pronunciation
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject doing the action in أَعْلَمُ (knows best)
Added و at the beginning to connect this to the previous statement, and added hamza to help pronunciation
Connected the root letters with vowels to form the basic past tense verb
Added أَ at the beginning and changed vowels to form comparative meaning. In this ayah, Allah is describing that He knows better than anyone about the enemies.

steps.m: to be hostile, to show enmity

steps.n: Root meaning 'hostile' becomes 'enemy' by adding vowels and doubling the last letter to show it's a strong trait in someone
This changes the singular noun 'enemy' into its broken plural form 'enemies'. The hamza and alif pattern is common for making plurals.
Added كُمْ at the end to show possession by multiple people ('your' when talking to more than one person). The آ and ئِ spelling changes happen because of the suffix.
Added بِ at the beginning to show the meaning 'with' or 'by'. This preposition connects this word to the verb كَفَىٰ (is sufficient) in the ayah.

steps.m: he/it was enough (verb in بِٱللَّهِ وَلِيًّا وَكَفَىٰ بِٱللَّهِ نَصِيرًا)
steps.n: Added fathas to make past tense. The ى at the end is because this is a weak verb ending in ي.

steps.m: and he/it was enough (connects to بِٱللَّهِ - 'Allah is sufficient')
steps.n: Added و to connect this word to what came before it. The small alif (ٰ) shows this is the end of the word.
This is the base word - Allah's proper name. It's unique and doesn't come from a normal root pattern.
Added بِ at the beginning to show this is being used as a tool or means
Added kasra at the end to show connection to the word نَصِيرًا (helper) that comes after it in the ayah. Also changed to the formal written style with ٱ and شدّة.

steps.m: to protect, guard, be close to

steps.n: Adding vowels turns the root into a past tense verb meaning 'to protect/be close to'
Adding shadda shows this is someone who really protects or is very close in protection

steps.m: a close protector/guardian (object of كَفَىٰ - sufficient is)
steps.n: Adding tanween alif shows this word is receiving the action of كَفَىٰ (sufficient is) in this ayah
Added fathas to make it past tense 'he was enough' - the ى at the end is how we write ا when it comes last

steps.m: and he was enough/sufficient (connected to أَعْلَمُ - knows better)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to the previous statement about Allah knowing better. Also added the small alif (ٰ) to show the proper pronunciation of the final ى.
This is the base word - Allah's proper name. It's unique and doesn't come from a root.
Added shadda on ل to show it's pronounced strongly
Added kasra at the end to show this connects to something before it, like saying 'of Allah'

Added بِ at start to show Allah is being used as a helper or tool (like saying 'by Allah' or 'with Allah'). In this ayah, it's connected to the word كَفَىٰ (sufficient) - saying Allah is sufficient as a helper.
Added ي between ص and ر with vowels to show someone who does this action a lot
Added ًا (tanween with alif) to show this is indefinite (like 'a' in English) and is being acted upon. Allah is described as being sufficient as this kind of helper.
This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from

The shadda (ّ) appears because this word comes after a word ending in a similar sound. It shows the sounds blend together when speaking, but doesn't change the meaning. This word connects to الَّذِينَ (those who) to tell us who something is from.
This is the basic form used to point to someone or something specific
Added ين at the end to make it plural (more than one)
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific, like saying 'the ones who' instead of just 'who'
Changed the writing style of ال to ٱلَّ and added shadda. This word is the subject (doer) of the verb يُحَرِّفُونَ (they distort) in this ayah.
Basic root meaning
The root letters got vowels to make it a past tense verb 'he'. The middle letter و changed to ا which often happens with weak letters.
Added وا at the end to show multiple people did this action. This word is the subject of يُحَرِّفُونَ (they distort) in this ayah.

steps.m: to change or alter from the original path/direction
steps.n: Basic root meaning
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels
Added shadda to show intensive or repeated action
Changed to present tense by adding يُ prefix and adjusting vowels
Added plural masculine ending to show multiple people doing the action. The subject is 'those who became Jews' (الَّذِينَ هَادُوا) mentioned earlier in the verse.
Adding the basic vowel pattern for past tense verbs

steps.m: the words (object of يُحَرِّفُونَ - they distort)
steps.n: Changed from 'he spoke' (a verb) to 'the words' (a noun) by adding 'the' (ال) at the start. The fatha at the end shows it's receiving the action of distorting
This is a preposition that shows separation or moving away from something. Here it shows they are changing words away from their proper places.

steps.m: to put or place something somewhere

steps.n: Changed from root letters to a noun meaning 'placing' by adding vowels
Added مَ at start to show location, changed middle vowel to ِ
Added ا after first root letter to make it plural form
Added ِ to show this word is possessed by something after it
Added هِ to show these are his/its places
The shadda makes the meaning stronger and more emphatic
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense form
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. The middle و affects pronunciation.
Added ونَ to show multiple people doing the action
Added وَ (and) to connect this word with what comes before it

steps.m: to hear or listen to something

steps.n: Adding vowels turns the root letters into a complete past tense verb meaning 'he heard'

steps.m: we heard (part of what they say in وَيَقُولُونَ)
steps.n: Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action. This word appears in what they say, as indicated by وَيَقُولُونَ (and they say) in the verse.
Basic meaning of disobeying or not following commands
Added vowels and changed final ي to ى to make past tense singular form
Added نا at the end to show 'we' did the action, and the ى changes back to يْ before suffixes
Added و at beginning to connect this word with what came before it (سَمِعْنَا - we heard)
Connected the root letters together
Adding ٱ at start makes it an imperative (command) verb
Adds 'and' to connect this command with what came before
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to create a noun meaning 'other than'
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb يَقُولُونَ (they say)

steps.m: something that is heard/listened to (object of غَيْرَ)
steps.n: Changed to passive participle form to show what is being heard. This word follows غَيْرَ (other than) in the ayah.

steps.m: something that is heard/listened to (indefinite, object of غَيْرَ)
steps.n: Added tanwin kasra to show this is indefinite and follows غَيْرَ (other than)

steps.m: to tend to, to observe, to look after

steps.n: Changed to command form telling someone to do the action

steps.m: tend to us! observe us! look after us!
steps.n: Added نَا ('us') at the end to show we are asking someone to look after us

steps.m: and tend to us! and observe us!
steps.n: Added و ('and') at the start to connect this word to what came before it
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
The و changes to ي and gets doubled with shadda to show intensity

steps.m: with twisting/distortion (describing how they distort words in يُحَرِّفُونَ)
steps.n: Added tanween (double marks) and alif to make it describe 'how' they twist/distort words - like saying 'with twisting'
Changes from action to the body part used for that action
Changes single tongue to multiple tongues
Adds بِ to show these tongues are being used as tools
Shows these are specifically their tongues
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Changed from a verb (he pierced) to a noun (piercing) and added ًا to show it's the object
Adds 'and' to connect this with the previous actions. In this ayah, it describes how they harm with their speech.
Basic preposition showing location or containment
Added ى at the end to complete the preposition. This word connects to الدِّينِ (the religion) in this ayah, showing something happening 'in the religion'

steps.m: to follow a way or custom

steps.n: The kasra and long vowel ي together create a noun showing a complete system or way
The shadda intensifies the meaning by doubling the first root letter
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific religion rather than any religion
The kasra ending shows this word follows the preposition فِى (in/concerning) in this ayah
Basic conditional particle that introduces hypothetical situations
Added و at the beginning to connect this condition with what came before it
Particle that introduces a clause + attached pronoun 'they'
Adding the basic vowel pattern for past tense verbs
Changed middle letter و to ا and added وا at the end to make it 'they said' instead of 'he said'
Adding vowels turns the root into a complete past tense verb meaning 'he heard'
Changed the final vowel to sukun to prepare for adding 'we'
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' are doing the hearing. This word is part of what the Jews say, as quoted in وَيَقُولُونَ (and they say)
The root letters ط-و-ع become طَاعَ (he obeyed) in the past tense by changing و to ا. This is part of the phrase 'we heard and we obeyed'.
Adding أ at start and نَا at end changes 'he obeyed' to 'we obeyed' in past tense
Adding وَ connects this word to سَمِعْنَا (we heard) before it, showing both actions happened together
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he heard' to 'listen!' by making it a command - starts with 'i' sound and ends with no vowel
Added و at beginning to mean 'and', with hamzat wasl (ٱ) replacing regular alif
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed from 'he looked' to 'look!' by making it a command - added U sound at start and made middle letter silent
Added نَا at the end to show who is being looked at

steps.m: and look at us/grant us time
steps.n: Added وَ (and) at the beginning to connect it to previous phrases
The root letters ك-و-ن become كَانَ (he was) in past tense - the و changes to ا, which happens a lot with middle-و words

steps.m: it would have been (in response to لَوْ)
steps.n: The لَ here is connected to the word لَوْ (if) earlier in the verse, together meaning 'if...then it would have been.' This is a common pattern in conditional sentences.
The root letters خ-ي-ر form a basic noun خَير meaning 'good/better' by adding the fatha mark
Adding sukun on middle letter completes the noun pattern
Adding tanween alif shows this word is the object of the verb لَكَانَ (it would have been) in this ayah
Starting with the basic particle ل that shows direction or purpose
Adding emphasis with shadda shows this is definitely or surely happening
Adding هُمْ shows this applies to a group of males (or mixed group)

steps.m: to stand upright, to be straight/correct

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Changed to the pattern that means 'more/most' (like adding -er/-est in English). The middle letter و stays as is.

steps.m: and more/most straight (compared to سَمِعْنَا وَعَصَيْنَا)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to mean 'and' and fatha at end because this word is connected to خَيْرًا (better) in the ayah
