steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Added يَ at start and changed vowels to show it happens in present/future
Added وا to show multiple people doing the action
Added kasra to show this is a preposition meaning 'with' or 'by'
Added the pronoun هِ (him/it) to show what/who the preposition connects to. Here it refers back to الطَّاغُوتِ (false deities/evil)
Added a small alif (ۦ) after هِ to help with pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.
Basic meaning of wanting or desiring something
Changed root word to show 'I want' by adding kasra after first letter and turning و into ي
Added يُ at beginning to show present tense and ُ at end to show the subject is 'he'
Added وَ at beginning to connect this word with the previous statement about what they want
Adds ي after first letter to make it mean 'someone who is rebellious', like turning 'rebel' into 'rebel person'

steps.m: the one who is very rebellious (Satan)
steps.n: Adds 'the' (ال) at the start to make it specific, like naming Satan

steps.m: the one who is very rebellious (Satan) (subject of يُرِيدُ - wants)
steps.n: Shows this word is doing the action of يُرِيدُ (wants) in this verse
This is a connecting word that introduces what someone wants, thinks, or says. Here it connects 'they want' (يُرِيدُونَ) with what they want to do.

steps.m: he went off the right path
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: he makes (someone) go off the right path
steps.n: Added يُ at start to show present tense, changed middle vowel to kasra, and added shadda to show causing someone else to do the action

steps.m: he leads them astray (object of يُرِيدُ - wants)
steps.n: Added هُمْ to show who is being led astray. The fatha before هُمْ is needed to connect it smoothly. This word shows what the Shaytan (mentioned in يُرِيدُ) wants to do.

steps.m: to wander off the right path

steps.n: Changed from basic root letters to a noun meaning 'going astray' by adding the vowel marks
Added alif to make the meaning stronger and more complete

steps.m: a far straying (object of يُضِلَّهُمْ/lead them astray)
steps.n: Added tanween to show this is any straying (not specific). This word describes what Satan wants to do to them - he wants to lead them into a complete state of being lost from the right path.
Added ي between ع and د and vowels to make it an adjective meaning 'far'
Added ًا at the end to show this describes how far the misguidance is
This is a compound word made of 'wa' (and) + 'idha' (when)
Connected the root letters and added vowels to make the basic past tense form

steps.m: it was said (when لَهُمْ was said to them)
steps.n: Changed the vowels to show this is happening to someone rather than someone doing it. This word connects to لَهُمْ (to them) in the ayah.
Added fatha to show how the letter is pronounced

steps.m: to them (who are being told تَعَالَوْا۟)
steps.n: Added هُمْ which means 'them' (referring to multiple people). This word connects to تَعَالَوْا۟ (come) in the ayah, showing who is being told to come.
Added تَ at beginning and changed ending to show mutual action - coming towards someone

steps.m: come! (all of you) (command to the hypocrites mentioned in يَصُدُّونَ)
steps.n: Changed from 'he came up' to 'all of you come!' by adding وْا at the end - this makes it a command for a group
Basic preposition showing direction or destination. In this ayah, it connects to 'what Allah has revealed' (مَآ أَنزَلَ ٱللَّهُ), showing where they are being called to.
Added an alif symbol (ىٰ) at the end to show the correct pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.
Basic interrogative particle used to ask about things or concepts
The alif is elongated to add emphasis. In this ayah, it refers specifically to what Allah revealed.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb

steps.m: he sent down (what اللَّهُ sent down)
steps.n: Adding أَ changes the meaning from something happening on its own to someone causing it to happen. In this ayah, Allah is the one doing the sending down.
Changed the root into a noun for something that is worshipped
Added ال with special spelling (ٱل) and a shadda to show this is THE one true deity. The ُ shows this is the subject doing something in the sentence.
This is the basic preposition that shows direction or destination
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to the previous command 'come'. This shows where they are being told to come to.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'messenger' by adding vowels and و. This pattern shows someone whose main role is delivering messages.
Added ال (the) at the start. The ل combines with ر to make it stronger (doubled)
Added ِ at the end because it comes after the word إِلَىٰ (to)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks. The ى at the end is because this is a weak verb ending in ي.
The ى changes back to يْ before adding endings
Added تَ at the end to show 'you' did the action

steps.m: to pass through, to spend secretly

steps.n: Adding مُ at the start and changing vowels makes it mean 'someone who hides their true beliefs' - like a person who acts one way in public but another in private
Sound masculine plural form to indicate multiple hypocrites. This word is the object (what is being seen) in the sentence after the verb رأيت (you saw).
Adding ال makes it refer to specific hypocrites mentioned in the Quran
The word gets a small diagonal line (fatHa) at the end because it's being acted on by the verb 'you saw' - like saying 'you saw THE HYPOCRITES'
Connected the root letters and added shadda to show emphasis on middle letter
Changes from past to present tense by adding يَ at start

steps.m: they block/turn away (referring to the hypocrites/المنافقين)
steps.n: Changes from 'he blocks' to 'they block' by adding ون at the end
Adds the final vowel mark to complete the indicative mood marking
This is a preposition that shows movement away from something or someone
Added كَ at the end to show this is happening to 'you'. This word is receiving the action of يَصُدُّونَ (they turn away)
Root letters were joined with vowels and doubled middle letter (shadda) to make past tense 'he' form
Changed to noun form showing a lot of turning away

steps.m: turning away (emphasizing the action of يَصُدُّونَ)
steps.n: Added tanween to show this word emphasizes the action of the main verb يَصُدُّونَ (they turn away)
Added vowel marks to connect the letters: fatha on ك and sukun on ي

steps.m: So how? (connects to previous statement about their actions)
steps.n: The prefix فَ (fa) connects this question to what was mentioned before about their actions, asking 'So in light of what was mentioned, how can they act this way?'
This is the basic form of the word that indicates a point in time
Adding ا makes it refer to future or conditional events, like saying 'when' something happens. In this ayah, it's talking about when a difficulty strikes them.
The alif is written with a long mark (آ) to show it's pronounced longer, but this doesn't change the meaning
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Added أَ at the beginning to show this happened to something else
Added تْ to show a feminine subject, and the long alif (ٰ) replaces the original alif

steps.m: it (feminine) afflicted/struck them (referring to مُصِيبَةٌ afflicting them)
steps.n: Added هُم to show who was affected. The feminine subject refers to مُصِيبَةٌ (calamity) in the ayah.
Changed root letters into past tense verb by adding the vowel 'aa' in middle
Changed from past tense 'struck' to present form showing who/what is doing the striking
Added feminine ending to specify one instance of affliction/calamity
Added shadda on م and tanween to show this is the subject doing the striking in the phrase 'when a calamity strikes them'
Starting with the relative pronoun ما which means 'what' or 'that which'. Added fatha to show correct pronunciation.

steps.m: with what/that which (connects to قَدَّمَتْ - they sent forth)
steps.n: Added the preposition بِ (with/by) at the beginning. In this ayah, it connects to قَدَّمَتْ (they sent forth) to show 'with what they sent forth'.

steps.m: to go ahead or put forward

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
Adding shadda makes the action more intense and changes it from just going forward to actively sending something forward
The ت at the end shows a feminine subject did this action in the past
Root letters ي-د-ي combined to make the simple word for 'hand'
The word changes to broken plural form by adding hamza and yaa
Adding the final vowel mark completes the plural form
Adds possession suffix to show the hands belong to them
This is a connecting word (particle) that shows one thing happens after another, with some time passing in between

steps.m: then/thereafter (connecting جَآءُوكَ - they came to you)
steps.n: Added damma (ُ) and shadda (ّ) to complete the word. In this ayah, it connects to the next phrase 'they came to you', showing this happened after the previous events.
Added past tense vowels and changed the middle letter ي to ا because it's a hollow verb
Added و and changed the last vowel to show multiple people did the action
Added كَ to show the action was directed towards 'you'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb
Changed from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowel to ِ (kasra)

steps.m: they swear (referring to the hypocrites in this ayah)
steps.n: Changed from 'he swears' to 'they swear' by adding ون at the end - shows multiple people doing the action
This is the base word - the proper name of God in Arabic. The shadda shows emphasis on the ل.
The small diagonal line (ِ) under the last letter shows this means 'of Allah' or 'belonging to Allah'

steps.m: by Allah (object of يَحْلِفُونَ - they swear)
steps.n: The بِ at the beginning means 'by' and shows what they are swearing by. The ٱ is just a different way of writing ال.

This is not actually the conditional إِنْ (if), but rather إِنْ meaning 'only/just' that comes before statements to emphasize their limitation. Here it connects to the verb أَرَدْنَآ (we intended) to say 'we only intended'
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Adding أَ at the start makes the verb transitive (affects something else)
Changed the middle vowel to sukun (ْ) to prepare for adding the 'we' ending
Added نَآ at the end to show 'we' are doing the action
This is the basic form of the exception particle that excludes what comes after it from what was mentioned before

steps.m: except/only (excluding what follows from يَحْلِفُونَ - they swear)
steps.n: Added the long alif (آ) at the end to emphasize and lengthen the sound. In this ayah, it excludes what follows from the previous statement about their swearing.
Basic vowels (fatha) added between letters to make past tense verb meaning 'he was good'
Changed from 'being good' (verb) to 'doing good things' (action noun)
Added tanween (double line marks) and alif at the end to show this word is the object being acted upon
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb

steps.m: act of making things fit together perfectly
steps.n: Changed to a noun form by adding تَ at start and lengthening middle vowel, showing the action itself rather than doing it

steps.m: and (an act of) making things fit together perfectly (object of يَحْلِفُونَ - they swear)
steps.n: Adds coordinating conjunction meaning 'and'
This is the base form of the demonstrative pronoun used to point to things or people
Adding ئِ changes this to specifically point to things that are far away

steps.m: those (pointing to people far away) (subject of يَعْلَمُ)
steps.n: Adding كَ at the end makes this word specifically point to people rather than things. This word is the subject of يَعْلَمُ (knows) in this ayah.
This is a relative pronoun that connects two parts of a sentence. It consists of the definite article ال + ذي (which/who) + plural suffix ين
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense form
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, sukun on middle letter, and ُ at end. This word describes what Allah knows about what's in their hearts.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity'
Changed to the proper name of God in Islam. The ال makes it definite (like 'the'), the shadda emphasizes the ل, and the ُ shows this is the subject of يَعْلَمُ (knows) in this ayah.
This is a relative pronoun meaning 'what' or 'that which' - here it connects to the word قُلُوبِهِمْ (their hearts) to mean 'what is in their hearts'
This is a preposition that shows location or position inside something. In this ayah, it shows what is inside their hearts.
The ي at the end is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.

steps.m: to turn/change (originally referring to turning something over, later came to mean heart as it turns emotions)

steps.n: Root meaning 'turn' became a simple noun meaning 'heart' - like how hearts turn our feelings
Changed the internal vowels and added و to make it plural
Added kasra to show this word is possessed by something
Added هِمْ to show these hearts belong to them. This word comes after فِى (in/inside) in the ayah.

steps.m: to turn away or avoid something

steps.n: Changes from basic root to a command meaning 'turn away!' by adding أَ at start and putting a sukoon at the end
Adds فَ to show this command comes as a result of what was mentioned before about Allah knowing what's in their hearts
This is the basic preposition meaning 'from' or 'away from'
Added هُمْ (them) to show who is being moved away from. This word connects to فَأَعْرِضْ (so turn away) in the ayah, showing who to turn away from.

steps.m: to advise or warn with wisdom and care

steps.n: Command form made by dropping the و and adding kasra to make عِظْ - like saying 'give advice!'
Added هُمْ to show who should receive the advice
Added وَ to connect this command with previous statements
Changed to a command form by dropping the middle و and adding ُ. This tells one person to do something.
Added و with fatha (وَ) at the beginning to connect this command with what came before it
Added fatha to show the basic vowel of the preposition ل
The letter ل gets doubled (shadda) because it comes after the word قُل, making the meaning stronger. Like saying 'definitely for them' instead of just 'for them'
Added هُمْ at the end to show who is being addressed - in this case 'them', referring to the people mentioned earlier in the ayah
Basic preposition showing location or containment inside something else. In this ayah, it shows the location of something inside their hearts.
Added a long vowel mark (ٓ) to show the extended sound at the end of the word when reading

steps.m: to breathe, to be precious, self

steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form the basic noun meaning 'self'
Changed to broken plural form by adding أَ at start and changing vowels
Added kasra to show this word is possessed by something
Added هِمْ to show the selves belong to them. This word follows the preposition فِي (in/within) in the ayah.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make it a noun. This word is what is being said (object) in relation to the command قُل (say!) in this ayah.
Adds -an ending to show this is 'a speech' and is the object being spoken
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
This form shows someone/something that has reached a high level in a quality - here, in the ability to reach others through speech

steps.m: penetrating speech (describes قَوْلًا - what kind of speech to say to them)
steps.n: The ًا ending shows this word is describing قَوْلًا (speech) - so it means 'tell them penetrating speech'
This is a negative particle that denies what comes after it
The و adds the meaning of 'and' to connect this with what came before
The آ elongates the final vowel sound for emphasis. This word negates the verb أَرْسَلْنَا (We sent) that comes after it.
Connected the root letters together with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Adding أ at the beginning makes the verb causative, showing someone made the action happen. The sukun (ْ) is added on س because of this pattern.
The نا at the end shows that 'we' did the action. This verb is connected to وَمَآ which means 'and not', so together they mean 'and we did not send'

steps.m: from/of/among (connects رَّسُولٍ to what comes before it)
steps.n: This is a preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects the word 'messenger' (رَّسُولٍ) to the previous phrase 'We did not send' (وَمَآ أَرْسَلْنَا), making the meaning 'We did not send any messenger'
The و makes it mean someone who does this action a lot
The ر has a shadda and the word ends with tanween kasra (-in sound) because it's describing 'any/which messenger' after مِن

This is an exception particle that excludes what comes after it from what came before. In this ayah, it's used with رَّسُولٍ (messenger) to say that Allah didn't send any messenger EXCEPT for a specific purpose.
Changed from root letters to past tense verb by adding the long 'aa' sound and vowels
Added يُ at the beginning to make it passive (someone/something is being obeyed) and changed the vowels inside the word

steps.m: so that he is obeyed (purpose of أَرْسَلْنَا)
steps.n: Added لِ at beginning to show purpose - connects to أَرْسَلْنَا (We sent) to explain why the messenger was sent
The root letters for 'allow' became the noun 'permission' by changing the pattern and adding vowel marks
Added بِ at the beginning to mean 'by' and ِ at the end because it's connected to اللَّهِ (of Allah) in the ayah
Changed to the proper name for God by adding ال and merging letters. The first ا changes to ل and combines with the second ل to make a stronger ل sound (shown by ّ)
Added ِ at the end to show possession or connection. The first ا is written as ٱ which shows how it's pronounced in this case.
This is a conditional particle that introduces hypothetical situations
Added و at the beginning to connect this condition with the previous statement
This is an emphatic particle that makes statements stronger and more certain
Added the attached pronoun هُمْ to show who the statement is about
This is a time word that connects two events in the past. Here it connects 'when they wronged themselves' to what happens next in the verse.

steps.m: to act unfairly or harm someone

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added وا to show many people did this action
Added shadda to show they did this strongly or repeatedly
وا ending shows this is 'they' (masculine plural)

This is a preposition showing something coming from somewhere or someone. Here it connects to لَمَن (indeed among) in the ayah.

This is a special word that points to unknown people or things. It's like saying 'whoever' or 'anyone who' in English.
The لَ at the start adds emphasis to show certainty. This word is the subject (doer) of the verb لَّيُبَطِّئَنَّ (surely they will hesitate) in this ayah.
Adding shadda makes the meaning causative - changing from being slow to making something slow
Adding يُ shows this is happening in the present/future and shows who is doing it
Adding لَ at start and نَّ at end adds strong emphasis, like saying 'surely/definitely'
Adding shadda to لَ is just for pronunciation, doesn't change meaning
This word starts a condition - if something happens. In this ayah, it specifically introduces a condition about a difficulty (مُّصِيبَةٌ) that might happen.

steps.m: and if/then if (connects to previous statement about لَّيُبَطِّئَنَّ/holding back)
steps.n: The فَ connects this condition to the previous statement about some people holding back (لَّيُبَطِّئَنَّ), showing what these people might say if something happens
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Adding أَ at the beginning makes the action affect something else directly
Adding كُمْ shows who was affected - you all (plural)
Adding تْ shows a feminine subject did the action (matching with مُّصِيبَةٌ 'calamity' which is feminine)

steps.m: it (feminine) afflicted you all (referring to مُّصِيبَةٌ 'calamity' afflicting the people)
steps.n: Adding كُم shows the action affects 'you all' (plural)

steps.m: something that strikes/afflicts (referring to a hardship in أَصٰبَتْكُم)
steps.n: The root ص-و-ب became مُصِيبَة by adding مُ at the start and changing the middle to make it mean 'a calamity/hardship that strikes'. It's used with أَصٰبَتْكُم (it struck you) in the ayah.
Added emphasis by doubling the م sound at the beginning to show the strong nature of the affliction
Added two dots at the end to make it 'a' disaster rather than 'the' disaster
Added vowels to make past tense form
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas. This is a common change in hollow verbs (verbs with و or ي in the middle)
This is a particle that emphasizes that something definitely happened in the past. In this ayah, it emphasizes that Allah definitely bestowed His favors.
Added vowels to make it 'he blessed/favored' in past tense
Added أَ at beginning and sukun on first root letter to show the action is done to someone else
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'
Added ال with special spelling (ٱ) and shadda (ّ) to show this is the one true deity. The ُ shows this is the subject of أَنْعَمَ (bestowed favor) in this ayah.
This is a basic preposition showing something is on top of or affecting something else
Added ىَّ at the end to show this affects 'me'
This word points to something that happened in the past. In this ayah, it connects to قَالَ (he said) to show when something was said.
This is a particle that makes present tense verbs negative and changes them to past tense meaning. Here it makes the verb أَكُن (I am) into 'I was not'.
Added basic vowels to connect the letters together
Changed from past tense 'was' to present tense 'be' by changing middle vowel from 'a' to 'u'
Added أَ at beginning to show 'I' am doing the action

steps.m: I am/be (in a conditional or negative context)
steps.n: The word is shortened by dropping the و when used after words like 'not' or 'if'
This shows being together with someone
Added هُمْ at the end to show who is being referred to (them)
Added emphasis mark (shadda) because this word comes after another word ending in a similar sound

steps.m: to witness or testify to something

steps.n: Added ي between second and third root letters to show someone who does this action as a regular quality or characteristic

steps.m: as a witness (with لَمْ أَكُن - I was not)
steps.n: The ًا ending makes this word 'a witness' into the object - showing who/what was being witnessed
This is the basic conditional particle that introduces hypothetical situations
Adding لَ at the beginning adds emphasis to the condition
Adding وَ connects this condition to what came before it

steps.m: to reach or hit something accurately

steps.n: Changed root letters into past tense verb by adding the vowel 'aa' between first two letters
Adding أَ at the beginning makes the verb affect others directly
Adding كُمْ shows who was affected - you all/plural
The ا changed to ٰ (alif to long a), but the meaning stays the same. كُمْ means 'you all' at the end
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to turn the action into a noun meaning the result of giving extra

steps.m: a favor, bounty (subject of أَصٰبَكُمْ - befalls you)
steps.n: Added tanween damma to show this is indefinite (like saying 'a' favor) and is doing the action of befalling/reaching you
Added a fatha at the end to help connect it smoothly with the next word (اللَّهِ)
Added emphasis by doubling the م with a shadda. This makes the meaning stronger, like saying 'directly from' or 'specifically from'
Changed first letter and added ل with shadda to make it specific to Allah, the One True God
Added kasra at the end to show possession, and changed alif to wasl-alif since it comes after another word. This word shows who the bounty (فَضْلٌ) belongs to.
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. The middle و affects pronunciation.
Added لَ for emphasis, making the statement stronger and more certain
Added نَّ at the end for extra emphasis, showing this will definitely happen
Added fatha to show how to pronounce the letter ك
Added أَ to strengthen the meaning of similarity

steps.m: as if (introducing a hypothetical situation in لَيَقُولَنَّ - they would surely say)
steps.n: Added ن to make it express hypothetical situations. In this ayah, it introduces what some people would say if they received favor from Allah.
This is a basic negative particle that makes verbs refer to the past and negates them, like saying 'did not' in English
Added emphasis to the ل with a shadda because it comes after another ل in the previous word (لَيَقُولَنَّ)
Root letters connected with vowels to make the basic noun form meaning 'being'
Added تَ at beginning to show 'you' (feminine) doing the action in present tense
The و is dropped because this word comes after لَمْ in the ayah, which requires this shortened form
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make it a preposition. The fatha at the end shows it's being used with another word.

steps.m: between you all (referring to a group being addressed)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show this applies to multiple people being spoken to. The ْ (sukun) at the end shows this is a complete word.

steps.m: to be separate or distinct from something

steps.n: Added vowel marks to make it a preposition showing space or relationship between things
Added هُ at the end to show this means 'between him'. This connects to the previous part of the ayah talking about there being no connection between you all.

steps.m: and between him (connected to بَيْنَكُمْ - between you)
steps.n: Added وَ at start to mean 'and'. This connects two phrases showing contrast between 'between you' and 'between him'.

steps.m: to feel love or wish for something

steps.n: Connected the root letters and added vowels to make it a past tense verb
Added مَ at beginning to turn the verb into a noun meaning the action itself
Added ة at the end to show one specific instance of the feeling
Added tanween dammah to make it indefinite (a love/affection rather than the love/affection)
This is the base word expressing a wish or desire
Added يٰ at the beginning to show calling out or exclamation
Added نِى at the end to show this is about myself wishing
This is the basic concept of existing or being
Changed from a verb root to a noun by adding damma and sukun marks, making it mean 'existence' or 'being'
The word changes from 'being' to a command 'be!' by dropping the و letter
Added تُ at the end to show 'I' did this in the past
This is the basic preposition meaning 'with'. It's one of the common prepositions in Arabic that doesn't come from a root.
Added the attached pronoun هُمْ which means 'them' (referring to a group of people)
Added vowels to connect the root letters
Added أَ at the beginning to make it mean 'I succeed' or 'that I succeed' - this أَ shows who is doing the action

steps.m: so that I may succeed (expressing hope after يٰلَيْتَنِى)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this is the hoped-for result after يٰلَيْتَنِى (Oh I wish) in the ayah
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to make it a noun

steps.m: a success/victory (object of فَأَفُوزَ - so that I may achieve)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the object being achieved. This connects to the verb فَأَفُوزَ (so that I may achieve) in the ayah.
Added ي and vowels to change from basic root meaning 'great' into an adjective meaning 'great/mighty', like changing 'big' to 'biggest'
Added ًا at the end to show this describes the victory (فَوْزًا) mentioned in the ayah
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show past tense meaning
Added alif to show mutual action - fighting involves two sides
Changed to present/future tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Adds 'so/then' to connect this action with what came before
This is a preposition that shows location or containment. Here it connects 'let them fight' to 'the path', meaning 'fight in the path'.
The ي at the end is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.

steps.m: to travel or follow a path

steps.n: Root letters س-ب-ل became the noun سَبِيل (path) by adding ي in the middle
Added kasra at the end because it's connected to 'Allah' - showing it's 'path of Allah'
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' by adding vowel marks
Added ال and merged letters to make it a proper noun referring to Allah. The shadda shows emphasis.
Added kasra at the end to show this belongs to or describes something else. In this ayah, it shows this is Allah's path (سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ means 'path of Allah').
Added ال with shadda to make it definite and stronger. This turns a basic relative word into a more specific pointer.

steps.m: those who (subject of يَشْرُونَ - they purchase)
steps.n: Added نَ to make it plural. This shows we're talking about multiple people who are doing the action in يَشْرُونَ (they purchase).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Added يَ at start to show present tense, with sukun on ش and kasra on ر

steps.m: they sell/trade (referring to those who sell/trade their worldly life for the hereafter)
steps.n: Added ون to show plural 'they', which causes the final ي to drop and changes kasra to damma before the ون
Root letters connected with vowels to make 'to live' into 'living', last letter changed to alif
Added و and ة to form the abstract noun meaning 'life'
Added the definite article ٱلْ to specify this particular life (referring to worldly life in this context)

steps.m: the life (object of يَشْرُونَ - they sell)
steps.n: Added the fatha ending because this word is the object of the verb يَشْرُونَ (they sell) in this ayah
Added damma and sukun to make it into a noun meaning the concept of being close
Added feminine ending and changed و to ي to make it an adjective describing something close or lower
Added ال to make it definite. The د gets a shadda because it's a sun letter. In this ayah, it's describing 'life' (الْحَيَوٰةَ), meaning the worldly or lower life as opposed to the afterlife.
Changes the root into a word meaning 'the one who/that which is last'
Adding ة creates a noun specifically referring to the afterlife/hereafter
Adding ال makes it refer to the specific hereafter rather than any afterlife
Adds بِ to show this is what they are trading for (contrasting with الحياة الدنيا - worldly life - mentioned earlier in the verse)
Adds a kasra (small i sound) at the end because it comes after bi (with/for)
Added fatha to clarify pronunciation. This word is a conditional pronoun meaning 'whoever' and is the subject of the verb يُقٰتِلْ (fights) that follows it.
Added و at the beginning to connect this clause with what came before it. This connects the condition 'whoever fights' to the previous command.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Added alif after first letter to show mutual action - fighting against each other

steps.m: let him fight (in سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ - the cause of Allah)
steps.n: Changed from past tense 'he fought' to a command 'let him fight' by adding يُ at start and changing the ending
This is a preposition that shows location or containment. Here it connects the word سَبِيلِ (path/way) with the verb يُقٰتِلْ (fight), showing where the fighting happens.
Added ي between second and third letters to make the word into a noun
Added kasra at the end to show this is Allah's path. This word is followed by اللَّهِ (Allah) in the ayah to show whose path it is.
Added shadda to show that the ل is doubled for emphasis
Changed to show it belongs to something else (like saying 'Allah's' instead of 'Allah'). The ِ sound at the end shows this ownership
Changed vowels to show this happened to someone instead of someone doing it
Added يُ at start to show it happens in present/future. Changed last vowel to sukun because it's in a special grammar position.

steps.m: and then he is killed (connected to يُقٰتِلْ - fights)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this happens after fighting (يُقٰتِلْ) mentioned earlier in the verse

This is a conjunction particle that gives a choice between two options. In this ayah, it connects 'then he is martyred' with 'or he becomes victorious', showing two possible outcomes for the one who fights in Allah's cause.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, changing vowels, and adding ُ at end
Changed the ending to sukun (ْ) because this verb is in a conditional phrase starting with مَن (whoever)
This is a basic particle that indicates something will happen in the future. It's stronger and indicates more distant future than just س-.
Added فَ at the beginning to show this happens after the fighting mentioned before. Added vowel marks to show exact pronunciation.
Changed to passive form by adding damma (ُ) at start and kasra (ِ) in middle to show something is being given/received
Added نُ prefix to show 'we' are doing the action, changed from passive to active voice

steps.m: we will give him (object of فَسَوْفَ)
steps.n: Added هِ suffix meaning 'him' as direct object. This word follows فَسَوْفَ (then soon) in the ayah, showing this is a future action.
Added vowel marks to make the root letters into a noun meaning the reward itself
Added ًا at the end to show this is the thing being given in the phrase 'We will give him a great reward'
Added ي between second and third root letters and vowels to create an adjective showing a strong quality
Added ًا at the end to show this describes the reward (أَجْرًا) mentioned in the ayah

steps.m: what (asking about لَكُمْ لَا تُقٰتِلُونَ - why don't you fight)
steps.n: Basic interrogative particle used here to ask 'what' or 'why'. In this ayah, it's questioning why the believers aren't fighting.

steps.m: and what (asking about لَكُمْ لَا تُقٰتِلُونَ - why don't you fight)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this question with what came before it
Added fatha to show the basic vowel sound of the preposition

steps.m: for you all (subject of لَا تُقٰتِلُونَ)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show this is directed at multiple people. This word connects to لَا تُقٰتِلُونَ (you do not fight) in this ayah, asking why they aren't fighting.
This is a negative particle that makes the verb تُقٰتِلُونَ (you fight) into 'you do not fight'. It's like adding 'not' in English to make something negative.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form basic past tense verb
Added alif after first letter to show mutual action between two parties
Changed قَ to تُقَ to show 'you' and changed ending to show ongoing action (present tense)
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action
Basic preposition showing location or being within something. Here it connects the word سَبِيلِ (path/way) with تُقٰتِلُونَ (you fight), showing where the fighting happens - in the path/way.
Connected the root letters and added vowels to form the basic noun meaning 'path'
Added kasra at the end to show this is Allah's path, connecting it to the word اللَّهِ that follows it in the ayah
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding vowel patterns
Changed from 'a deity' to 'The God' by adding ال and merging letters. The shadda shows emphasis. This is a unique proper name for the One True God in Islam.
Added kasra at the end to show this connects to the word سَبِيلِ (path), making it mean 'path of Allah'
Added vowels to make it a past tense verb showing someone becoming weak

steps.m: one who is made weak by others
steps.n: The prefix مست changes the verb into a noun meaning 'someone who is forced into weakness by others'
Adding 'ين' makes it plural for more than two people
Adding definite article to specify 'the weakened ones'

steps.m: and the weakened ones (object of تُقٰتِلُونَ)
steps.n: Added 'and' at beginning and case ending to show this is the object of 'you all fight' (تُقٰتِلُونَ)
This is a basic preposition that shows something coming from somewhere or someone
Added fatha at the end to connect smoothly to the next word الرِّجَالِ (the men). This is like adding a connecting sound to make the words flow better when speaking.

steps.m: to be a man, walk like a man

steps.n: Root letters get vowels to make the word for 'man'
Changes to broken plural form to mean multiple men
Adding ال makes it definite and causes shadda on ر
Adding kasra shows this word comes after مِنَ (from/of) in the ayah
Root letters n-s-w become the plural word 'women' by adding special vowels - like turning 'woman' into 'women' in English
Adding definite article makes the noun definite. The ن gets a shadda (double letter mark) after ال
Adding conjunction 'and' to connect with previous phrase (الرِّجَالِ - the men). The final ِ shows this word follows مِنَ (from) in the sentence.
Root letters stay same but vowels change to make it mean 'one child' instead of 'having children'
Adding ان makes it mean 'multiple children' instead of just one child
Added ال to make it specific
Added و at beginning to mean 'and'
Added two dots under the last letter to show these children are receiving the action
Starting with the basic demonstrative word and changing it to make a relative pronoun
Added ن at the end to make it refer to multiple people/things (plural)
Added ال to make it definite. This word introduces people who are saying something (يَقُولُونَ means 'they say')
Added proper pronunciation marks and changed alif to wasl form
Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense. The middle و changes to ا in this form.
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. The middle letter returns to و.
Added ونَ to show multiple people (they) are doing the action
Adding fatha and shadda creates an intensive noun meaning 'Lord' - someone who provides complete care and nurturing

steps.m: our Lord (the one calling out to Allah)
steps.n: Adding نا shows this is 'our Lord'. This word appears in a prayer where people are calling out to Allah for help.
The extra alif shows this is someone calling out 'O our Lord!' in a prayer
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Adding أ at the start changes it from 'exit' to 'make someone exit'
Adding نا changes it from 'he expelled' to 'we expelled'
This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or starts. In this ayah, it's used with أَخْرِجْنَا (take us out) to show what the people want to be taken out from.
This is the basic demonstrative word used to point to something near
Added alif above ه to help with pronunciation
Added kasra to help connect to the next part
Added ه with kasra at the end to make it feminine, showing we're pointing to a feminine noun

steps.m: to gather, to settle in a place

steps.n: Changes from root meaning 'to gather/settle' into noun meaning 'a settlement'
Makes the word specific by adding 'the'
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific village
Adding kasra shows this village belongs to or is connected to something (in this case, it belongs to هٰذِهِ meaning 'this')
Added vowels to make a past tense verb
Changed to active participle form showing who does the action
Added definite article and doubled the first letter
Added kasra at the end to show this describes the town (القرية) mentioned in the verse

steps.m: to settle, inhabit, or be populated

steps.n: Added vowel marks to the root letters to make the word mean 'people/family' instead of the verb 'to settle'
Added damma to prepare for the suffix

steps.m: its/her people (referring to الْقَرْيَةِ - the town)
steps.n: Added ها which refers back to 'the town' (القرية) mentioned in the ayah

steps.m: to make or set something in a certain way

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to show this happened in the past
Changed from 'he made' to 'make!' by adding alif at start to make it a command
Added و at beginning to mean 'and', and changed spelling of alif to match Quran writing style
Added fatha to show the basic vowel of the preposition ل
Added shadda to emphasize the ل, which happens when it follows certain words
Added نا which means 'us' to show who the action is for
This is a basic preposition showing where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it's used with أَخْرِجْنَا (take us out) to show what the people want to leave from.
Added the kasra (ِ) mark under م to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'min'

steps.m: near, close by, in the presence of
from near, from the presence of (with emphasis)
steps.n: Added emphasis by doubling the first letter
Added 'your' by attaching the second person masculine singular pronoun كَ

steps.m: to protect, guard, be close to

steps.n: Adding vowels turns the root into a past tense verb meaning 'to protect/be close to'
Changed from a past tense verb (he protected) to a noun meaning protector/guardian
Added tanween fatHa to show this is the object of the verb اجْعَل (make/appoint) in the ayah
Added vowels to connect the root letters together and show this happened in the past
Changed from 'he made' to 'make!' (command form) by adding alif at start

steps.m: and make!/put! (command to Allah in a prayer)

Combined two parts: ل meaning 'for/to' and نا meaning 'us'. This shows who will receive the وَلِيًّا (protector) that is being asked for.

This is a basic preposition that shows something's starting point or origin. In this ayah, it connects to لَّدُنكَ (from Your presence) to show where the helper and supporter should come from.
Becomes a noun meaning presence or nearness
Final form as a preposition meaning 'from the presence/side of'

Added tanween alif (ًا) to show this is the object being appointed as a helper
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun used to connect descriptions to people or things
Added ال at the beginning and doubled the ذ with shadda to make it specific
Added ين at the end to make it refer to multiple people. This word is describing the people who are the subject of يُقٰتِلُونَ (they fight)
Added alif after first letter to strengthen meaning from just trusting to having complete faith
Added وا at the end to show multiple people did the action
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
The alif shows two people are fighting each other, not just one person attacking
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Added plural ending ون to show multiple people fighting and changed alif to alif with line above for Quranic spelling
Basic particle that shows something is inside, within, or part of something else

steps.m: in (connects to سَبِيلِ meaning 'in the path/way')
steps.n: The ى at the end helps connect this word to what comes after it. In this ayah, it connects to 'سَبِيلِ' to mean 'in the path/way'
Adding ي and vowels changes it from a verb into a noun meaning 'path'
The kasra at the end shows this word belongs to or is connected to Allah in this ayah
Added shadda to emphasize the ل, showing this is a special name
Added kasra at the end to show this belongs to or is connected to something (in this case, سَبِيلِ meaning 'path')
The basic word ذا (this) becomes ذِي (who/which) to create a relative pronoun
Adding ال makes it more specific, like saying 'the one who' instead of just 'who'
Adding ين makes it refer to many people instead of just one person
Adding و at the beginning connects this word to what came before it in the verse

Added وا with damma before it to show multiple people did this action. This refers to the disbelievers who fight in the way of false deities (الطَّاغُوتِ) mentioned later in the ayah.
Added ۟ (small circle) above وا to show it's a plural marker for 'they'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create basic past tense verb
Adding alif shows mutual action - fighting against each other
Adding يُ at start and changing vowels shows ongoing action
Adding ون shows multiple people doing the action. In this ayah, it refers to 'those who believe' (ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟)
This is a basic preposition showing location or containment. In this ayah, it shows that the fighting happens in/along Allah's path.
The ي at the end is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Connected the root letters and added vowels to make a noun meaning 'path'
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to what comes after it (Allah)
Added basic vowels to connect the letters and make a past tense verb
Changed the verb into a noun describing someone who does this action
Added وت to create a special noun form showing something that embodies transgression completely
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the ط with shadda because it comes after ال

steps.m: of the false deity (possessed by سَبِيلِ)

Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
Added alif after first letter to show intensive or reciprocal action
Changed from past tense 'he fought' to a command 'fight!' by changing the middle vowel and ending
Added plural marker to make command for group
Added فَ to show this is a response or result

steps.m: to protect, be close to, or support
