Changed from singular 'protector' to plural 'protectors'. This word shows who is being fought against in the command 'so fight against'
Added fatha at end to show this word is being acted upon in the sentence
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Adding ي turns this into a special type of verb meaning 'to act like/become something'
Adding ا after second root letter turns a verb about acting evil into a noun meaning someone who is evil
Adding ال makes it refer to the specific devil. The ش gets a shadda because it comes after ال
Adding kasra at the end shows this word is possessed by another word (كَيْدَ/plot) in the phrase 'plot of the devil'
This is the basic form of the particle that can mean 'if' in conditional sentences
Adding shadda changes this from a conditional particle to an emphasis particle. In this ayah, it emphasizes that Satan's plot is truly weak.
Changed from action to the name of the action. This word is what إِنَّ is talking about in this ayah.
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon by إِنَّ
Root letters got vowels to make a noun meaning 'one who rebels/is distant' - like changing 'rebel' to 'rebel-er'
Adding ا makes it stronger - like changing 'rebel' to 'great rebel'
Added 'the' (ال) at the start, which makes the ش double (shown by the shadda)

steps.m: of the extremely rebellious one (of Satan)
steps.n: Changed to show 'of Satan' by adding the -i sound at the end and stretching the middle a sound

steps.m: he was (describes كَيْدَ which was weak)
steps.n: Added fathas to make it past tense. This word tells us about كَيْدَ (the plot/plan) in this ayah, saying it was weak.
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a hollow verb (has a weak middle letter)
Added ي to show this describes a quality someone has
The ًا ending shows this word is describing Satan's plan (كَيْدَ الشَّيْطَانِ) in the sentence
Adding مْ changes the negation from present to past tense in a simple way that a beginner can understand

steps.m: did not? (expecting 'yes, it did' as answer)
steps.n: Adding أَ at the start turns this into a question that expects 'yes' as an answer. In this ayah, it's asking 'Have you not seen?' (أَلَمْ تَرَ)
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show past tense
Added تَ at beginning to make it 'you' and changed the ending to make it present tense 'see' instead of past tense 'saw'
The final ى is dropped because this word comes after أَلَمْ which requires the final letter to be dropped
This is a basic preposition that shows direction or destination. In this ayah, it connects the verb تَرَ (have you not seen) with ٱلَّذِينَ (those who), showing who is being looked at.
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that connects descriptions to people or things
Added ٱلّ at the beginning to make it more specific, like adding 'the' in English
Added نَ at the end to show we're talking about multiple people. This word is the subject of قِيلَ (it was said) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters and added fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed the vowels to show this happened to someone/something rather than someone doing it
Adding ي to complete passive voice pattern
Added fatha to the letter lam to show it's a preposition meaning 'to' or 'for'
Added هُ (him) to show who the action is for

steps.m: to/for them (referring to those who were told)
steps.n: Added م with sukun to change 'him' to 'them', showing it refers to multiple people
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
Changed to command form by adding shadda and 'u' vowel - like telling someone 'Stop!'
Added وا to make the command apply to a group instead of one person
Connected the root letters to form the basic singular noun
Added alif at start to make it plural
Added final vowel to complete the plural form
Adds possessive pronoun suffix for 'your (plural masculine)'
Connected the root letters with vowels to make basic past tense verb
Added hamza at beginning to show causing action
Changed to present tense with damma ending
Added وا at the end to change from 'I establish' to 'you all establish!' - makes it a command for many people

steps.m: and you all establish! (command to establish الصَّلَوٰةَ)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect with previous phrase. This word is commanding the establishment of الصَّلَوٰةَ (the prayer).
Basic vowel marks (fatha) added above letters to make them readable and show movement/action
Added ة to make it a feminine noun referring to one instance of prayer
Added ٱل to make it definite and doubled the ص with shadda
Added fatha at end to show it's the object of the command 'establish' (أَقِيمُوا۟), and changed to long alif
Changed to Form IV pattern which shifts meaning from 'come' to 'give'
Adds damma (ُ) to show this is in the nominative case
Added 'وا' at the end to make it a command for a group of people
Added conjunction 'and' at beginning. This word is commanding people to give zakat (charity).

steps.m: to purify and grow something by giving from it

steps.n: Changed from root letters to past tense verb by adding vowels
Changed to a noun meaning the actual payment given for purification
Changed alif to dagger alif above واو for Quranic spelling
Added definite article and strengthened the ز with shadda

steps.m: the obligatory charity payment (object of وَءَاتُوا۟)
steps.n: Added fatha to show this is the object of the command 'give' (وَءَاتُوا۟)
This is a word that shows when something happens. The shadda (ّ) makes the م stronger, and the ا at the end makes it specifically mean 'when'.

steps.m: then when (connects to كُتِبَ - was prescribed)
steps.n: Adding فَ at the beginning shows this happens right after something else. Here it connects to what happens when fighting was prescribed for them.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed vowels to show this happened to someone rather than someone doing it. This word connects to عَلَيْهِمُ (upon them) in the ayah to show what was prescribed upon them.
This is the basic preposition meaning 'on' or 'upon'
The ى changes to يْ when pronouns are attached. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning.

steps.m: upon them (subject of كُتِبَ - was prescribed)
steps.n: Added هِمُ which means 'them'. This word connects to كُتِبَ (was prescribed) to show who something was prescribed upon.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changed from a verb (he fought) to a noun meaning the general activity of fighting - like changing 'fought' to 'fighting'
Added the definite article 'the' to specify this particular fighting
Added damma to show this word is the subject of كُتِبَ (was prescribed) in this ayah

steps.m: when (introduces what happens next in فَرِيقٌ)
steps.n: This is a time word that shows when something happens. In this ayah, it connects to the next part that tells us what happens - a group (فَرِيقٌ) becomes afraid.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and create the basic meaning

steps.m: a group (that is separated from others)
steps.n: Added ي between second and third root letters to show this is a distinct group
Added tanween damma to show this is the subject doing the action of fearing (يَخْشَوْنَ) in this ayah
Basic preposition showing where something starts or comes from
Added emphasis by doubling the م sound

steps.m: from them (referring to فَرِيقٌ - a group)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show this applies to 'them' (referring back to the group mentioned as فَرِيقٌ in the ayah)
Connected the root letters together
Added يَ at the beginning to show present tense (he/it), and changed the ending due to the weak letter ي

steps.m: they fear (referring to a group mentioned earlier who fear people as they fear Allah or more)
steps.n: Added ون (as وْنَ) at the end to show multiple people doing the action. The ى changes to و before this ending.
Forms the noun 'people'
Added vowels to connect the root letters
Changed from a verb about closeness into a plural noun meaning people/mankind
Added 'ال' (the) at the start to make it specific, which caused the ن to be doubled when pronounced

steps.m: the people (who are being feared by يَخْشَوْنَ)
steps.n: Added a mark to show these are the people being feared in the phrase 'they fear the people'
Changes the basic idea of fearing into a noun meaning the feeling of fear itself
Adds كَ at the beginning to show this fear is being compared to something else
Adds ِ at the end to show this word connects to 'Allah' coming after it, like saying 'fear of Allah'
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding specific vowel patterns

This is the unique name for the one true God in Islam. The ال makes it definite, and the shadda emphasizes the uniqueness. The kasra at the end shows it's being acted upon by the word كَخَشْيَةِ (like the fear of).
This is a basic conjunction that connects two options. In this ayah, it connects two levels of fear - fearing people like they fear Allah, or even stronger fear.
This root carries the basic meaning of strength or intensity
Changed from root letters to a past tense verb by adding vowels and marking the doubled د with shadda

steps.m: more/most intense (than خَشْيَةِ ٱللَّهِ - fear of Allah)
steps.n: Added أَ at the beginning to make it comparative, showing their fear of people is even stronger than their fear of Allah

steps.m: to fear or be afraid of something

steps.n: Root letters خ-ش-ي were connected with vowels (َ and ْ) to make the active participle meaning 'fearing'
Added ة to show this is one specific instance of fear

steps.m: fear (as an object being described in the ayah)
steps.n: Added tanween to show this word is being used as an object in the sentence, specifically describing how they fear people like they fear Allah or even more

steps.m: to speak, tell, or say something

steps.n: The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a hollow verb (has و in middle). This is a common pattern in Arabic.
Adding وا at the end changes 'he said' to 'they said', showing that a group of people (3 or more) did the action

steps.m: and they said (to express their objection)
steps.n: Added و with fatha at beginning to connect this with the previous statement. In this ayah, it shows their objection to what came before.

steps.m: to nurture and care for completely
Lord (one who nurtures and cares completely)
steps.n: The doubled middle letter (shadda) makes this a noun showing who does the action intensively

steps.m: Our Lord (the one who nurtures and cares for us completely)
steps.n: Added نَا at the end to show this is 'our Lord' - the one who nurtures and cares for us
This word combines two parts: لِ (for/to) and مَ (what). Together they ask 'for what?' meaning 'why?'. In this ayah, it's asking why Allah prescribed fighting for them.
Adding fatha vowels creates the basic past tense verb form
Changed the final vowel to sukun to prepare for the suffix
Added تَ at the end to show 'you' did the action
This is the basic preposition meaning 'on' or 'upon'
The ى changes to يْ when connecting to pronouns
Added نا at the end to show this applies to 'us'
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changed the word to show the abstract concept of fighting rather than a specific instance of fighting. This word is what was prescribed (كُتِبَ) upon them.
Added 'the' (ال) to make it specific, and fatḥa at the end because it's what was prescribed (the thing being written/ordered)
Combined two particles: لَوْ (if) and لَا (not) to create a new meaning. When these join together, they ask 'why didn't something happen?' instead of just saying 'if not'
Added the long alif (آ) at the end to show emphasis in questions. This is common when asking important questions, especially when speaking to Allah.

steps.m: to delay or put something later

steps.n: The doubled خ shows this is a strong or deliberate delay
Adding تَ changes 'he' to 'you'

steps.m: you delayed us (object of قَالُوا۟/they said)
steps.n: Adding نَآ shows who was delayed (us). The long alif at the end shows this is at the end of a phrase.
This is the basic preposition showing direction or destination
Added vowel marks to show exact pronunciation: kasra under إ and fatha over ل
Added small alif (ٰ) above the ى to show it's pronounced as a long 'aa' sound
Added a small vertical line (ٓ) to show this word connects to the next word أَجَلٍ (appointed term)
Added fatha marks to connect the letters and form the noun
Added tanween kasra to show this word is connected to إِلَىٰٓ (to/until) in the ayah
Root 'to come near' becomes an adjective 'near' with double kasra (-in) at the end to mean 'a near' instead of 'the near'
This is the basic concept of speaking or saying something
The weak letter و drops out and is replaced with a sukun because it's between a damma and sukun
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past-tense verb
Added alif to make it a noun meaning the thing itself. This word is the subject being described as قَلِيلٌ (little) in the phrase متاع الدنيا قليل (the enjoyment of this world is little).
The damma shows this is the subject of the sentence. The alif is written as a long alif (ٰ) in this Quranic spelling.
Forms the feminine adjective meaning 'lower' or 'nearer' which became used to refer to this worldly life as it is closer to us than the afterlife

Adding ال makes it specific and adding shadda emphasizes the meaning. In this ayah, it refers to the temporary life of this world in contrast to the eternal afterlife mentioned right after it (وَٱلْءَاخِرَةُ)
Doubling the د to emphasize the meaning of worldliness

steps.m: little/small (describing متٰعُ ٱلدُّنْيَا - the enjoyment of this world)
steps.n: Changed the root into an adjective that describes how small or brief something is. In this ayah, it's describing how the enjoyments of this world are small/brief.
Root letters changed to make 'last/final' - hamza with alif at start shows it's a noun not a verb
Adding ة makes this word specifically refer to the afterlife - the final destination that comes after this life
ال makes it refer to THE afterlife specifically. The ل gets a sukun (ْ) mark to connect it to the next letter
Adds و (and) at start to connect it with previous word. The ُ at the end shows this word is the subject being described as خَيْرٌ (better) in this ayah
Changes from verb 'to be good' into the noun 'good/goodness'
Adding double dammah makes it 'a good thing' instead of just 'good'
This is a relative pronoun that doesn't come from a root. It's used to refer to people in a general way.
Added لِ at beginning to show purpose or direction
Added shadda on ل for emphasis and kasra at end to show connection with following verb اتَّقَىٰ (fears/is mindful)

steps.m: to protect oneself or guard against harm

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters into a past tense verb
Changed initial و to ا and added ت to show doing action to oneself
The ت and ق combine to make a doubled letter with shadda
This is a negative particle that negates the verb تُظْلَمُونَ (you will be wronged) that comes after it
Added وَ (and) at beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to show someone receiving the unfair treatment instead of doing it
Added ونَ suffix to make plural masculine form, changing meaning to 'you all'

steps.m: to twist or spin something into a thread

steps.n: Connected the letters together and added vowels to make it a past-tense verb
Changes from a verb (twisting) to a noun (the twisted thing)

steps.m: even a thread's worth (as the object being denied)
steps.n: Adds tanween alif to show this is the object being denied in the phrase 'you will not be wronged even a thread's worth'
This is a question word meaning 'where'. It's used to ask about location.
Adding ما changes 'where' to 'wherever', making it apply to any location. This word connects to تَكُونُوا (you all are) in the ayah.
Added basic vowels to connect the letters. The و is a weak letter between vowels.
Adding ت at start makes it 'you' form of verb
Adds plural masculine subject marker to make verb apply to multiple people

steps.m: to reach or catch up to

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added يُ at start and changed vowels to show present tense
Changed final vowel to sukun to prepare for suffix
Added كُّمُ to show the action affects multiple people being spoken to
Added vowel marks to create the noun form. The و with sukun (وْ) makes a long 'o' sound.
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific death rather than death in general
The ُ ending shows this word is doing the action in يُدْرِككُّمُ (reaches you)
Basic conditional particle that introduces hypothetical situations
Added و at the beginning to connect this condition with what came before it
Added damma (ُ) on first letter and sukun (ْ) on last letter to make basic command form. The و drops because it's a hollow verb.
Added تُ at the end to show 'I' did this in the past (not 'you')
Changed from 'I was' to 'you all were' by adding م at the end to show it's talking to multiple people
Basic preposition showing location or containment. In this ayah, it shows the towers/fortresses that people might be inside.
The ي at the end is written as ى when it's at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.

steps.m: to stand out or rise up high

steps.n: Changed the verb into a noun for a tall structure
Added و between second and third letters to make it plural
Added tanwin kasra at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the preposition في (in) that comes before it
Added vowels and doubled middle letter to show intense building action
Added مُ at the beginning to change it from 'built' to 'being built' - like changing from active to passive
Added feminine marker ة to match with بُرُوج (towers) which is feminine
Added genitive case ending ٍ because it follows في (in) and doubled the م because it follows a word ending in a vowel
The word stays the same except for removing the shadda (doubling mark) from the first letter م - both mean 'fortified/strongly built'
This is a basic conditional particle that introduces a condition. In this ayah, it introduces what happens if something bad befalls them.
Added و at the beginning to connect this condition with the previous statement in the ayah
Added an alif between first two root letters and vowels to make past tense 'he struck'
Changed to present tense feminine form by adding ت at start and changing vowels
Changed to 'if/when' form by dropping the vowel at the end and making the last letter silent
Added هُمْ at the end to show who is being struck. This word comes after إِن (if) in the ayah, making it conditional.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form the basic adjective
Adding ة changes this from a general adjective to a specific feminine noun meaning 'a good thing/deed'
Adding tanween dammah shows this is the subject (doer) of the verb تُصِبْهُمْ (befalls them) in this ayah
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changes past tense to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Changes singular 'he' to plural 'they' by adding وا at the end
Changed the basic demonstrative pronoun to its feminine form by adding هِ
Added هٰ at the beginning to make it clearer and easier to pronounce
Added a small ي (ۦ) at the end as a pronunciation helper
A basic preposition showing where something comes from or starts. In this ayah, it connects 'this' (هٰذِهِۦ) to 'from' (عِندِ), showing where something originates.
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to make it into a noun meaning 'presence' or 'nearness'
The ِ at the end shows this word is connected to اللَّهِ (Allah), showing something is from Allah's presence
Changed first letter to ل and added shadda to make it the proper name for The One God
Added kasra at the end to show possession, and changed alif to wasl-alif since it comes after another word

steps.m: if (introduces condition for يَقُولُوا - they say)
steps.n: This is a conditional particle that introduces a situation - if something happens. Here it introduces what these people say when something happens to them.
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this condition with the previous statement, showing this is another situation being described
Changed the root letters ص-و-ب into a past tense verb by putting a long 'aa' sound in the middle
Added ت at start and changed vowels to make it present tense
Changed the end of the word to show it's part of a condition (if/when something happens)

steps.m: it strikes them (in the condition إِن if)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show who is being struck. This word comes after إِن (if) in the ayah, showing it's part of a conditional statement.
The و changes to ي and gets doubled with shadda to show intensity of the quality
Adding ة changes this from a description (bad) to a thing (a bad thing)
The ٌ shows this word is doing the action in تُصِبْهُمْ (befalls them)
This is the basic meaning showing the action of speaking or saying something
Connected the letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Added يَ at start to make it 'he says' (present tense), and middle vowels changed from قَوَ to قُو because it's a special verb with و in middle
Added وا۟ at the end to show multiple people are doing the action. This word is what 'they' do if something happens to them in this ayah.
This is the basic demonstrative word used to point to things. The alif above ه (هٰ) helps extend the sound.
Adding ه with kasra (ِ) makes it refer to feminine things. This word is what they say in the verse.
The small ۦ mark helps with pronunciation when connecting to the next word (مِنْ) but doesn't change the meaning
This is a preposition showing where something comes from. In this ayah, it connects 'this' (هٰذِهِۦ) to 'presence' (عِندِ) to show where something originated.
Added the vowels عِ (i) and دِ (i) to connect the letters. The last vowel (i) shows this word follows مِنْ (from).
Adding كَ (ka) at the end means 'your' - so it changes from just 'near/beside' to 'near you'. This happens because مِنْ (from) comes before it.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense form

Changed to a command form by: 1) dropping the middle weak letter و, 2) adding damma on first letter, 3) adding sukun on last letter. This is Allah commanding Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to speak in response to what the hypocrites were saying.
This is a quantifier showing totality or completeness
The shadda (double letter) makes the meaning stronger and more complete
Added double damma (ٌ) at the end to show this word is the subject doing the action in the sentence
This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or what it's made of, like saying 'from the store' or 'made of wood' in English
Added the kasra (ِ) mark under the م to show how to pronounce it correctly
Added shadda (ّ) on the م to show it should be pronounced strongly because it comes after another م in the ayah
Added sukun (ْ) on the ن to show where to stop when pronouncing the word
This is a special word (semi-preposition) that shows where something is or who has something.
Added kasra under ع to connect the letters together. This word shows location or possession, like saying 'by me' or 'at the house'.
Added kasra at the end to show this connects to the next word (اللَّهِ - Allah). It's like saying 'from near Allah' in English.
Changed إِلَه to اللَه to make it a proper name for the One God. The ال here is part of the name itself, not just 'the'.
Added ِ at the end to show this is the object of مِنْ عِندِ (from). Also added shadda on ل and changed initial alif to wasl form since it comes after another word.
This is a question word that comes from the idea of something being turned or inclined away from normal. The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two vowels.

steps.m: what is wrong with (connected to هٰٓؤُلَآءِ - these)
steps.n: The ِ at the end shows this word connects to the word after it (these people)
The فَ at the start shows this follows from the previous statement - they say bad things come from you (O Muhammad), so what is wrong with these people?
This is the basic demonstrative element meaning 'this' or 'here'
Added the plural marker ؤُ to show we're talking about multiple things
Added لَآ to emphasize and strengthen the meaning

steps.m: these ones/these people (subject of لَا يَكَادُونَ)
steps.n: Added ءِ at the end to complete the demonstrative pronoun. This word points to 'the people' being discussed who 'can hardly understand' (لَا يَكَادُونَ)

steps.m: to stand, rise, get up, establish

steps.n: Adding fatha and sukun changes the verb 'to stand' into the noun 'people' - think of how people 'stand together' as a group
The kasra (ِ) at the end shows this word is being acted upon by something else or belongs to something else
Adding ال makes it specific which people we're talking about. This word is connected to هٰٓؤُلَآءِ (these) in the sentence to mean 'these people'
This is a negative particle used to negate the verb يَفْقَهُونَ (they understand) that comes after it, changing its meaning to 'they do not understand'
Added fatha mark (َ) to show the correct pronunciation as 'laa'
Added fathas to make the basic past tense form
Added يَ at start to show present tense and changed last vowel to ُ
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. This word connects to يَفْقَهُونَ (they understand) in the next ayah.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show past tense meaning
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action
Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb
Changed from a verb (he spoke) to a noun (speech) by changing the middle vowel and adding a long 'ee' sound
Added tanween alif to show this word is the object being acted upon in the sentence
Basic question word that can also connect parts of sentences, like 'what' in English

Adding the shadda (ّ) and long alif (آ) makes this word more general, changing it from just 'what' to 'whatever'. This word connects to أَصَابَكَ (befalls you) in this ayah to talk about things that might happen to someone.
Added basic vowels to connect the letters. The middle root letter و changes to ا which is common in this type of word.
Added أَ at the beginning to show something happening to someone or something

steps.m: it befell you (as something that happened to you in مَّآ أَصَابَكَ مِن سَيِّئَةٍ - whatever bad befalls you)
steps.n: Adds كَ meaning 'you' to show who was affected by the action. This word is part of a larger phrase talking about good and bad things that happen to someone.

Basic preposition that shows where something comes from or what it's made of. In this ayah, it connects the word سَيِّئَةٍ (bad deed) to نَّفْسِكَ (yourself), showing the bad deed comes from yourself.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make it a past tense verb
Adding ة makes it a feminine noun meaning 'a good thing'
Adding tanween kasra shows this word comes after مِنْ (from/of) in the ayah
Basic preposition meaning 'from'. The fatha at the end helps connect it to the next word (اللَّهِ).
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from what came before. It connects this phrase to the previous statement about good things.
Connected the letters and added vowels to make the basic word for 'deity'
Added ال to make it specific/definite - 'the god' instead of just 'a god'

steps.m: of the deity, of the god
steps.n: Added kasra at end to show this word is being described by another word
Basic form of the word that asks about or refers to things in a general way, like saying 'what' or 'whatever' in English
Adds و at the beginning to connect this part to what came before it in the verse
The alif is written longer (آ instead of ا) to show proper pronunciation when stopping at this word
Added hamza with fatha at beginning to show something causing an effect. This word relates to حَسَنَةٍ (good) in the ayah, showing what happens to someone.
Added كَ at the end to show the action is happening to 'you' (masculine singular)
This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from. In this ayah, it connects the word سَيِّئَةٍ (evil/bad deed) to نَّفْسِكَ (yourself), showing the bad deed comes from yourself.
Root meaning 'bad' turned into noun meaning 'badness'
The و changes to ي and gets doubled to show intensity
Added ة to make it refer to one specific instance of evil/harm
Added tanwin kasra to show this is the object of the word مِن (from) in the ayah
This is a basic particle meaning 'from' that shows where something comes from or its source

steps.m: so from (connects to حَسَنَةٍ 'good' in previous phrase)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to connect this idea to what came before, showing a result or sequence. Here it connects to the mention of 'good things' (حَسَنَةٍ) in the previous phrase.

steps.m: to breathe, to be precious, to be a self

steps.n: Root letters were connected with vowels (َ and ْ) to make the noun meaning 'self' or 'soul'
Added kasra at the end because it comes after the preposition مِن
Added ك at the end to show it means 'your self'
Word remains the same as previous step since no additional changes are required

steps.m: to send or deliver something from one place/person to another

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Adding أ at the beginning makes the verb causative - showing someone caused the sending to happen
Adding نا shows that 'we' are doing the action instead of 'he'
Adding و connects this action to what came before it in the verse

steps.m: and we sent you (as a messenger to النَّاسِ)
steps.n: Adding ك shows that 'you' are the one being sent. The word relates to النَّاسِ (the people) who are being sent to.

steps.m: people (those who are familiar with each other)
steps.n: Changes from verb 'to be familiar' to noun meaning 'people'
Added 'the' to make it specific
Added لِ at the beginning to show this is for/to the people, and added ِ at the end because it follows لِ
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed from a verb (sent) to a noun (messenger) by adding و and changing vowels

steps.m: a messenger (object of أَرْسَلْنٰكَ - we sent you)
steps.n: The ًا ending shows this word is receiving the action of the verb أَرْسَلْنٰكَ (we sent you) in this ayah
Added fathas to make it past tense. The ى at the end is because this is a special type of word that ends in ي
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this with the previous statement, showing this is part of a longer thought

steps.m: and he/it was sufficient (as a statement about اللَّهِ)
steps.n: Added the small alif (ٰ) to show this is a complete statement. This word is talking about Allah being sufficient as a witness.
This is Allah's proper name. It's unique and doesn't come from a root like other Arabic words.
Added shadda on ل to show it's pronounced strongly
Added kasra at the end to show possession or connection
Added بِ at beginning to show how something is done or the means by which it happens. The ٱ is added as a helper letter for pronunciation.

steps.m: to witness or testify to something

steps.n: Adding vowels makes this a past tense verb meaning 'to witness'
Changed from past tense verb 'witnessed' to noun 'witness' by adding ي in the middle
Added tanween alif to show this describes how Allah sent the messenger - as a witness to people
This is a conditional word that starts a condition - whoever does something, something else will happen. Here it means 'whoever obeys...'
Added emphasis by doubling the م sound with a shadda. This emphasizes the condition - WHOEVER does this...
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
ي at start shows it's happening now (present tense) for 'he'

steps.m: he obeys (in response to الرَّسُولَ - the Messenger)
steps.n: The word changes from 'obeys willingly' to a shorter form 'obeys' because it comes after 'whoever' (مَن). The shorter form shows it's connected to 'the Messenger' (الرَّسُولَ) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changed verb 'send' into noun 'messenger' by adding و

steps.m: the messenger (specifically Muhammad ﷺ in this context)
steps.n: Adding ال (the) makes this 'the messenger' and puts a shadda mark on the first letter
The fatha at the end shows this word is being acted upon by the verb يُطِعِ (obeys) in مَّن يُطِعِ ٱلرَّسُولَ (whoever obeys the messenger)
Added vowel marks to show exact pronunciation
Added فَ at the beginning to connect this statement to what comes after it. This shows that obeying the messenger leads to (then) certainly obeying Allah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Added hamza with fatha at start. The و changed to ا due to being a weak letter. This word is the main verb whose subject is مَن (whoever) at the start of the phrase.
Changed from 'a deity' to 'The Deity' by adding ال. The first letter changes to ل and combines with the second ل to make a double letter with shadda.

steps.m: Allah (as the object being obeyed in أَطَاعَ)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show Allah is the object being obeyed in the phrase. The alif at the start changes to a special connecting alif (ٱ).
This is a special word (particle) that doesn't come from a root. It's used to start conditional statements or questions about people.
Added و at the beginning to connect this part to what came before. This word is the subject (doer) of the verb تَوَلَّىٰ (turns away) in this ayah.

steps.m: to turn away/aside, to take charge

steps.n: Doubling the middle letter creates intensive/causative meaning
Adding تَ shows he did this action to himself. This word relates to the previous mention of طاعة (obedience) in the ayah, showing someone turning away from being obedient.
The final ي changes to ى with alif above it (ىٰ) because it's at the end of the word
Added fatha to show correct pronunciation
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from the previous statement. Here it connects to the previous condition about obeying the messenger.
Added alif with madda (آ) to show long vowel sound. This is just a spelling variation and doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to send or dispatch (a message or messenger)

steps.n: Basic past tense verb form of root meaning 'to send' - not Form IV (that would be أرسل)
Adding أ (alif) at the start makes it mean 'caused to send' instead of just 'sent'. The نا suffix changes it to 'we sent'
Adding the suffix ك shows who received the action (the object)
Basic preposition meaning 'on' or 'upon'
The ى changes to يْ to prepare for adding the pronoun suffix
Added هِمْ at the end to show this applies to 'them' (a group)

steps.m: to protect or guard something carefully

steps.n: Adding the basic vowels turns the root into a complete past tense verb
Added ي to show this describes someone who does this action strongly or frequently

steps.m: a guardian (object of أَرْسَلْنٰكَ/We sent you)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this word is being acted upon by the verb 'We sent you' (أَرْسَلْنٰكَ)
Added vowels to connect the root letters. The middle و changes to ا in past tense.
Changes to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. The middle ا returns to و.
Adds conjunction prefix و meaning 'and'
Connected letters with vowels. The weak letter و changes to ا after fatha.
Changes from 'he obeyed' (verb) to 'an act of obedience' (noun) by adding ة at the end
The word 'obedience' gets a special mark (tanween) to show it's what they are saying in the sentence
This is a conditional particle that shows when something happens. It connects two events in time, like saying 'When X happens, then Y happens'
The فَ shows this is a response or result following what came before. In this ayah, it connects to when they claim obedience (طَاعَةٌ)
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make past tense verb

steps.m: they emerged openly (subject of يَقُولُونَ - they say)
steps.n: Added وا to show multiple people did the action. This word connects to يَقُولُونَ (they say) earlier in the verse - it's the same group of people doing both actions.
This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. It's one of the most common Arabic words.
Added vowel marks to show exact pronunciation: kasra (ِ) under م and sukun (ْ) on ن. In this ayah, it shows people moving away from the Prophet's presence.

steps.m: to be near, to be present

steps.n: The root letters get a kasra vowel mark to make the preposition meaning 'near/with'
Adding the sukun (circle mark) on the middle letter helps show this is a complete word
Final kasra completes the genitive case marking

steps.m: near you, with you (after the word مِنْ)
steps.n: Adding كَ at the end shows this relates to 'you'

steps.m: to plan or decide at night

steps.n: Added basic past tense vowels to show who did the action and when

steps.m: he schemed/plotted carefully at night (subject of يُبَيِّتُونَ)
steps.n: Added shadda to show this was done with extra effort or intensity. This word is related to يُبَيِّتُونَ (they scheme) in the ayah, showing what they did in the past.
Added vowels to connect the letters and make a past tense verb
Changed from verb (he went around) to noun (one who goes around)
Added ة (taa marbutah) at the end to change from 'one who circles/moves' to mean 'a group moving together'
Added tanween dammah to show this is indefinite (like saying 'a group' instead of 'the group')
This is a preposition that shows where something comes from or is part of
The shadda is added due to assimilation with a preceding letter in the ayah
Added هُمْ which means 'them' (for a group of males). This refers to the group mentioned in the ayah who say one thing but do another when they leave.
Root letters got vowel marks (َ and ْ) to make a word meaning 'other than' or 'not'
Added fatha at the end to show this word is describing what comes after it in the ayah
This is the basic form of the word that connects parts of a sentence, like saying 'which' or 'who' in English
Added ٱلْ at the beginning and doubled the ذ letter to make it stronger. This word connects what someone says to the person who says it.
Basic root letters that form the foundation of the word
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from past tense 'he said' to present tense 'saying' by changing vowels to u-sounds and dropping the middle و letter
Added تَ at the beginning to show 'you' are doing the action
This is a unique proper name that doesn't come from a root. It's the personal name of God in Islam.

steps.m: Allah (as the subject of يَكْتُبُ - writes)
steps.n: The ُ (damma) at the end shows this word is doing the action in the sentence - Allah is the one who writes.

steps.m: and Allah (as the subject of يَكْتُبُ - writes)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this with what came before, and the ٱ is just how the ال looks after وَ.
This is the basic concept of writing
Added fathas to connect the letters and show past tense
Removed first 'a' sound and added 'u' sounds to make it present tense
Added يَ at the beginning to show 'he' is doing the action in the present tense. This word describes what 'he' does in relation to يَقُولُ (he says) in this ayah.
This is a relative pronoun connecting to يَكْتُبُ (writes/records) in this ayah. It refers to the secret plans mentioned in the previous word يُبَيِّتُونَ (they plan at night).

steps.m: to plan or decide something at night

steps.n: Added shadda to show this is done with extra effort or intensity
Added يُ at start to show this happens in present/future. Changed middle vowel to kasra for this form.
Added ون at the end to show multiple people are doing this

steps.m: to turn away or present something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Changed from 'he turned away' to a command 'turn away!' by adding أ at the start and changing the vowels
Added فَ at beginning to show this action follows from what was mentioned before
This is a preposition that shows movement away from something. It connects to the command فَأَعْرِضْ (so turn away) in this ayah.
عَنْ (away from) + هُمْ (them) = away from them. هُمْ refers back to the group who change their words in private

steps.m: to trust or depend on someone

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Doubling the middle letter makes the meaning stronger

steps.m: he put his complete trust (in someone)
steps.n: Adding تَ shows doing the action to yourself - here meaning to place your own trust in someone
Changed from 'he trusted' to 'you trust!' by making it a command
Added و at beginning to mean 'and'
This is a preposition that shows something is on top of, depending on, or relying upon something else. In this ayah, it connects to اللَّهِ (Allah) to show reliance upon Allah.
Added ال to specify this is THE one true deity, and the hamza merged with the lam getting a shadda
The word 'Allah' got an 'i' sound at the end because it comes after the word 'upon'. The first 'a' sound changed to connect smoothly with the word before it.
Added fathas to make it past tense 'he was sufficient'. The ى at the end is how we write this type of word when it ends with a ي sound.

steps.m: and he/it was enough (connected to يَكْتُبُ - writes)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this word to the previous statement. The small alif (ٰ) is added to show the proper pronunciation of the ى.
This is the basic word for deity or object of worship
Adding shadda on ل makes this the proper name for the One True God in Islam
Adding fatha on the shadda helps with pronunciation
Adding kasra at the end shows possession or connection
Adding بِ at the beginning shows how something is done or what it's done with. The ٱ is just a different way of writing the regular ال.

steps.m: to trust someone with something, to put in charge
one who is trusted with responsibility

Added ًا at the end to show this word is being acted upon by كَفَىٰ (sufficient) in the phrase 'Allah is sufficient as a guardian'
This particle turns a statement into a question, like adding a question mark in English
Added فَ which shows one thing follows another, like saying 'then' or 'so' in English

steps.m: Then do they not? (questioning يَتَدَبَّرُونَ - they ponder)
steps.n: Added لَا which makes the question negative. This combination questions why people are not doing something (specifically pondering/reflecting on the Quran, as shown by يَتَدَبَّرُونَ in the ayah)

steps.m: to think about consequences or outcomes

steps.n: Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels
Doubling the middle letter makes the meaning more intense
Adding تَ shows doing the action thoroughly to oneself
Adding يَ at start and ُ at end makes it present tense
Adding ونَ makes it plural - shows multiple people doing the action

steps.m: that which is read/recited (object of يَتَدَبَّرُونَ)

steps.m: the Quran (as the object being pondered over)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being acted upon in the sentence

steps.m: the Quran (specifically referring to Allah's revelation that is being pondered over)

This is a conditional particle that introduces hypothetical scenarios. It's like saying 'what if' or 'even if' in English.
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this condition with the previous statement. In this ayah, it connects to the question 'Do they not ponder over the Quran?'
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters
Changed و to ا because it's a hollow verb (has weak middle letter)

steps.m: from (connects عِندِ to what comes before it)

Added vowel marks to make it into a preposition showing location or possession
Added kasra at the end to show this word is possessed by غَيْرِ (other than) in the phrase 'from other than Allah'
Connected the root letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter to create the basic noun
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to Allah (ٱللَّهِ) that comes after it
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding specific vowel patterns
Adding 'al' makes it specific - now talking about a particular deity

steps.m: of the deity, of the god

Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense verb
Added وا at the end to show multiple people did the action
Added لَ at the beginning to show this is a hypothetical result (what would happen)
Basic preposition meaning 'in'
Added kasra vowel mark to show correct pronunciation
Added هِ (hi) at the end to show what is being referred to. Here it refers back to the Quran mentioned earlier in the ayah.

Added اِ at start and ت after first letter to show people disagreeing with each other
Changed the verb into a noun meaning the act itself
Added the accusative case ending to show this word is the object
Word is already in its final form with all necessary elements
The vowels show this is about something increasing or becoming more
Changed from a verb (action) to a describing word by adding ي and changing the middle vowel

steps.m: much (describes the amount of اختلافا/disagreement they would find)

This is a basic word that shows when something happens. It's like saying 'at the time when' or 'whenever' in English.

steps.m: and when (connects this condition to what came before)
steps.n: Adding وَ at the beginning connects this condition to what was said before, like adding 'and' in English
Changed from basic root letters to past tense 'he came' by adding the long aa sound (alif) and fatha marks
Added the long alif with hamza to emphasize the action of coming
Added هُمْ at the end to show who was affected by the action (them)
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to make it a noun meaning the result of commanding
Added tanween damma to show this is an indefinite noun. In this ayah, it refers to a matter or news that came to them.
The shadda indicates assimilation with the following word's first letter. The fatha is added because it's connected to the following word.
Root letters turned into a noun meaning 'safety' by adding marks (fatha-sukun-nothing)

Added kasra to show this word follows مِّنَ (from) in the ayah

Added kasra at the end because it's followed by a word starting with ال. This is a pronunciation helper that doesn't change the meaning.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make a noun from the basic meaning
Added ال to specify a particular fear
Added kasra at the end because it comes after the word مِنَ (from/of) in the ayah
Connected the root letters with vowels. The weak letter ي changes to ا in this pattern.

Added kasra to the preposition ب to make it pronounceable

steps.m: and even if (connects to أَذَاعُوا۟ - they spread it)

The و makes it mean someone who does this action as their main role
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the ر with shadda

steps.m: of the messenger (connected to رَدُّوهُ - they returned it to)

Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it, like saying 'and' in English

Added ال to specify a particular command or matter being discussed

Added كُمْ at the end to show this applies to multiple people being spoken to

steps.m: to show kindness and care for someone

steps.n: Adding vowels to create the basic noun meaning 'mercy'

steps.m: to follow or go after something

Added ا at start and doubled ت to show doing something with extra effort or purpose
Added تُم at the end to show many people (you all) did this action in the past
Added لَ at start to show this is part of a 'what if' statement, and ُ at end to connect to next word

The ا makes it a special pattern that means someone who does this rebellious action as their main quality

Adds a fatHa mark (َ) at the end to show this is the object being acted upon
This is a basic word that doesn't come from a root. The shadda shows emphasis on the ل.
The ا at the end helps connect this word to what comes after it. Here it introduces an exception - قَلِيلًا (a few/little).

steps.m: to be small in amount or number

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Added ي between second and third letters and changed vowels to make an adjective
Added tanween alif to show this describes how much of something there is
Added alif after first letter and fatha marks to show mutual action - fighting against someone
Changed vowels to make it a command form - telling someone to fight

This is a basic preposition that shows location or being inside something. Here it connects 'fight' (فَقٰتِلْ) with 'path' (سَبِيلِ) to mean 'fight in the path'.
The ي changes to ى at the end of words in Arabic writing. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.

steps.m: to travel a path or way

Added kasra at the end because it's followed by اللَّهِ (Allah) showing this is Allah's path

steps.m: you are burdened (connected to لَا before it meaning 'you are not burdened')
steps.n: Adding تُ at start and ُ at end changes it from 'he burdened' to 'you are being burdened' - showing someone else is putting the burden on you

steps.m: to breathe, to be precious, soul

steps.n: Root letters ن-ف-س got vowels (َ and ْ) to make the common noun meaning 'self' or 'soul'

steps.m: to urge strongly or encourage vigorously

steps.n: Added short 'a' vowels (fatha) between the root letters to make 'he urged strongly' in the past tense
Added shadda to intensify the meaning
Changed from past tense 'he urged' to a command 'urge!' by changing the vowels and adding a stop mark at the end

steps.m: and urge very strongly! (command to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to encourage الْمُؤْمِنِينَ)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to mean 'and'. Changed final vowel to kasra because it connects to the next word الْمُؤْمِنِينَ (the believers).
Added an alif at the start to mean 'he entered into a state of believing/trusting'
Changed to show who does the believing
Added ين to show there are many believers. This word is being acted upon by حَرِّضِ (encourage) in this ayah.
Adding 'the' (ال) at the start + special ending (ين) because it's receiving the action
Basic verb root got vowels (fat-ha) to make it a complete word meaning 'it is possible'
Added ى at the end to complete the word's proper spelling
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
Changed from the general word for 'a god' to the special name 'Allah' by adding ال and joining letters together
Added ُ to show this is the subject doing the action in أَشَدُّ (is more intense)
This is a word that connects two parts of a sentence, like 'that' in English
Added fatha mark on أ to show correct pronunciation. This word introduces what Allah might do (يَكُفَّ/restrain) after the word عَسَى (perhaps/it may be that).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
When two same letters come together, we combine them with a shadda mark to show emphasis
Added يَ at start and changed first vowel to show this is happening in the future. This verb comes after أَن (that) in the ayah, showing what Allah might do.
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to make it a noun meaning 'might' or 'strength'
Added fatha at the end because this word is being acted upon by the verb يَكُفَّ (to restrain/prevent)
Starting with the basic demonstrative word and changing it to make it relative
Added ين to make it refer to multiple people or things
Added ال to make it specific. This word points to the people who did كَفَرُوا (disbelieved) in this ayah.
Added vowel marks and changed spelling to match Uthmani script

steps.m: to reject or cover up truth

steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past

steps.m: they rejected (truth) (subject of عَسَى ٱللَّهُ أَن يَكُفَّ بَأْسَ)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to show multiple people did this action. This refers to the disbelievers whose harm Allah might restrain, as mentioned in the ayah.
This is a unique proper name that doesn't come from a root. It's the personal name of God in Islam.
Added shadda on ل to show it's pronounced strongly

steps.m: God (as the subject of يَكُفَّ - will restrain)
steps.n: Added damma at the end to show this is the doer of the action in the sentence

steps.m: and God (as the subject of يَكُفَّ - will restrain)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this with the previous statement
Root letters ش-د-د became شَدِيد by adding vowels and ي to make a word meaning 'strong'
Added أَ at beginning and merged double د with shadda to make comparative form
Added damma at end because this word is describing بَأْسًا (might) and needs to match it grammatically
Changed from root letters to a noun meaning strength/power by adding vowels
Added tanween alif to show this word is the object being compared in the phrase 'stronger in might'
Added ي between second and third root letters to create adjective pattern
Added أ at the start to make it mean 'more/most', like turning 'strong' into 'stronger'

steps.m: and stronger/more intense (in might/strength than بَأْسًا)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect to previous phrase. This word compares Allah's might to that of the disbelievers.

steps.m: to punish severely or make an example of someone

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb
Added shadda to middle letter to show stronger/more intense action
Changed verb into noun showing the action itself
Added tanween alif to show this word is being acted upon in the sentence
Base form of the relative pronoun used to refer to people. Used here to introduce the person who will intercede.
Added shadda for emphasis and to connect with the following word more smoothly in pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the past tense verb
Added يَ at beginning to show present tense, changed vowels to show ongoing action
Changed final vowel to sukun because this word appears in a conditional phrase after مَن (whoever)

steps.m: to intercede or mediate between two things

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changing the middle letter to a long 'aa' sound turns the action 'he interceded' into the name of that action 'intercession'
Adding ة makes it refer to one specific instance of intercession
Adding tanween (double fatha) makes it indefinite and shows it's the object
Changed from root letters to a complete word meaning 'was good/beautiful' by adding the short 'a' vowels
Added ة to make it a noun referring to one specific good action

steps.m: a good deed (object of يَشْفَعْ - intercedes)
steps.n: Added tanween to show this is the object being acted upon by the verb يَشْفَعْ (intercedes)
Added vowels to connect the root letters
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
The word is shortened by dropping the last vowel and و because it comes after من (whoever) at the start of an 'if' statement
Combined the preposition ل (to/for) with the pronoun ه (him) to show something belongs to or is for him
Added shadda to emphasize the meaning. This often happens when words are combined with words before them.
Added the elongation mark ۥ after هُ to show proper pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.
Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb
Changes from verb 'to establish' to noun meaning 'a portion'

steps.m: a share/portion (that will be established for someone who intercedes)
steps.n: Added tanween dammah to show this is any share/portion, not a specific one. In this ayah, it refers to the reward that will be established for someone who makes intercession.
Basic preposition meaning 'from'
Added emphasis on م because it comes after a word ending in a vowel
Added ها which refers back to شَفٰعَةً سَيِّئَةً (evil intercession) mentioned earlier in the ayah
This is a basic interrogative/conditional particle that means 'whoever' or 'who'. In this ayah, it introduces the second part of a pair of conditions (whoever does good... and whoever does bad...)
Added و at the beginning to connect this condition with the previous one. This word starts the second of two parallel conditions about interceding for good vs. bad.

steps.m: to mediate or help someone by speaking for them

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters and show this happened in the past

steps.m: he mediates/helps (in a conditional statement with مَن)
steps.n: The يَ at the start shows this is happening now. The sukun marks (ْ) show this is part of a conditional statement starting with مَن (whoever).

steps.m: to join things together or pair them up

steps.n: Added fatha marks to make it mean 'he joined' in the past tense
Added alif after second root letter to make it into a noun meaning the action itself
Added ة at the end to show one specific instance of intercession
Added tanween (the -an sound) to show this is the thing being interceded for, like when you say 'I interceded an intercession'
Root meaning 'bad' turned into a noun meaning 'badness'
The و changes to ي and gets doubled with shadda to show intensity
Added ة to make it refer to one specific bad action

steps.m: an evil deed (object of يَشْفَعْ - intercedes)
steps.n: Added tanween to show this is the object being interceded for in the verse

steps.m: to be, exist, happen, or occur

steps.n: Adds يَ at start to make it 'he/it' in present tense, with a ُ (damma) on ك to show action
Adds ْ (sukun) at the end to show it's in jussive mood (like after لَمْ)
Connected the basic letters with vowel marks to form the word meaning 'for him'
The shadda (ّ) appears because this word follows a word ending in ن, causing the ن to merge into the ل. The ۥ is just a pronunciation guide that doesn't change the meaning.
Added kasra and sukun to create a noun meaning 'share' or 'portion' from the concept of taking responsibility
Added tanween damma to show this is the subject of يَكُن (there will be)
Added shadda to show connection with previous word. This is a pronunciation helper that doesn't change meaning.
Added sukun to show the 'n' sound stops clearly. This is just a pronunciation helper.
Added ها which means 'it/her' to show what is being referred to. Here it refers back to شَفٰعَةً (intercession) which is feminine.
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a hollow verb (has و in middle)

steps.m: and he was/existed (connects to مَّن يَشْفَعْ)
steps.n: Adds و at beginning to mean 'and', connecting this phrase to the previous part about 'whoever intercedes'
Changed the root into the basic word for 'deity' by adding vowels
This is the unique name for The One True God in Islam. The ال makes it definite (like 'the'), the shadda emphasizes the ل, and the ُ shows this is the subject of كَانَ (was) in this ayah.
Basic form showing the concept of being high or elevated
Adding ى changes it from a concept to a preposition that shows one thing is positioned above another
Adding the small alif (ٰ) helps with pronunciation but doesn't change the meaning. This word connects to كُلِّ (all) in the ayah.
Added damma (ُ) and doubled the middle letter to show completeness
Added kasra (ِ) at the end to show this word is connected to شَىْءٍ (thing) that comes after it, making the meaning 'of every thing'
Root letters put together to make the common word for 'thing'
The ٍ shows this word belongs to كُلِّ (every/all) in the ayah

steps.m: to provide, sustain, or have power over
one who has complete power and control
steps.n: Changes the root word into a special form meaning 'someone who does the action' - in this case someone who has power and control over others
The shadda emphasizes the completeness and absoluteness of Allah's power and authority

steps.m: All-Powerful, having complete authority over everything (object of كَانَ)
steps.n: Makes the word the object of كَانَ (was) in this ayah, showing that Allah was and continues to be All-Powerful over everything

steps.m: when (introducing a condition that will happen)
steps.n: This is a time word that introduces something that will happen in the future. It's different from other words for 'when' because it expects the action to actually occur.

steps.m: and when (introducing the next part of حُيِّيتُم - when you are greeted)
steps.n: The و connects this condition with what came before. Here it introduces what to do when someone greets you.

steps.m: to greet warmly, to give life

steps.n: Changed root letters into a past tense verb by adding vowels and doubling middle letter to show strong greeting action
Changed vowels to show this happened to someone rather than someone doing it
Added تُم at the end to show this happened to multiple people being addressed
Changes from basic root to noun meaning 'a greeting'
The tanween shows this is indefinite (any greeting) and is being acted upon by the preposition ب
Added shadda to combine the double ي and added fatha to show past tense
Added وا to show multiple people did the action
Adds فَ to show this is a response or result that should follow after being greeted (connects to حُيِّيتُم in previous part of verse)
Connected the root letters with fatha to create basic past tense verb
Adding hamza prefix and sukun creates comparative form meaning 'better/more beautiful than'. This word is compared to مِنْهَآ (than it) in this ayah.
Preposition بِ adds meaning of 'with' or 'by means of'. This word is part of the command فَحَيُّوا۟ (then respond) in this ayah, telling how to respond.
This is a basic word showing where something comes from or starts, like saying 'from' in English
The هَا refers back to the greeting (تَحِيَّةٍ) mentioned earlier in the verse. It shows we're talking about responding to or returning that same greeting.
The alif is written long (آ) when it comes at the end of a word for clarity in reading, but the meaning stays the same

This is a connecting word that gives a choice between two options: either respond with a better greeting (حَيُّوا) or return it equally (رُدُّوهَآ). It's like the English word 'or' when giving choices.
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels
Changed from 'he returned' to 'you return!' by adding a shadda and changing the vowel to damma - this makes it a command

steps.m: you all give back! (command to group)
steps.n: Added plural command ending

steps.m: you all give it back! (command to group, referring to feminine object)
steps.n: Added feminine object pronoun with elongation to show what is being returned

Adding the shadda (ّ) changes this from a conditional word into an emphasis word. It makes what comes after it stronger, like adding an exclamation mark in English. Here it emphasizes that Allah is the one who keeps count of all things.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity'
Added ال (the) and merged it with the word, then added shadda to show emphasis. This special form is only used for Allah, the one true God.
Added fatha at the end because Allah is the object of إِنَّ (indeed/verily) in this ayah
The root letters got vowel marks and the middle letter و changed to ا to make the past tense 'he was'. This word tells us about Allah in this ayah.
Basic preposition that shows something is physically or metaphorically on top of something else, like saying 'the book is on the table' or 'the responsibility is upon you'
Added the small alif (ىٰ) at the end to show this is connected to the word حَسِيبًا (keeper of accounts) in this ayah, meaning Allah is keeping account over all things
Added damma and doubled the middle letter to create a word meaning 'all' or 'every'
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to the word after it (شَىْءٍ meaning 'thing')

steps.m: to want or wish for something

steps.n: The root ش-ي-أ becomes the common noun شَيْء meaning 'thing'. The root meaning 'to want' is not directly related - they just share letters. This word is in the genitive case after كُلِّ (every/all).
The ٍ (kasratan) at the end shows this is indefinite and in the genitive case, like after 'of' in English
The tanwin kasra (ٍ) was already there in both words - the only change is the letter ي was written as ى, which is just a writing style difference with the same meaning and pronunciation

steps.m: to count, calculate, take account of
one who takes full account (of everything)
steps.n: The فَعِيل pattern here shows Allah's attribute of being comprehensive in His accounting of all things
The tanween (ًا) here shows this word is describing how Allah is, connected to كَانَ اللَّهُ (Allah was/is) in the ayah
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding vowel marks
Added 'al' to make it specific/definite - like changing 'a god' to 'the god'
Added damma to show this word is the subject of the sentence
Basic negative particle that negates what comes after it. In this ayah, it negates إِلٰهَ (deity) to say 'there is no deity'.
Added fatha to show clear pronunciation
Changed alif to long alif (آ) to add extra emphasis, showing this is a strong statement that there is absolutely no deity except Allah
Changes from verb 'to worship' into noun meaning 'a deity'
Added َ at the end because it's being affected by لا (no). The long alif (ٰ) is just a writing style variation.

steps.m: except (excluding هُوَ from what came before)
steps.n: This is a basic particle that excludes what comes after it (هُوَ meaning 'He') from what came before (other gods/deities). In this ayah, it's emphasizing that there is no deity except Allah.
This is a pronoun that refers back to Allah mentioned just before. In this ayah, it emphasizes that Allah alone will gather everyone.

steps.m: to gather, collect, or bring together

steps.n: Added يَ at beginning to show 'he' does the action in present/future time, with ْ (sukun) to show basic form
Added emphatic lam prefix to indicate certainty
Added heavy emphasis نَّ suffix which requires fatha before it

steps.m: most surely he will gather you all (referring to all humans on the Day of Judgment)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at end to show who is being gathered. In this ayah, Allah is saying He will definitely gather all people on the Day of Judgment.

This is a basic preposition that shows movement or direction towards something. In this ayah, it connects يَجْمَعَنَّكُمْ (He will gather you) to يَوْمِ (day), showing you will be gathered to/towards the Day of Judgment.
Added an alif symbol (ىٰ) at the end to show this is a complete word. This doesn't change the meaning, it just helps with reading.
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to make the basic word for 'day'. This word is connected to القيامة to show which day we're talking about.
Added ِ at the end to show this word belongs to or is connected to the word after it (القيامة/Resurrection)
Root letters combined with vowels to make a noun meaning 'people/folk'
Changed from 'standing' to mean 'the act of rising up' by adding ي. Like changing from 'stand' to 'standing up' in English
Added ة to specify one instance of rising/resurrection
Added ال for definiteness and kasra because it follows إِلَىٰ يَوْمِ (to the day of). The alif is written as a long alif.

This is a negative particle that makes the word after it (رَيْبَ meaning 'doubt') into its opposite - so 'no doubt'. In this ayah, it's part of a stronger statement that there is absolutely no doubt about the Day of Judgment.
Added fatha mark on ل to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'laa'

steps.m: to doubt, be uncertain, or feel suspicion

steps.n: Changed the root letters into a noun meaning the actual feeling of doubt
Added a fatha mark at the end to show this word is being used as a direct object
This is a preposition that shows something is inside or within something else

steps.m: in it (referring to يَوْمِ ٱلْقِيٰمَةِ - Day of Resurrection)
steps.n: Added the pronoun ه (him/it) to show what is being referred to. In this ayah, it refers back to the Day of Resurrection mentioned earlier.

steps.m: who (asks about or refers to people)
steps.n: This is a question word used to ask about people (like 'who' in English) or refer to people (like 'whoever' or 'one who' in English)
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this question/statement to what came before it
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Added أَ at the beginning and changed vowels to make it mean 'more truthful'. This word is being compared with 'than Allah' (مِنَ ٱللَّهِ) in this ayah.
This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or its origin
Added َ (fatha) at the end to connect smoothly to the next word اللَّهِ (Allah). This combination means 'from Allah'
Added emphasis with shadda to show this is the supreme, ultimate deity
Added kasra at the end to show possession or connection, and changed alif to wasl-alif since it connects with previous word مِنَ

steps.m: to tell or speak about something new

steps.n: Changes from verb 'to tell' into noun/adjective meaning 'new/speech'
Added tanween alif to show this word is the object being compared in the phrase 'who is more truthful in speech than Allah'
Starting with the basic question word ما which means 'what'

steps.m: then what (questioning about the state of المنٰفقين)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this question follows from something before. In this ayah, it's questioning about the state of the hypocrites (المنٰفقين).
Added fatha to show the basic vowel of the preposition

steps.m: for you all (related to فَمَا - so what)
steps.n: Added the pronoun كُمْ which means 'you all'. This word connects to فَمَا (so what) to ask 'so what is for you all?'
Basic preposition showing location or position inside something
Added ى at the end to complete the word. This preposition connects to the word فِئَتَيْنِ (two groups) to show they are divided into two groups.

steps.m: to pass through, to spend (with hidden meaning of going out/depleting)
steps.n: Basic past tense form of the verb
The ا shows someone pretending to do something - here pretending to have faith
Changes verb 'to act hypocritically' into a noun meaning 'person who acts hypocritically' by adding مُ at start
Added plural suffix ين to make word plural. The word comes after the preposition في which puts it in the genitive case.
Added ال to make the word definite. The word remains in genitive case due to preposition.
Added final vowel mark to show this word is the object of the preposition فِي (in/concerning) in this ayah

steps.m: to separate or divide into parts
a group (that has separated from others)
steps.n: Changes the root letters into a noun meaning 'group' by adding the vowel marks i-a

steps.m: two groups (in the ayah: referring to two groups of people with different opinions about the hypocrites)
steps.n: Changes the word to show exactly two groups by adding يْنِ at the end. The ة changes to ت when we add endings.
The word already has the dual ending ين (ayn) - it wasn't added as a transformation. The only change is removing the short vowel marks.
Connected the root letters to form the basic word for deity
Added 'L' sound (shadda) to show this is not just any god, but the one true God - Allah
Added fatha on the shadda for pronunciation
Added damma to show Allah is the subject doing the action in this sentence
Added و at beginning to mean 'and', with helping alif after it

steps.m: he turned back (to a worse state)
steps.n: Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters

steps.m: he caused to turn back (by Allah)
steps.n: Added أَ prefix and sukun to create causative form. Allah is the subject doing this action to others.

steps.m: He (Allah) caused them to turn back
steps.n: Added هُم suffix to show who the action was done to (them)
Combines preposition بِ (with/by) with relative pronoun مَا (what)

steps.m: to gain, acquire, or earn through effort

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past

steps.m: they gained/earned (subject of أَرْكَسَهُم - cast them back)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to show a group did this action. This word explains what the group did before Allah cast them back (أَرْكَسَهُم).
This is the basic concept of wanting or seeking something
Added vowel marks to the root letters to make it mean 'he wanted'
Changed from 'he wanted' to 'you/she/it want' by adding تُ at start and changing middle vowel to ي
Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action
Added أَ at the beginning to make it a question
This is a connecting word that links تُرِيدُونَ (you want) to تَهْدُوا۟ (to guide), showing what is wanted. It's like saying 'you want that you guide' in English.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks. The final ي changes to ى because it's a weak letter at the end.
Added تَ at start to show 'you' and changed vowels to show present tense. The ى changes back to ي in present tense.
Added وا at the end to show multiple people are doing the action. The ي drops out when وا is added.
This is a special word used to ask about or refer to people. In this ayah, it's the object (receiver) of the verb تَهْدُوا (you want to guide), showing who they want to guide.

steps.m: to lose the way, to stray from the right path
he strayed from the right path
steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters together
Added أَ at the beginning to show causing someone else to do the action. Changed first vowel to sukun.
When two same letters come together at the end, they combine with a shadda. Changed middle vowel back to fatha for easier pronunciation.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
Changed to the unique name of the One True God by adding ال and merging letters. The ُ shows this is the doer of the action in this sentence.
Combines و (and) with مَن (who)

steps.m: to lead astray/cause to go astray

steps.n: Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels
Changed from past to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing middle vowel to kasra (i sound)
Added kasra (i sound) at end to show jussive mood - used when something depends on something else
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
This is the unique name for The One True God in Islam. The ال makes it definite (like 'the'), the shadda emphasizes the ل, and the ُ shows this is the subject doing the action in this sentence.
This is a negative particle that makes the verb after it negative in the future tense, like saying 'will never' or 'will not' in English

steps.m: so never/so will not (negates تَجِدَ 'you will find')
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result or consequence. This word makes the verb تَجِدَ (you will find) negative in the future.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb

steps.m: you find (in لَن تَجِدَ - will never find)
steps.n: Changed to present tense 'you' form. The word appears after لَن which means it will never happen in the future.
Added fatha to show the vowel sound on the preposition
Added the pronoun هُ (him) to show who the preposition is directed to
Added the prolongation mark (ۥ) to show the extended pronunciation of the final vowel

steps.m: to make a path or way

steps.n: Adding ي and vowels creates the pattern for 'path'

steps.m: a way (object of تَجِدَ - you will find)
steps.n: Added ًا to show this is the object being found in the phrase 'you will not find for him a way'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make past tense verb
The doubled middle letter shows intensity of feeling
The ُ at the end shows this is the subject of the sentence

steps.m: they wished/loved intensely (subject of لَوْ تَكْفُرُونَ)
steps.n: Added وا to show multiple people doing the action. This word connects to لَوْ (if/that) which introduces what they wished for.
This is a basic particle that introduces a hypothetical situation that didn't happen. In this ayah, it connects to تَكْفُرُونَ (you disbelieve) to show what others wished would happen but didn't.
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation: fatha (َ) on ل and sukun (ْ) on و
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make a past tense verb
Changed to present tense with you as subject by adding تَ at start and changing vowels
Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action
This is a comparison particle that shows similarity

steps.m: just as (connects to كَفَرُوا۟ - they disbelieved)
steps.n: Adding ما makes the comparison more specific and connects it to what follows. Here it connects to how 'they disbelieved' (كَفَرُوا۟)

steps.m: to reject or cover up truth

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added وا at the end to show many people did this action together
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters
Added تَ at start to mean 'you/she' and ُ at end to show it's happening now
Added plural suffix ون to show multiple people

steps.m: so you all become (consequence of كَفَرُوا)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this is a result of the previous action (their disbelief)

steps.m: to be the same as something else

steps.n: Changes from verb to noun meaning 'state of being equal'
Added ً at the end to show this word is describing how they are equal in their disbelief
Basic negation word that stops something from happening

Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result or consequence of what came before. Here it connects to the next verb تَتَّخِذُوا۟ (take/adopt), showing what not to do as a result of the previous situation.

steps.m: to grasp or take hold of something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
The ت combines with first letter to show doing something with more effort
The ت at start shows it's about 'she' or 'you'
The ُ at the end shows this is a normal statement (not a command or wish)
Changed from 'she takes' to 'you all take' by adding وا at the end - makes it a command for a group
This is a preposition that shows something coming from somewhere or someone

steps.m: from them (referring to those who disbelieve)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show who is being referred to. In this ayah, it refers back to the disbelievers mentioned earlier.
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb
Adding أ at start makes it mean 'to cause someone to do the action'
