Added vowel marks and elongated alif to show proper pronunciation. The fatha at the end shows this is the object of the verb اتَّخَذَ (took/adopted) in this ayah.

steps.m: to turn away from falsehood towards truth
one who turns away from falsehood towards truth
steps.n: Added ي after first root letter with vowels to show this describes someone who has this quality as part of their nature

steps.m: sincerely turning away from falsehood (describing إِبْرٰهِيمَ)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this word is describing Ibrahim in this ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed initial hamza to kasra and added ت with shadda to show doing action to/for oneself

steps.m: and he took for himself (Allah took إبراهيم as a friend)
steps.n: Added و to connect with previous phrase. The word إبراهيم comes after this verb as the object (the one who was taken as a friend).
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
Added the U mark at the end to show this is the subject
This is a proper name of Hebrew origin, so it doesn't have an Arabic root. It's the name of the prophet Abraham.
The fatha at the end shows this is the object of the verb 'followed' - meaning someone followed Abraham's way or path
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic past tense verb
Forms a noun meaning someone characterized by deep friendship and intimacy

steps.m: a very close friend (who Allah took Ibrahim as)
steps.n: The ًا ending shows this word is receiving the action of اتخذ (took/made) in the ayah - Allah took Ibrahim as a very close friend
Starting with the base word Allah, which is a proper noun referring to God
The ل added at the start means 'for' or 'to', showing something belongs to or is for Allah
The lam letter is doubled (has shadda) because two lams came together - the first lam is from لِ meaning 'for/to' and the second is from the word Allah
Added kasra at the end to show this word is being acted upon by something

steps.m: and to/for Allah (connected to ما في السماوات)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect this phrase with what follows in the ayah about what is in the heavens and earth belonging to Allah

steps.m: what/that which (refers to everything in السَّمٰوٰتِ)
steps.n: This is a special word that connects to what comes after it to show ownership or existence. Here it's used with فِى (in) to say 'what is in' or 'whatever exists in'
This is a basic preposition showing location or position

steps.m: in (connects السَّمٰوٰتِ 'the heavens' to what comes before)
steps.n: Added ى at the end to complete the preposition. In this ayah, it connects 'the heavens' to the previous phrase, showing what belongs to Allah.
Adds اء to make it a noun referring to something high up
Changed اء to او and added ات to make it plural
Added ٱل to make it specific and doubled the س with shadda
Added ِ at the end because it comes after فِى (in) in the phrase مَا فِى (what is in). Also changed alif to alif maqsurah (ٰ) in writing style.
This is a special word that shows something unknown or general. Here it refers to everything in the earth, connecting to the previous phrase about what's in the heavens.

steps.m: and what/whatever (connects to مَا فِى ٱلسَّمٰوٰتِ)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase about what's in the earth with the previous phrase about what's in the heavens
This is a basic preposition that shows location or containment. In this ayah, it connects 'what' (ما) with 'the heavens' (السَّمٰوٰتِ), showing what is located in the heavens belongs to Allah.
Added vowel marks to show pronunciation: fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter
Adding 'al' makes it refer to the specific earth. The alif has a waṣla mark (ٱ) because it connects with the previous word فِى
Adding kasra shows this word follows فِى (in/on). Together they mean "in/on the earth"
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas in a past tense verb

steps.m: and he was (connects to ٱللَّهُ - Allah)
steps.n: Added و with fatha at the beginning to mean 'and'. This word connects to ٱللَّهُ (Allah) in the ayah.
Changed from 'a god' to 'The God' - this is a unique word that became a proper name for the One True God
Added shadda on ل and ُ at the end to show this is the doer of the action 'encompassing' (مُّحِيطًا) in this ayah
Root meaning of completeness or entirety
The shadda combines the second and third letters to show completeness
The kasra shows this word belongs to شَىْءٍ (thing) that comes after it
The بِ at the beginning means 'with' and connects to مُّحِيطًا (encompassing) to show how Allah encompasses everything
Basic meaning of something existing or being willed
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make the basic noun. The hamza (ء) appears at the end because this is a special case where the root letter أ moves to the end.
Added the genitive case ending because this word comes after كُلِّ (meaning 'every') in the phrase 'every thing'
Basic meaning of surrounding or encompassing
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added مُ at start and changed middle letter to ي to make it mean 'the one who surrounds'
Added shadda to emphasize the completeness of the surrounding
Added an alif at the end to show this word is describing something (in this case Allah) - like adding 'ing' in English
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form basic past tense verb

steps.c: Present, Verb, Active, Masculine, Singular, Third, Seeking
steps.n: Added يست at the beginning to change 'giving a ruling' into 'asking for a ruling' - like changing 'teach' to 'ask to be taught'
The suffix ون changes subject from singular 'he' to plural 'they'
Added ك at the end to show you are the one being asked for the ruling
Adds conjunction 'and' to connect with previous statement
This is a basic preposition that shows what something is about or where it happens. Here it introduces what the people are asking about (women's issues).
The ي at the end is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Basic root meaning related to femininity
Root meaning 'female' becomes plural word for 'women' using special pattern
Adding definite article makes the noun definite and causes the ن to take a shadda
Adding kasra at the end shows this word follows في (in/concerning) in the ayah
This is the basic concept of speaking or saying something
Changed to command form. The weak letter و drops out, and damma is added to show it's a command.
The kasra at the end is added because this word is connected to what comes after it in the ayah
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding vowel patterns
Changed from the general word for 'god' to the special name 'Allah' by adding ال. The ُ at the end shows Allah is doing the action in this sentence.
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters

steps.m: he gives/will give a ruling (response to يَسْتَفْتُونَكَ - they ask you for a ruling)
steps.n: Changes from past tense (he gave) to present/future tense (he gives/will give) by adding يُ at the start

steps.m: he gives/will give you all a ruling (Allah responding to people's questions)
steps.n: Adds plural 'you' to show Allah is addressing multiple people asking for rulings
Basic preposition that shows location or containment - like being inside something
Added the vowel mark kasra (ِ) under ف to show how it's pronounced
Added هِنَّ at the end to show this refers to a group of females being talked about (in this case, referring back to the women mentioned in the verse)
This is a question/relative pronoun particle that can mean 'what' or 'whatever' depending on context. In this ayah, it means 'whatever' since it's referring to any good deeds that might be done.

steps.m: and whatever (is recited to you in the Book)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this clause with what came before. This connects to يُتْلَىٰ (is recited) which comes right after it in the ayah.

steps.m: to recite or read out loud

steps.n: Adding fatha vowels creates the basic past tense verb form
Changed to passive voice by adding يُ at start and putting sukun on middle letter. This shows the reciting is being done to something rather than someone doing the reciting.
The ى with ٰ (alif maqsura) at the end is needed because this word ends with و (a weak letter)
Added vowel marks to show the correct pronunciation of the basic preposition
Changed the final ى to يْ to prepare for adding the pronoun suffix. This is a spelling change that happens before pronouns.
Added كُمْ at the end to show this applies to multiple people being spoken to
Basic preposition showing what something is about or where it happens. Here it shows the topic they're asking about (the women).
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
The verb 'he wrote' becomes a noun 'book/writing' by changing the middle vowel to a long ā sound. Like how drawing (verb) becomes a drawing (noun) in English.
Added ال to make it specific and ِ at the end because it comes after the word في (in/concerning)
This is a preposition showing that the question/seeking verdict is about or concerning women. It connects يَسْتَفْتُونَكَ (they ask you for a verdict) with ٱلنِّسَآءِ (the women).

steps.m: to lose a parent, to be without protection
steps.n: Basic root meaning
Changes verb into noun describing someone affected by the action
Changes from one orphan to many orphans. The middle letter stretches longer and the ending changes to make it plural.
Basic root meaning
Changes the root into a plural noun for a group of women
Adds ٱل to show we're talking about specific women
Adds kasra at the end to show possession or after certain prepositions
Basic relative pronoun form for feminine singular

steps.m: who, those who (feminine plural, referring to يَتٰمَى ٱلنِّسَآءِ - orphan women)
steps.n: Changed from singular 'which/who' to plural 'those who' by adding ا and a shadda mark on ل. Points back to the orphan women mentioned.
Basic negative word that tells us something is not happening. In this ayah, it's saying you are not giving them what was written for them.
Basic root meaning
Root أتي (to come) changed to make it mean 'to make someone/something give' - like turning 'come' into 'bring'
Changed to passive voice - means 'is given' instead of 'gives'
Added masculine plural ending
Added feminine plural object pronoun to show who receives the action
This is a relative pronoun that connects to the verb يُتْلَىٰ (is recited). It introduces what is being recited in the Book.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb

steps.m: it was written (what was prescribed in the Book)
steps.n: Changed the vowels to show that the writing was done to something rather than someone doing the writing. This relates to what was prescribed or written in the Book mentioned earlier in the ayah (فِى ٱلْكِتٰبِ).
Added fatha to the letter lam to show its pronunciation
Added هُ (him/it) to show who the preposition is directed to
Added نَّ to change the pronoun from masculine singular (him) to feminine plural (them)
Basic root meaning
Changed from root letters to past tense 'he' form by adding vowels
Changed to present tense with ت prefix for 'you' and final damma for present tense
Added ون ending to show plural 'you all'

steps.m: and you (all) desire/want something (to marry them)
steps.n: Added و prefix meaning 'and'. From context, this refers to the desire to marry the orphan women mentioned earlier in the verse.

steps.m: that (connects to تَنكِحُوهُنَّ - you marry them)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that links two ideas. Here it connects the idea of 'wanting' (تَرْغَبُونَ) with 'marrying' (تَنكِحُوهُنَّ). It works like the word 'that' in English when you say 'I want that you help me.'
Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense with second person subject
Added plural masculine marker to show multiple people doing the action
Added 'them' (feminine) as the object of the verb - the ones being married
Added vowels to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: one who is made weak, oppressed
steps.n: The prefix مست changes the verb 'to be weak' into 'someone who is forced to be weak by others' - like a victim
Adding ين makes it plural - means more than one oppressed person
Adding the definite article ال makes the word definite
Adding the coordinating conjunction و connects this phrase to the previous one
This is a particle that shows something comes from somewhere or belongs to a group
The fatha is added to connect this word smoothly to الْوِلْدٰنِ (the children) that follows it. This is like adding glue between words to make them flow better when speaking.

steps.m: to be born, to be young

steps.n: Root letters got short 'a' vowels (fatha) to make the noun meaning 'child'
Changes to plural form showing many children

steps.m: the children (who are mentioned with المستضعفين/the weak ones)
steps.n: Added 'the' (ال) at the start to make it specific children, and changed the ending because it comes after the word 'of' (من)
This is a particle used to introduce a new clause. Here it introduces what should be done - taking care of orphans with justice.
و connects this to the previous statements about orphans, adding another aspect of caring for them
Added damma on first letter and connected letters to form basic command
Added ت at the beginning to show it's happening now or in the future
Added plural masculine ending وا to indicate multiple male subjects

steps.m: to lack something essential (like a parent)
an orphan (someone who lost their parent)
steps.n: Changed from root meaning 'to be orphaned' into noun meaning 'an orphan' by adding vowels
Changed the word to show many orphans instead of just one
Added ال to show we're talking about specific orphans
Added لِ to show the orphans are receiving something
Root letters turned into a noun meaning 'justice' by adding i-sound (kasra) at start and stopping sound (sukun) in middle
Added بِ at beginning to mean 'with' and kasra at end because it's followed by another word

steps.m: with the justice (how to treat يَتٰمَى/orphans)
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific justice, referring to how orphans should be treated fairly as mentioned in the ayah
Added fatha to show proper pronunciation

steps.m: and whatever (connects to يُتْلَىٰ - is recited)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it. This word connects to يُتْلَىٰ (is recited) in the ayah.
Added short 'a' vowels (fatha) to make it mean 'he did' in the past
Changed to present tense by adding ت at start and changing vowels
Added وا to show multiple people are doing the action
The وا was already there - this is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning

This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or what group it belongs to. In this ayah, it shows that the weak/oppressed ones (وَٱلْمُسْتَضْعَفِينَ) are from among the children (ٱلْوِلْدٰنِ).
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make it a noun referring to something good or beneficial
Added the tanwin kasra to show this is the object of the word مِنْ (from/of) in the phrase 'from any good deed'
This is a particle that emphasizes what comes after it. It's like adding 'indeed' or 'surely' in English.

steps.m: then surely (connects to previous statement about doing good deeds)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to connect this with the previous statement. It shows that this is a result or consequence of what was mentioned before.
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word that became the name for the One True God.
Added shadda on ل, changed initial alif to wasl form, and added fatha at end to show this is the object of إِنَّ (indeed/verily)

steps.m: he was (past tense verb in this ayah)
steps.n: Added fatha marks to make the basic past tense form. This shows the action happened in the past.
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a hollow verb (has و in middle). This is just for pronunciation and doesn't change the meaning.
Added kasra to show how the preposition is pronounced
Added the pronoun ه with kasra to mean 'him'
Added small ya (ۦ) after ه as a pronunciation guide to show it's pronounced 'bihi' not 'bih'

steps.m: to know deeply or be certain of something

steps.n: Added vowel marks to connect the root letters into a past tense verb
Added ي between second and third root letters to show someone who has this quality very strongly
Added ًا at the end to show this word is used as a description in the sentence
This is the basic conditional particle that shows something depends on something else
Added و at the beginning to connect this condition with what came before it
Root letters connected with vowels to make the basic word for 'person'
Added helper alif with kasra at start to make pronunciation easier
Added feminine marker ة to specify a female person
Changed initial alif to wasl form and added tanween to show this is indefinite and is the subject doing the action of fearing
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a hollow verb (has a weak middle letter)
Adding ت shows this is about a female (she). This word refers back to امْرَأَةٌ (a woman) mentioned earlier in the verse.
This is a preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. Here it connects to بَعْلِهَا (her husband) to show the fear is coming from him.
Added a small ن (called nun ghunna) at the end to help with pronunciation when connecting to the next word

steps.m: to be a husband or master
steps.n: The root refers to being a husband or having authority
Added kasra at the end to show this word is possessed by something/someone
Added ها to show the husband belongs to her (the wife mentioned earlier)
The root conveys the idea of rising or elevation
The pattern creates a noun describing the specific act of rebellion or defiance within a relationship context

steps.m: rebellion/defiance (that she feared from her husband)
steps.n: Added the indefinite accusative case ending ًا. This word نُشُوزًا is what the wife fears (object of خَافَتْ - she feared) from her husband (مِنۢ بَعْلِهَا).
This is a conjunction that gives a choice between alternatives. Here it connects two possible negative behaviors from the husband: 'ill-conduct' or 'turning away'
The root relates to showing or turning away
Added إِ at the beginning and ا after ر to make it into a noun meaning 'the act of doing something'

steps.m: turning away (object of خَافَتْ - she feared)
steps.n: Added tanween fatah (ًا) at the end to show this is what the wife feared from her husband
Added fatha to show proper pronunciation

steps.m: then not (connected to خَافَتْ - she feared)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from the previous action. This connects to the earlier word خَافَتْ (she feared), showing what happens after that fear.

steps.m: to lean or turn away from the right path
sin or blame (something that makes one turn from the right path)
steps.n: Changes the action of turning away into the concept of sin/blame that results from turning away

steps.m: sin or blame (object being negated by لا)
steps.n: Adds fatha at the end because this word is being negated by لا in the ayah, showing there is no sin/blame in this situation
This is the basic preposition meaning 'on' or 'upon'
The ى changes to يْ when connecting to pronouns. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning.
Added هِمَا at the end to show this applies to exactly two people
The alif is written with a special mark (آ) at the end of words. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning.

steps.m: that (introduces يُصْلِحَا - they both make peace)
steps.n: This is a helper word that connects two parts of the sentence. Here it introduces what the couple can do - they can make peace between themselves. It works like 'that' in English when you say 'I hope that you are well'.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make it a past tense verb
Added hamza at beginning to make the verb causative - showing someone making something happen
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Added ا at the end to show two people doing the action
This makes it a noun meaning 'between' or 'among'

steps.m: between them both (referring to the husband and wife mentioned in the ayah)
steps.n: Added هُمَا at the end to show this means 'between them both', specifically referring to the husband and wife mentioned earlier in the verse
Changed the action into a noun by adding damma (ُ) on first letter and sukun (ْ) on middle letter

steps.m: a peace, a reconciliation (object of يُصْلِحَا - they both make)
steps.n: Added tanween alif (ًا) to show this is the object being made in the phrase 'they both make peace'
Changed the verb into a noun meaning the result of making peace
Added ال to make it specific and ُ to show it's the subject of خَيْرٌ (is better). Also doubled the ص because it comes after ال.
Added و at the beginning to connect this with what came before. The ا in ال changes to ٱ after و.
Root letters got vowels to make the word meaning 'good' or 'better'
Added double-dammah to make it indefinite and show it's the subject
Changes to passive voice - something was done to the subject
Added ت to show this refers to a feminine subject (souls/الْأَنفُسُ which is feminine in Arabic)
Adds conjunction prefix و meaning 'and'

steps.m: to breathe, be precious, be valuable

steps.n: Changes from root meaning to concrete noun meaning 'soul/self'
Changes from singular to plural by adding أ at the start and changing the vowels - a common plural pattern in Arabic

steps.m: the souls (subject of أُحْضِرَتِ - was made present)
steps.n: ٱلْ makes it specific/definite, and ُ shows it's the subject doing the action

steps.m: to withhold, be stingy or greedy

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
Changed the verb into a noun showing the quality of greed with extra intensity
Added the definite article to specify this particular greed

steps.m: the intense greed/stinginess (as object of أُحْضِرَتِ - was made present)
steps.n: Added the accusative case ending because this word is the object of the verb أُحْضِرَتِ (was made present)
Compound particle combining 'and' with conditional 'if'
Added vowels to make the basic past tense verb
تُ at start shows 'you' are doing it, and the word changed to mean 'make something good' instead of just 'being good'
The وا shows that multiple people are being addressed
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Added تَ at start to mean 'you', and doubled the ت to mean 'protect yourself' instead of just 'protect'
Changed the ending to show multiple people are doing the action

steps.m: and you all protect yourselves (connected to تُحْسِنُوا۟ - you do good)
steps.n: Added 'and' to connect this with the previous word تُحْسِنُوا۟ (you do good). The small circle at the end (۟) helps with pronunciation.
Basic emphatic particle that adds emphasis to a statement, like adding 'indeed' or 'certainly' in English

steps.m: then indeed (connects to تُحْسِنُوا۟ وَتَتَّقُوا۟ - if you do good and fear Allah)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result of the previous statement. Here it connects to the condition 'if you do good and fear Allah' mentioned before.
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word in Arabic that always refers to The One True God.

steps.m: Allah (as the object being described as خَبِيرًا/All-Aware)
steps.n: Added the fatha at the end to show Allah is being described. Also added shadda on ل and changed initial alif to wasl form since it comes after another word.
Added basic past tense pattern with fathas
Changed to hollow verb form with alif
This is a relative pronoun that means 'what' or 'that which'. In this ayah, it refers to the actions being done (تَعْمَلُونَ - you do/perform).
Added the preposition بِ (with/by) to show how Allah is aware of or knowledgeable about the actions. This connects to خَبِيرًا (All-Aware) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Added تَ at start to show this is happening now and being done by 'you'. The sukun on ع shows it's happening now.
The ون at the end shows this is being done by a group of people (you all)

steps.m: to be aware of something deeply

steps.n: Adding the vowels and ي makes this describe someone who has this quality strongly
Adding ًا (an) at the end makes this word an adjective describing Allah, showing He is the All-Aware One
This is a negative particle that strongly denies something happening in the future. In this ayah, it's used with تَسْتَطِيعُوٓا۟ (you will be able) to say 'you will never be able'.
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it. The و here means 'and'.
Added تَسْ at the beginning to mean 'seeking to do something', changed the root letters to show 'you can/are able to' in the present tense
Added وا at the end to show this refers to a group of people

steps.m: that (connects to تَسْتَطِيعُوٓا۟ - you are able)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that introduces what someone is/isn't able to do. Here it connects 'you are not able' (تَسْتَطِيعُوٓا۟) with 'to be just' (تَعْدِلُوا۟), showing what they aren't able to do.

steps.m: to act justly, to be balanced

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, changing vowels, and adding ُ at end
Changed from 'he' to 'you' by replacing يَ with تَ at the beginning
Added وا۟ to show multiple people are doing the action. This is the subjunctive form after أَن (that/to)

steps.m: to be between or separate things

steps.n: Adding vowels changes this from a root into a usable word meaning 'between'
Root meaning 'female' becomes plural noun meaning 'women' - like changing 'woman' to 'women'

steps.m: these ones/people (in this ayah, referring to a specific group being discussed)

Combines the conjunction 'and' with the relative pronoun 'whoever'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed the vowels to make it mean 'cause someone to do the action' instead of just doing it

steps.m: he leads astray (subject of Allah in this ayah)
steps.n: Added يُ at the beginning to show it's happening now or in the future. The ِ at the end shows this is in a special grammar position after مَن (whoever).
Added ال to specify this is THE one true God, and the hamza was dropped because this is a special divine name

steps.m: Allah (the subject of يُضْلِلِ - the one who misguides)
steps.n: Added shadda to emphasize the ل, changed initial alif to wasl form, and added damma to show this is the doer of the action in this sentence
This is a negative particle that makes the verb after it negative in the future tense. Here it negates يُضْلِلِ (mislead).
Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result or consequence of what came before
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb
Changed to present tense form with ت prefix for 'you'. The فَلَن before it makes it mean 'will never find'
Added fatha to show this is a preposition meaning 'for' or 'to'
Added the pronoun هُ meaning 'him' to show who the preposition is directed to
Added the small و mark (ۥ) which is just a pronunciation helper that doesn't change the meaning

steps.m: to make a path or way

steps.n: Adding ي and vowels changes it from verb to noun meaning 'path'

steps.m: a path/way (object of تَجِدَ/you will find)
steps.n: Added ًا to show this is the object being found in the phrase 'you will not find for him a path'

steps.m: to point to or direct attention to something

steps.n: The fatha shows this is being used to call attention
The damma shows this word is in the nominative case
Added the calling particle يٰٓ to show we're addressing someone
Added ها to make it address a group of people
This is a special demonstrative pronoun used to connect descriptions to people or things
Adding long alif at start makes it mean 'to make someone do the action'
Added وا at the end to show many people did this action. This word is connected to لَا تَتَّخِذُوا۟ (do not take) in the ayah - it tells us who is being told not to take the disbelievers as allies.
This is a negative particle that comes before verbs to negate them. Here it negates the command 'take/adopt', telling believers not to take disbelievers as allies.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form the basic past tense verb
Added اِتَّ at the beginning to show someone took something for their own benefit or purpose
Changed to present tense with 'you' as subject by adding تَ at start and changing vowels
Added وا at the end to show multiple people are being addressed

steps.m: to reject or cover up truth

steps.n: Added vowels to make it a past tense verb
Changed to describe someone who does the action of rejecting truth

steps.m: those who reject/cover up truth (acted upon)
steps.n: Makes the word plural and shows it's being acted upon in the phrase 'do not take the disbelievers as allies'
Adds 'the' to specify these particular disbelievers and adds final vowel mark to show it's being acted upon

steps.m: to protect, be close to, or support

steps.n: Adding vowel marks changes the root into a past tense verb meaning 'to protect'
Changed from single protector/friend to multiple protectors by making it plural (like going from 'friend' to 'friends')

steps.m: protectors, close supporters (as object of لَا تَتَّخِذُوا - do not take)
steps.n: Added fatha at end to show this is the object of the verb 'do not take' (لَا تَتَّخِذُوا)
Changed the long alif (آ) to regular alif (ا) - both words mean the same thing and are pronounced similarly
Basic form of the preposition that shows where something starts or comes from
Added kasra (ِ) under م to show how to pronounce it correctly. In this ayah, it comes before the word دُونِ to show separation or distance from the believers.
Connected the root letters that express the basic concept of being lower or beneath something
Added damma (ُ) to make it describe something that is lower or beneath
Added kasra (ِ) to show this word connects to 'the believers' (الْمُؤْمِنِينَ) that comes after it
Changed the verb to show believing in something or someone, like changing 'trust' to 'have faith in'
Changed from an action (believing) to describing someone who does that action (believer)
Added ين at the end to show we're talking about many male believers
Added ٱلْ at the beginning to show we're talking about specific believers. This word is being acted upon by the verb لَا تَتَّخِذُوا۟ (do not take) in this ayah.
Added vowels to make it mean 'he wanted' in the past tense
Added تُ at start to show 'you' and changed vowels to show present tense
Added ون at the end to show multiple people are being addressed
Added أَ at start to make it a question
Basic form of the particle that connects to and introduces the verb تَجْعَلُوا۟ (you make/establish)
Added fatha mark to show correct pronunciation

steps.m: to make something into something else, to put, to set

steps.n: The ت at the beginning shows this is addressing 'you' in the present tense
Adding vowel marks to show how to pronounce the word correctly
Added وا with ُ before it to show this is addressing multiple people
This is the base word Allah, with shadda on ل to show emphasis
Added لِ at beginning to show direction/purpose and ِ at end to connect it to what comes after
This is a basic preposition showing something is above or over something else
The ى changes to ي before adding pronouns
The كُمْ shows this affects multiple people being spoken to
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed from a verb (he dominated) to a noun (authority) by changing the vowels inside the word
Added ان at the end makes it mean 'strong/clear authority' - like adding 'very' in English
Added tanwin alif (ًا) to show this is the object being made/placed in the sentence
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added مُ at start and changed vowels to make it describe who/what does the action
Added emphasis to the م with a shadda
Added tanween alif to show this word is receiving the action of تَجْعَلُوا (you make/place)
This is a special word that makes what comes after it stronger or more certain. It makes the next word ٱلْمُنٰفِقِينَ (the hypocrites) take a special ending to show it's being emphasized.

steps.m: to pass through with hidden intent

steps.n: Adding alif shows the action is done with intent to deceive others
Adding مُ at start and changing vowels shows who does the action
Adding ين makes it plural, showing many people who act hypocritically
Adding ٱلْ makes it specific. The word is the subject of إِنَّ (indeed/verily), which is why it ends with َ
Basic preposition showing location or being inside something. Here it connects 'the hypocrites' to 'the lowest depths'
The ي changes to ى when it comes at the end of the word. This doesn't change the meaning.
Added vowel marks to form a noun meaning a level or depth. The sukun (ْ) shows where one syllable ends and another begins.
Added ال to make it specific and ِ at the end because it's connected to the next word (lowest). The shadda (ّ) makes the د sound stronger.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
Added أَ at start and changed vowels to make it mean 'most/lowest'
Added ٱلْ to show we're talking about a specific lowest thing
Added kasra at the end because this word is describing الدَّرْكِ (the level/bottom) in the ayah
Basic preposition showing where something comes from or what it belongs to
Added fatha at the end to connect it smoothly to the next word النَّارِ (the Fire). Together they mean 'from the Fire'
Root meaning 'light/fire' became the noun 'fire'. The middle letter و changed to ا to make it easier to say.
Added ال to make it specific. The ن gets a shadda when it comes after ال.
Added kasra at the end because it comes after the word مِنَ (from/of) in the ayah
This is a special word that makes something definitely not happen in the future. It's like saying 'absolutely won't' in English.

steps.m: and will never (connects to تَجِدَ - you will find)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it. The word تَجِدَ (you will find) comes after it to complete the meaning.

steps.m: to find or come across something

steps.n: Connected the root letters and added fathas to show this happened in the past
Changed to present/future tense by: 1) adding يَ at start, 2) changing middle vowel to ِ

steps.m: you will find (addressing Prophet Muhammad ﷺ)
steps.n: Changed from 'he' to 'you' by replacing يَ with تَ at the start. In this ayah, Allah is telling Prophet Muhammad ﷺ that he will not find helpers for the hypocrites.
Added fatha to help with pronunciation
Added هُ which means 'him/it'
Changed from singular 'him' to plural 'them' by adding م with sukun
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed from verb 'helped' to noun meaning 'one who helps a lot'

steps.m: a helper (object of تَجِدَ - you will find)
steps.n: Added tanween fatha to show this is the object being found in the phrase 'you will not find for them a helper'
This is a basic exception word that shows something or someone is not included in what was mentioned before. It's like saying "but not" in English.

Added the long alif (ا) at the end which helps extend the sound. In this ayah, it introduces an exception to those who repented (ٱلَّذِينَ تَابُوا۟), showing that they are not included in the previous judgment.
Base form of the relative pronoun that connects descriptions to people or things
Added ين to make it refer to multiple people or things
Added ٱلْ with shadda on lam to make it specific and added fatha at end to show it's connected to the verb تَابُوا (they repented) that comes after it

steps.m: to return back (from wrong to right)
he returned back (from wrong to right)
steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and changed و to ا because it's in past tense. This shows one person did the action in the past.

steps.m: they returned back (from wrong to right)
steps.n: Adding وا shows that a group of people did the action together. The ُ before وا helps connect it smoothly.
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters and make it a past tense verb
Adding أ at the start makes the verb mean 'to make something' do the action. This is a verb, so it doesn't have case endings like nouns do.
Adding وا indicates plural masculine subject (they)
Connected to the previous word تَابُوا۟ (they repented) with 'and'
Added vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added اِعْتَ to show doing the action strongly and deliberately for oneself, like grabbing and holding on tight
Added وا to show many people did the action

steps.m: and they held firmly (to بِٱللَّهِ - to Allah)
steps.n: Added وَ meaning 'and' at the beginning. This word connects to بِٱللَّهِ (to Allah) which comes after it in the ayah.
This is the base word 'Allah', which is a proper name and doesn't come from a typical root pattern
Added shadda on ل to show it's pronounced strongly
Added بِ at start to mean 'by/with' and ِ at end to connect it to the previous verb 'held fast to'
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Adding أ at the beginning makes the verb transitive - now someone is making something else pure (in this case, their religion)
Adding وا changes 'he' to 'they' (masculine plural)
Adding و connects this word to the previous actions in the verse. The word دِينَهُمْ (their religion) is what they made pure.
Root letters formed into a noun meaning 'way of life', with kasra and fatha vowels
Added هُمْ to show it belongs to them. This word is what they made sincere/pure (أَخْلَصُوا۟) for Allah.
Added shadda on ل to show emphasis in pronunciation of Allah's name
Added لِ at beginning to show this is being done for Allah or belongs to Allah. The ا from الله is removed when لِ is added.

steps.m: for/to Allah (connected to دِينَهُمْ - their religion)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show this connects to the word دِينَهُمْ (their religion) in the ayah, indicating they made their religion purely for Allah
This is the basic demonstrative word that points to people or things. It's used when pointing to multiple things or people.
Adding ئِ shows we're pointing to something far away rather than close by
Adding كَ makes it point to specific people or things that were just mentioned
Adding فَ at the beginning shows this follows from what came before - in this case, it refers to the people who repented and reformed
The small changes (۟ and ٰٓ) are just pronunciation helpers and don't change the meaning
This word shows that the repentant ones (ٱلَّذِينَ) are together with the believers (ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ). It's a particle that connects two groups.
Adding مُ at the start and changing the middle to ؤْ creates a word for someone who does the action
Adding ين makes the word refer to many believers
Adding ال makes it specific believers, like saying "the" in English

steps.m: the believers (as the object being acted upon)
steps.n: The َ at the end shows this word is receiving the action in the sentence
This word shows that something will definitely happen in the future. It's stronger and more certain than just س at the beginning of a word.

steps.m: and will (connects to يُؤْتِ - will give)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it, showing that this future action follows after the previous actions mentioned.

steps.m: to give, bring, or come with something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed from past tense (أَتَى) to present tense (يُؤْتِي) by adding يُ at the start. The middle letter changed to make it easier to say.
Final ي drops because it's a weak letter at the end. This word is what Allah (اللَّهُ) does - He gives.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
Added ال to specify the One True God, doubled the ل with shadda, and added ُ to show this is the subject doing the giving (يُؤْتِ) in this ayah
Added vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he believed' to 'one who believes' - like changing from a verb to a person
Adding the masculine plural suffix ين to indicate multiple male believers
Makes the noun definite and shows these are the specific believers mentioned with those who repented (تَابُوا۟)
Changed from basic root letters to a noun meaning 'payment' by adding vowel marks

steps.m: a reward (object of يُؤْتِ - will give)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the object receiving the action of 'giving' in the ayah
Added vowels to make basic past tense verb
Changes from 'became great' (verb) to 'great' (describing word)
The word gets an -an ending to match and describe the word 'reward' (أَجْرًا) that comes before it

Added shadda (doubling mark) on م for emphasis. This is common when ما comes after certain words or at the start of a sentence.
