steps.m: a deity, god, or object of worship
steps.n: Changed the concept of worship into a noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'

Added ال to specify THE one true deity, doubled the ل with shadda, and added ُ to show this is the subject of the sentence. The hamza from إله is dropped when ال is added. This is the proper name for The One True God in Islam.
Added vowels to connect the root letters together and make a past tense verb
Adding an alif between the second and third root letters turns this into a noun describing the result of the action
Added بِ (bi) at the start to mean 'with/by' the punishment

steps.m: by/with your (plural) punishment (referring to the punishment of all those being addressed)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show the punishment belongs to multiple people being spoken to

steps.m: if (introduces the condition شَكَرْتُمْ وَءَامَنتُمْ - if you are grateful and believe)
steps.n: This is a conditional particle that introduces a condition - it tells us that something will happen only if something else happens first. In this ayah, it connects Allah's response to people's gratitude and belief.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense form
Added sukun to prepare for adding the ending

steps.m: you all thanked (connected to وَءَامَنتُمْ - 'and you believed')
steps.n: Added تُمْ at the end to show that many people are being spoken to. This word is connected to وَءَامَنتُمْ (and you believed) in the ayah.
Added alif after first letter to show causing someone to do something (in this case, causing trust to happen = believing)
Changes from 'he believed' to 'you all believed'

steps.m: and you all believed/trusted (connected to شَكَرْتُمْ - you were grateful)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) to connect this with the previous word شَكَرْتُمْ (you were grateful)
Added fathas to show past tense. The و changes to ا because it's between two fathas.
Added و at the beginning to connect this to the previous phrase about being grateful and believing
Changed the concept of worship into a noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'

steps.m: Allah (the One True God) (subject of يَفْعَلُ)
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific and unique, doubled the ل with shadda, and added ُ to show this is the doer of the action in يَفْعَلُ (does/will do). This is the proper name for the One True God in Islam.
Changes from action to describing someone who does the action. This word describes Allah (اللَّهُ) in this ayah.
The ًا ending shows this word describes how Allah is after the word كَانَ (was/is). It's like saying 'Allah is appreciative' in English.
Added ي between first and second root letters with vowels to show this is a permanent, strong quality
Added tanween alif to show this is describing Allah in this sentence
This is a negative particle that tells us Allah does not love something
The shadda (doubled L) makes the negation stronger, like saying 'definitely not' instead of just 'not'
The root letters tell us this is about loving or liking something
Added vowel marks (fatha) to make the root letters into 'he loved' in past tense
When two same letters come together, we combine them with a shadda
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels. This word describes what Allah does in this ayah.
Changed the concept of worship into a noun meaning 'a deity'
This is the unique name for the One True God in Islam. The ال makes it specific, and the doubled ل shows the special importance. The ُ shows this is the subject of يُحِبُّ (loves/likes) in this ayah.
The root tells us this is about speaking out loud or making something public

steps.m: speaking openly/loudly (object of يُحِبُّ - does not love)
steps.n: Changed into a noun meaning 'open/loud speech'. This word is what Allah does not love in this ayah.
Adds ٱلْ to make it specific and َ because it's the object of يُحِبُّ (Allah does not love). This refers specifically to speaking evil about others openly.
The root tells us this is about something bad or evil
Changed from a verb meaning 'to do evil' into a noun meaning 'evil itself' by adding damma (ُ)
Added ٱل to specify we're talking about a particular evil
Added long vowel mark (ٓ) to emphasize the word
Added بِ at start to show 'with' and ِ at end because it follows يُحِبُّ (He does not love)
This is the basic form of the preposition that shows where something comes from or what it belongs to
Added fatha at the end to help connect it smoothly to the next word ٱلْقَوْلِ (speech/words). This combination means 'from the speech/words'
The root tells us this is about speaking or saying something
Changed the verb into a noun. This word follows مِنَ (from) in the ayah, showing what kind of thing is being talked about.
The kasra (ِ) shows this word comes after مِنَ (from)

steps.m: the speech (object of مِنَ in ٱلْجَهْرَ بِٱلسُّوٓءِ مِنَ ٱلْقَوْلِ - public speaking of evil speech)
steps.n: Added ال to show we're talking about specific speech. This is part of the phrase 'public speaking of evil speech'
This is a basic negative particle. Starting with the simplest form since it's not derived from a root.

steps.m: except (connects لَّا يُحِبُّ with مَن ظُلِمَ)
steps.n: Adding إِ at the beginning and a shadda changes this from a negative particle to an exception particle. In this ayah, it connects what Allah doesn't like (speaking evil) with the exception (those who were wronged).
This is a special word that refers to people - like saying 'who' or 'whoever'. In this ayah, it refers to the person who was wronged (ظُلِمَ means 'was wronged').
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: he was wronged (exception to the rule about speaking evil in this ayah)
steps.n: Changed the vowels to show that someone else did the wronging to him. This word is mentioned as an exception in the ayah, showing when it's okay to speak about evil - when you've been wronged.
The root tells us this is about being or existing
Added fathas to show this happened in the past. The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak letter in the middle of the word. This word describes Allah (اللَّهُ) in this ayah.
Added وَ (and) at the beginning to connect this to the previous statement
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity'
Added 'the' to make it specific/definite
Added damma to show this is the subject
The root tells us this is about hearing or listening
Connected the letters together with vowel marks to make it a past tense verb
Adding ي between second and third letters makes it mean someone who does this action perfectly

steps.m: All-Hearing (as a divine attribute of اللَّهُ)
steps.n: Adding ًا (tanween fatha) at the end shows this word is describing how Allah was - like saying 'Allah was All-Hearing'
The root tells us this is about knowing or having knowledge
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past-tense verb
The pattern with ي in the middle shows this is a permanent, intense quality (like the difference between 'knows' and 'All-Knowing')

steps.m: All-Knowing (describing Allah in وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ سَمِيعًا عَلِيمًا)
steps.n: The ًا ending shows this word is describing Allah in the phrase 'and Allah is All-Hearing, All-Knowing'
This is a conditional word that means 'if' - it introduces the first part of an if-then statement. Here it starts the condition 'if you disclose...'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Added تُ at start to make it 'you' and changed middle vowel to ِ (kasra) to make it present tense
Added وا at the end to show that multiple people are doing the action
Added vowel marks to the root letters to make a noun meaning goodness or better/best

steps.m: a good thing (object of تُبْدُوا - you display)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this is receiving the action of displaying/revealing
This word connects two choices in this ayah: showing something openly OR hiding it. It's like when you say 'Would you like an apple OR an orange?'
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make a past tense verb
Added تُ at start to show 'you' and changed vowels to show present tense
Added و to show multiple people doing the action
Added هُ at the end to show what is being hidden. This matches with تُبْدُوا (you reveal) in the ayah.
This is a connecting word that gives a choice between two options: showing something openly (تُبْدُوا) or hiding it (تُخْفُوهُ)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks. The final و changes to ا because it's a weak letter at the end.
Added ت at beginning for 'you' and changed vowels for present tense. The final و is the original root letter returning.
Added ا with small و above it to show this is addressing multiple people
This is a preposition that shows either physical distance from something or talking about a topic. In this ayah, it connects to سُوٓءٍ (evil/bad) to mean 'from evil'
The fatha (َ) on عَ shows this word is complete and ready to connect to the next word. It's like adding a space in English before the next word.
Changed from a root meaning 'to harm' into a noun meaning 'harm' by adding damma (u sound) in the middle
Added a long vowel mark (alif) after و to show the extended sound
Added tanwin kasra at the end to show this is any general harm or trouble
This is a basic emphasis word that makes statements stronger, like adding 'surely' in English
Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result of what came before. This word connects to عَفُوًّا (Most Pardoning) in the ayah, showing Allah's attribute as a result.
Added ال to specify this is THE one true God, and the hamza was dropped because this is a proper name. The ل is doubled with a shadda.

steps.m: Allah (as the object being described as عَفُوًّا قَدِيرًا - pardoning and capable)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show Allah is being described. The alif at the start changed to a wasl form since it connects to the previous word. The ل has a shadda showing it's doubled.
Added basic past tense pattern with fatha vowels
Changed و to ا because it's a hollow verb (has weak middle letter)
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels
The word changed from a verb (he was forgiving) to a special form that means 'very forgiving' by adding ًّا at the end - this form is often used to describe Allah's perfect qualities
Connected the root letters with fatha to create basic past tense verb
Creates an intensive adjective showing Allah's complete power and capability

steps.m: very powerful/capable (describing that Allah was this way)
steps.n: Adds tanween to show this describes the state Allah was in (after كَانَ)

This is a special word called a particle that adds emphasis to what comes after it. It's like adding 'definitely' or 'truly' at the start of a sentence in English. In this ayah, it emphasizes the description of those who disbelieve that follows.
This is a special word that points to things. Adding ي makes it connect descriptions to what's being described.

steps.m: the one who/which (points to a specific thing)
steps.n: Adding ٱلَّ makes it point to something specific instead of just any thing.

steps.m: those who (subject of يَكْفُرُونَ - they disbelieve)
steps.n: Adding ينَ makes it talk about many people instead of just one. This word points to the people who are doing the disbelieving mentioned right after it.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: they reject/deny (the truth about اللَّهِ and His messengers)
steps.n: Added ون at the end to show multiple people are doing this. This word connects to بِٱللَّهِ (about Allah) in the ayah.
This is the unique proper name of God in Islam. While it comes from the root meaning 'divine/worship', it is treated as a distinct proper noun.
Added shadda on ل to show it is doubled in pronunciation
Added kasra at the end to show possession or connection

Added بِ at beginning to show how something is done or the means by which it happens. Also added hamza-alif (ٱ) which is a special way of writing the definite article ال when it comes after certain letters like بِ.
Changed vowels to create plural form for people who deliver messages
Added kasra at the end to show this word belongs to something
Added هِ at the end to show these are Allah's messengers
Added وَ at beginning to connect this word with what came before it (Allah)
This is the basic concept of wanting or seeking something
Connected the root letters together. The و becomes ي because it's between two kasras
Added يُ at the beginning to show present tense action
Added ون to show multiple people (men) doing the action
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this with the previous statement
This is a particle that connects يُرِيدُونَ (they want) to what comes after it. It works like 'to' in English phrases like 'they want to do something'.

steps.m: to separate or make a distinction between things

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make basic past tense verb
Adding shadda shows doing the action with more force or multiple times
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Added plural ending to show multiple people doing the action. This word describes what the disbelievers try to do between Allah and His messengers.
Changes from verb 'to separate' into noun meaning 'between'
Added fatha at the end because this word is the object of the verb يُفَرِّقُوا (they differentiate/make distinction)
Added shadda to emphasize the L sound

steps.m: of Allah (object of يَكْفُرُونَ - they disbelieve)
steps.n: Added a small i-sound at the end to show Allah is what they disbelieve in
This pattern is used for someone who does something a lot - in this case, someone who delivers messages
Changes the word from singular (one messenger) to plural (many messengers) by changing the vowels
Adding conjunction و (waw) to connect this word with previous phrase
Adding kasra at end to show this word is connected to something after it
Adding هِۦ to show these are Allah's messengers
Added vowels to make the basic past tense form. The middle و changes to ا in this pattern.
Changes from past tense (he said) to present tense (he says) by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowel
Adding ون shows that multiple people (all male or mixed group) are doing the action
Adding و at the start connects this word to what came before it, like saying 'and'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Adding alif after first letter changes meaning from just feeling secure to actively believing or having faith

steps.m: we believe (in response to those who say they believe in some messengers but not others)
steps.n: Changed to present tense 'we' form by adding نُ at start and changing vowels. The ؤ replaces آ in present tense.

steps.m: to divide or separate into parts

steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowel marks to create the noun meaning 'some' or 'part'

steps.m: with/by some (object of نُؤْمِنُ - we believe)
steps.n: Added the preposition بِ to show this is what we believe in. This connects to the verb نُؤْمِنُ (we believe) in the ayah.
Added tanween kasra (ٍ) to show this is indefinite and is affected by the preposition بِ

steps.m: to reject, deny, or be ungrateful

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
Removed first vowel 'a' and changed middle 'a' to 'u' to make present tense base
Added نَ at beginning to show 'we' are doing the action

steps.m: and we reject/deny (connected to نُؤْمِنُ - we believe)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect this with the previous statement about believing
This word means a part or portion of something
Added vowels to make it a noun meaning 'a part' or 'portion'
Added the -in ending to show this word is being acted upon by another word

steps.m: in/with some (object of نُؤْمِنُ - we believe)
steps.n: Added بِ at beginning to show this is what they believe in. This connects to the verb نُؤْمِنُ (we believe)
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Added يُ at the start to make it present tense. The middle letter و becomes ي because it's between two vowels. The vowels change to match the present tense pattern.
Added ون suffix to show multiple people (they) are doing the action

steps.m: and they want/desire (connected to يَكْفُرُونَ - they disbelieve)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect this action with the previous action (disbelieving) in the verse
This is a joining word that connects two verbs. Here it connects 'they want' (يُرِيدُونَ) with 'they separate' (يُفَرِّقُوا), showing what they want to do.
Connected the root letters together
Added ا at start and ت that combines with خ to make تّ. This shows doing something for yourself.
Changed from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels - shows ongoing action
Added وا at the end to show many people are doing it
Starting with the basic root letters that carry the concept of separation or distinction
Added vowel marks to change this from a verb into a preposition. In this ayah, it shows the space or relationship between Allah and His messengers that some people try to divide.
Basic demonstrative pronoun for pointing to something nearby
The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) to show elongation
Adding لِ changes it from pointing to something near to something farther away

steps.m: that (pointing to something not very close)
steps.n: The كَ at the end shows we are pointing to something that is not very close but also not very far away

steps.m: to travel or follow a path

steps.n: Changes from basic root to noun meaning 'path'
Added tanween alif to show this is the object being taken/chosen in the phrase 'they want to take a path'
This is the basic form of the word used to point to things or people
Added ئِ to show we're talking about multiple people or things

steps.m: those (pointing to people far away) (subject of هُمُ)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show we're pointing to something far away. This word is the subject that هُمُ (they) refers back to in this ayah.
This is the basic form of the third person plural masculine pronoun, used to refer to a group of people (all male or mixed gender)
Added damma vowel marks (ُ) to help with pronunciation. The final damma shows this is connected to the next word ٱلْكٰفِرُونَ (the disbelievers).

steps.m: to reject or cover up truth

steps.n: Added vowels to make it a past tense verb
Changed the word to show who does the action
Added ون to show it's talking about many people
Added ال to point to specific rejecters. This word follows هُمُ (they are) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
The second and third root letters combine with shadda to make a noun meaning the essence of being true
Adding tanween alif (ًا) changes the noun into an adverb that describes how something is done
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Added hamza with fatha at start and sukun on first root letter to make the verb causative
Added نا at the end to show 'we' as the subject, and added sukun before it. This word connects to عَذَابًا (punishment) which is what was prepared.
Added و at start to connect this clause with what came before

steps.m: to reject or cover up truth

steps.n: The root letters got vowels added to make a past tense verb meaning 'he rejected'. Like changing 'reject' to 'rejected' in English.
Changed from a verb (rejected) to a noun meaning 'rejecter' by adding ا after first letter
Makes the word plural to refer to multiple people who reject
Makes the word definite by adding 'the'
Added لِ (li) at the beginning to mean 'for' or 'to' the disbelievers
Transforms root into noun indicating intense punishment
Shows this is the object of the verb أَعْتَدْنَا (We have prepared) in this ayah
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a past tense verb
Added مُ at start to make it mean 'one who does something' and changed vowels to make it humiliate others instead of being humiliated. The و changed to ي because of grammar rules.
Added emphasis by doubling the م with a shadda
Added ًا at the end to show this word describes the punishment (عَذَابًا) mentioned in the ayah
This is a special word used to point to things. It doesn't come from a normal root.
Adding ال and ي changes this from just pointing to something to describing who or what we're talking about
Adding ن makes it refer to multiple people or things instead of just one
Added و at the start to mean 'and'. This word connects to ءَامَنُوا۟ (they believed) in the ayah.
This is the basic meaning of the root
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
Adding long alif at start changes meaning to 'make someone do the action'
Added وا to show multiple people did the action. This word is the subject of يُؤْتِيهِمْ (He will give them) in this ayah.
This is the unique proper name of God in Arabic. It comes from the root meaning of divinity but is used exclusively as God's name.
Added بِ at the beginning to show how someone relates to Allah, and ِ at the end to connect it to the rest of the sentence. The ٱ is just a different way to write the same ال sound.
This is the basic meaning of the root
The و makes it mean someone who does this action as their main job/role
Changes from singular 'messenger' to plural 'messengers' using the broken plural pattern

steps.m: and His messengers (referring to Allah's messengers)
steps.n: Adds هِ to show these are specifically Allah's messengers, mentioned in relation to believing in Allah (بِٱللَّهِ) earlier in the verse
This word is used to say something didn't happen in the past
The و at the start connects this word to what came before. This word negates the verb يُفَرِّقُوا۟ (they differentiate) that comes after it, making it mean 'they did not differentiate'
This is the basic meaning of the root
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
Doubling the middle letter shows doing something with more force or repeatedly
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: they separate thoroughly (after لَمْ in this ayah)
steps.n: Added وا to show multiple people doing the action. The word comes after لَمْ (not) in this ayah, making it mean 'they did not separate'
This is the basic meaning of the root
Adding vowels changes it from a root into a usable word meaning 'between'
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic word meaning 'one' or 'anyone'

steps.m: anyone (being described by مِّنْهُمْ - from among them)
steps.n: Added tanwin kasra to show this word is indefinite and is being described by the phrase 'from among them' that follows it
This is a basic word that shows something comes from or originates from somewhere else, like saying 'from' in English
Added emphasis mark (shadda) to strengthen the 'm' sound
Added هُمْ at the end to show this applies to 'them' (a group of people)
This is the base form of the word used to point to multiple people or things
Adding ئِ shows that what we're pointing to is far away

steps.m: those (pointing to the believers mentioned before)
steps.n: Adding كَ makes it specific to what was just mentioned - in this case, the believers who didn't discriminate between any of Allah's messengers

This is a particle that tells us something will definitely happen in the future. It's stronger and more certain than just using سَ. Here it's telling us that Allah will definitely give them their rewards.
This is the basic meaning of the root
Connected the root letters together to form the basic verb
Added يُ at the beginning to show present/future tense and 'he' as the doer

steps.m: he gives them (سَوْفَ will give them their rewards)
steps.n: Added هِمْ at the end to show who receives the action (them). This connects to سَوْفَ (will) and أُجُورَهُمْ (their rewards) in the ayah.
This root relates to giving payment or reward for work or service
Changed the root into a noun meaning the reward itself
Added damma marks and و to make it plural
Added fatha to show this is the object (thing being given) in the phrase 'He will give them their rewards'
Added هُمْ at the end to show these are their rewards
This is the basic meaning of the root
Added fatha marks and changed و to ا because it's a hollow verb (has a weak middle letter). This makes it past tense.

steps.m: and he was (connects to غَفُورًا رَّحِيمًا - forgiving and merciful)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to mean 'and'. This connects 'he was' to the following description 'forgiving and merciful' in the ayah.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
This is the unique name for the one true God in Islam. The ال makes it specific, and the doubled ل shows the special importance. The ُ shows this is the subject doing something in the sentence.
This is the basic meaning of the root

steps.m: one who forgives a lot (describing اللَّهُ)
steps.n: Added و and vowels to show this describes someone who does a lot of forgiving. This word describes Allah in this ayah.
The ًا ending makes this word describe 'how' something is (like an adverb), showing Allah's forgiving nature
This is the basic meaning of the root
Adding ي between the second and third letters with vowels makes this describe someone who has this quality very strongly
The shadda (ّ) at the start makes the meaning even stronger

steps.m: extremely merciful (describing Allah in this ayah)
steps.n: The ًا (tanween) ending shows this word is being used as an adjective/description in this sentence
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, sukun on س, and ُ at end. This shows the action is happening now.

steps.m: he asks you (subject of أَهْلُ ٱلْكِتٰبِ)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show who is being asked. In this ayah, the People of the Book (أَهْلُ ٱلْكِتٰبِ) are doing the asking.
Changes from root to common noun meaning 'family/people'
Added ُ to show this word is doing the action of asking (يَسْـَٔلُ) in the verse
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form the basic past tense verb
Changed vowel pattern and added alif to create noun for the result of writing
Added ال to make it specific (used here to refer to scripture)
Added kasra at the end to show it's possessed by the word أَهْلُ (people), forming 'people of the book'

steps.m: that (introduces what the People of the Book are asking for)
steps.n: This is a particle that connects يَسْـَٔلُكَ (they ask you) to what follows. It shows that what comes after is the thing being asked for.
Basic meaning of descent or downward movement
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
The doubled middle letter shows the action happens multiple times or with extra effort
Added تُ at start to show 'you' as subject, changed vowels to show present tense, and added َ at end to show subjunctive mood (because it follows أَن)
This is a compound preposition made up of على (upon) and هم (them)
Changes from verb 'to write' into noun 'book'
Adds tanween fatHa to show this is the object of the sentence
This is the basic preposition that shows where something comes from or starts.
Added fatha at the end to connect it with the next word السَّمَآءِ (the sky/heaven). This shows it's part of a phrase meaning 'from the sky/heaven'.
The م gets doubled (shadda) because it comes after a word ending in ن, which is a common pronunciation rule in Arabic
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changed the word into a noun by adding a long alif and changing the final و to ء. This creates a noun for the space that is 'elevated' above us.
Added ال (with ٱ due to connecting to previous word) to make it specific, and doubled the س with shadda because it comes after ال
Added kasra at the end because it comes after مِنَ (from/of). The alif is written as آ when it comes before hamza with kasra.
This is a basic particle that adds emphasis and certainty to what follows
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from and connects to what comes next (سَأَلُوا۟ - they asked)
Adding the short vowels 'a' (فتحة) to make it mean 'he asked' in the past
Added وا at the end to show that a group of people (they) did the action instead of just one person (he)
This is a proper noun, the name of the prophet Moses. In this ayah, the People of the Book are asking about him, referring to when his people asked him for even greater signs.
Added an alif that shows the long 'aa' sound at the end of the name. This is just a writing style and doesn't change the meaning.
Added a small alif (ٓ) above the word to show that the sound is stretched slightly when reading. This is just for pronunciation and doesn't change the meaning.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added أَ at the beginning and changed vowels to make it comparative. In this ayah, it compares to ذٰلِكَ (that), showing they asked for something even bigger than that.

steps.m: from (used to compare with أَكْبَرَ - bigger than)
steps.n: Basic preposition showing comparison. In this ayah, it's used with أَكْبَرَ (bigger) to compare what they asked from Musa with what they asked from Muhammad ﷺ.
Basic demonstrative pronoun for something near
Adding the alif above (ٰ) extends the pointing distance
Adding ل with kasra (لِ) makes it point to something far away

steps.m: that (referring to what was mentioned before)
steps.n: Adding كَ at the end makes it specifically point to something mentioned before
Added basic past tense vowels to show who did the action and when
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas
Added وا at the end to show many people did the action
Added فَ at the beginning to show this happened after they asked Moses
Adding أ at the beginning makes it mean 'cause to see' or 'show'
Changed to command form by dropping the final ى and changing the middle vowel to kasra
Added نَا at the end to show who is being shown (us)
Added ال to specify this is THE one true God, and the hamza was dropped because this is a special word used so often
Added fatha at end to show this word is being acted upon in the sentence
Connected the root letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on second to form basic noun
Adding ة changes it from general 'clarity' to mean 'one clear instance' or 'one time of being clear'
Adding tanween (ً) makes it indefinite, meaning 'a' clear instance rather than 'the' clear instance
Connected the root letters with vowels to make past tense verb
Added ت at the end to change from 'he took' to 'she took'
Added هُمُ to show who was taken

steps.m: and so she took them (after أَرِنَا)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this happened as a result of what came before

steps.m: to strike suddenly with a powerful force

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed pattern to show who does the action
Added ة to make it a specific thing rather than just describing who does something
Added ال for 'the' and ُ to show this is the subject doing something in the sentence
The root letters were made into a noun by adding the vowel marks ُ and ْ to show the action itself (wrongdoing) rather than doing the action
Added ب with kasra at start to mean 'because of' and kasra at end to connect to next word
Added هِمْ at the end to show it belongs to them

steps.m: then (after they asked to see Allah)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that shows one thing happened after another, with some time or space between them. In this ayah, it connects what the people did after they asked to see Allah directly.
Added vowels to connect the letters and make a past tense verb
The ت is doubled (has shadda) to show this is done with more effort or intention than just 'taking'
Added وا at the end to show many people did the action
The root meaning of quick movement becomes a word for a baby cow, since calves are known for moving quickly and playfully
Added ال ('the') at the start and َ at the end to show this is the specific calf that was being taken as an object of worship
This is a basic preposition that shows something coming from somewhere or starting from a point. In this ayah, it connects 'that' (ذٰلِكَ) to 'greater' (أَكْبَرَ) to mean 'greater than that'.
Added a small ن (called nun ghunna) at the end to help with pronunciation when connecting to the next word. This doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to be distant or far away from something

steps.n: The fatha and sukoon pattern turns the idea of distance into the concept of 'after' (being distant in time)

steps.m: after (connected to مَا that follows it)
steps.n: The kasra at the end shows this word is connected to the word مَا that comes right after it, forming the phrase 'after what'

steps.m: what/that (connects to جَآءَتْهُمُ - came to them)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that links two parts of the sentence. Here it connects 'after' with 'clear signs came to them'. It's like saying 'after what/that came to them' in English.
The middle letter ي changes to ا with a special mark (آ) to show lengthening. Added fatha vowels.
Adding ت with sukun makes it feminine past tense
Adding هُمُ shows who received the action (them)
Changed the long alif (آ) to a regular alif (ا) - same meaning and pronunciation
Changed from basic root 'to make clear' into an adjective meaning 'very clear' by doubling the middle letter
Added feminine plural ending to create noun meaning 'clear proofs'
Added ال to make it specific and ُ to show it's doing the action. This word is the subject of the verb جَآءَتْهُمُ (came to them) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb. The و at the end changes to ا because it's a weak letter.
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action. The weak letter و returns because it's no longer at the end.
فَ at the start means 'so' or 'then', showing this happened as a result of something

steps.m: from, away from (pardoning them from ذٰلِكَ - that)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition that shows moving away from something or pardoning someone. In this ayah, it shows Allah pardoning the Children of Israel from their serious offense.
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun used to point to something nearby
The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) to show it's a long vowel
Adding لِ changes it from pointing to something near to pointing to something far away

steps.m: that (pointing to something far) (refers to asking for a book from heaven)
steps.n: Adding كَ completes the word, making it specifically point to something far away. In this ayah, it refers back to their request for a book to be sent down from heaven.
Changed from basic root to past tense 'he gave' - made longer to show someone giving something
Changed ى to يْ before adding suffix
Added نا at the end to show 'we' did the action
Added و at beginning to connect this word to what came before. This word shows what was given to Moses.

steps.m: Moses (the prophet being asked by فَقَدْ سَأَلُوا۟)
steps.n: This is a proper noun, the name of the prophet Moses. It's originally from Egyptian/Hebrew (meaning 'drawn from water') that has been adapted into Arabic. The long alif (ىٰ) at the end is called 'alif maqsurah' and shows this is a foreign name adapted to Arabic.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
The verb 'he had power' becomes a strong noun meaning 'authority/power' - like turning 'rule' into 'ruler'

steps.m: a clear authority/evidence (that We gave to Moses)
steps.n: Makes the noun indefinite and shows it's being given. This word refers to the clear proofs and authority that Allah gave to Moses.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Added مُ at start and changed middle vowel to kasra to show who/what does the action
Added shadda on م to emphasize the meaning
Added -an ending to change it from 'clear' to 'clearly' - like adding -ly in English
Added fatha vowels to connect the root letters and show this happened in the past
Added نا at the end to show Allah is speaking about Himself using the royal 'We'

steps.m: and We lifted up (referring to Allah)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this to the previous statement about how Allah dealt with the Children of Israel
Root letters f-w-q turned into a noun meaning 'above' by adding fatha and sukoon vowels. Shows what was raised up in the sentence.
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb رَفَعْنَا (we raised)

steps.m: above them (object of رَفَعْنَا - we raised)
steps.n: Added هُمُ at the end to show this is happening above them specifically
Added damma to specify this is the noun form referring to a specific mountain

steps.m: the mount (object being raised in وَرَفَعْنَا)
steps.n: Added ال to make it definite, shadda to emphasize the ط, and fatha at the end because it's the object being raised in the phrase 'and We raised above them'

steps.m: to make firm, secure, or bind

steps.n: Added مِي at start and ا after ث to make a noun meaning 'a binding agreement/promise'
Added kasra to show possession relationship
Added هِمْ to show it belongs to them
Added بِ to show 'by means of' or 'with'
Command form 'say!' made by adding damma and sukun. Not past tense.
Added نا at the end to show 'we' did the action in the past
Added و at the beginning to mean 'and', connecting this action to the previous one (وَرَفَعْنَا - and We raised)
Added fatha to show this is the preposition meaning 'to' or 'for'
Added هُ which means 'him/it'
Added مُ to change 'him' to 'them'. This word connects to ٱدْخُلُوا۟ (enter) showing who should enter.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Removed first vowel and changed middle vowel to make the basic present tense form
Added alif at beginning to make it a command to one person (like saying 'you, enter!')

steps.m: enter! (all of you) (command to enter الْبَابَ - the gate)
steps.n: Added plural marker to show this command is for a group. This word is connected to الْبَابَ (the gate) which is what they are commanded to enter.
Changes from root letters to actual noun form
Added ال to make it specific and fatha at the end because it's the object of the command 'enter'

steps.m: to bow down with forehead touching ground

steps.n: The root س-ج-د becomes سُجَّد (sujjad) - a plural noun meaning 'those who prostrate'. The ُ (damma) and ّ (shadda) change it from an action to mean 'people who do this action'

steps.m: bowing down (as a state/condition they were told to enter in)
steps.n: The ًا at the end shows this describes how they were told to enter (in a state of bowing down). This connects to ٱدْخُلُوا۟ (enter) in the ayah.
Added vowels to connect the letters together and make it past tense
The verb قَوَلَ changes to قُلْنَا by: 1) The middle letter drops out 2) The first vowel changes to 'u' 3) Adding نا at the end makes it mean 'we' instead of 'he'
Adding conjunction و to connect with previous statement
Added fatha to show the basic preposition meaning
Added هُ (him) to show who the preposition points to

steps.m: to/for them (referring to the Children of Israel being addressed in this ayah)
steps.n: Added م with sukun to change 'him' to 'them', making it plural
This is a negative particle that tells people not to do something. Here it connects with the word تَعْدُوا۟ (transgress) to say 'do not transgress'
Added fatha mark to help with pronunciation
Added vowels to make it past tense - 'he crossed limits'
Added ت at start to show 'you' and changed vowels for present tense

steps.m: you (all) cross limits (in لا تَعْدُوا۟ - do not cross limits)
steps.n: Added ا and ۟ to show this refers to a group of people. This word appears after لا (not/don't) to form a prohibition.
This is a preposition that shows something happens inside a time period (the Sabbath) or location. In this ayah, it connects to the word ٱلسَّبْتِ to show what happened during the Sabbath.
Connected the root letters together with vowel marks to make the noun for 'Sabbath'
Added ال (the) at the beginning to make it 'the Sabbath'. The س gets doubled when ال is added, which is a normal spelling rule.
Added kasra at the end because it comes after the word في (in/on) in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fathas to create the past tense verb meaning 'he took'
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action

steps.m: and we took (connected to وَرَفَعْنَا - and We raised)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) at the beginning to connect this action with the previous action 'We raised'
This is the basic preposition that shows something coming from somewhere or someone
Added هُ (meaning 'him/it') to show who/what is being referred to

steps.m: to bind firmly or make secure

steps.n: Added مِ at the start to show this is a thing used for binding/securing
Added ي after first letter to show this is a formal agreement or promise
Added long ا to emphasize this is a formal, binding agreement
Added emphasis mark (shadda) on م and added -an ending to show this is receiving the action
Forms an adjective describing the covenant mentioned in the ayah as being firm/strong
Shows this word is describing the covenant (مِّيثٰقًا) that was taken from them, mentioned earlier in the ayah
Starting with the basic question word ما (what)
Added بِ at the beginning to show cause or reason
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from something before it

steps.m: to break or violate (something like a promise)

steps.n: Changes from verb to noun meaning 'a break/violation'
Added kasra to show this word connects to what comes after it
Added هِم at the end to show this belongs to them. This word connects to مِّيثٰقَهُمْ (their covenant) which comes right after it in the ayah.

steps.m: to bind firmly or make secure

steps.n: Changed the action of binding into a noun for the thing that binds people together (an agreement)
The fatha ending shows this word is being acted upon in the sentence
Changed the alif to an alif maksura for writing style
Added هُمْ at the end to show the agreement belongs to them
Changed the verb into a noun showing the concept of rejection
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this with the previous word نَقْضِهِم (their breaking)
Added هِم at the end to show it belongs to them. The ِ before هِم shows this word is connected to what comes after it (بِـَٔايٰتِ ٱللَّهِ - the signs of Allah)

steps.m: to show, appear, or become clear
sign, miracle, or verse (something that shows or makes clear)
steps.n: Changed the root into a noun for one specific sign or miracle. The hamza and yaa combine into a long آ.
Makes the word plural to show multiple signs or verses
Added بِ to show these signs are being used as tools or means
Added kasra at the end because this word is possessed by Allah in the phrase 'signs of Allah'
Changed to the special form used only for Allah, with ال merged into the word and the first letter changed
The word 'Allah' got a kasra (i) sound at the end to show it belongs to something (the signs). Also has a stronger L sound (shadda) and a different type of alif at the start.
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Changed the verb into a noun showing the action itself
Added kasra to show this word is possessed by something
Added هِمُ (him) at the end to mean 'their' - shows who was killed
Added conjunction to connect with previous phrase about their disbelief
Changes the word from an action to a person who does that action, specifically someone who delivers messages from Allah
Makes the word plural to show we're talking about many prophets
Adds ال to show we're talking about specific prophets, not just any prophets

steps.m: the prophets (being acted upon in قَتْلِهِمُ/their killing of)
steps.n: Shows the prophets are being acted upon (they are being killed) in this part of the verse
Basic noun form showing something that is different or other than something else
Shows this word is being possessed by or connected to something after it
Adds meaning of 'with' or 'by means of' to the word
Connected the letters and doubled the middle letter to show this is a real, concrete truth

steps.m: any truth/right (being acted upon by بِغَيْرِ - without)
steps.n: Added the ٍ ending because this word comes after بِغَيْرِ (without) in the ayah, showing it's being affected by that word
Changes the verb into a noun meaning the actual speech or statement itself
Adds conjunction 'and' to connect with previous phrase

steps.m: and a saying/statement (connected to previous words)
steps.n: The ِ shows this word is connected to the words before it in the sentence

steps.m: and their saying (connected to previous words about their breaking of promises)
steps.n: Adds هِمْ which means 'their', showing this saying belongs to them. This connects to the earlier parts of the verse about their actions.
The root meaning of turning/changing becomes 'heart' because the heart is always turning emotions and pumping blood. This word is what's being described as covered (غُلْفٌ) in their statement.
Changes the inside of the word to show many hearts instead of just one heart. The ُ (damma) sounds and و (waw) make it plural.
Added first person plural possessive pronoun suffix to indicate ownership. This word is the subject of the predicate غُلْفٌ (covered/sealed) in their statement.
Changes to a noun meaning 'covered' or 'wrapped'
Added the double damma mark (tanween) to show these hearts are doing the action of being covered
This word is used to say 'No, that's not right - actually...' when correcting what someone said. Here it's saying 'No' to their claim that 'Our hearts are covered' and introducing what's really true.

steps.m: to seal, to stamp, to imprint
he sealed (subject of Allah sealing their hearts)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb. In this ayah, Allah is the one doing the sealing of their hearts because of their disbelief.

steps.m: to worship, to take as a deity

steps.n: Root meaning 'worship' became a noun meaning 'something that is worshipped'
The alif is elongated to show a long 'aa' sound
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific deity rather than any deity

steps.m: Allah (as the subject of طَبَعَ - sealed)
steps.n: This is a special word that comes from 'the god' but changes completely to become Allah's name. The ُ shows Allah is doing the action.
Basic preposition showing position or relationship. This word doesn't come from a root - it's a fundamental preposition.
Changed the ending to prepare for adding the pronoun suffix

steps.m: upon it/her (referring to their hearts that Allah sealed)
steps.n: Added feminine pronoun suffix هَا to show what is being acted upon. In this ayah, it refers back to their hearts (قُلُوب) that Allah has sealed.

steps.m: to reject/deny truth by covering it up

steps.n: Changed the verb into a noun that names the action itself, like changing 'reject' to 'rejection'
Added بِ at the beginning to show this is the reason for what happened in the previous part of the verse (فَبِمَا نَقْضِهِم - 'so because of their breaking')
Added هِمْ at the end to show this belongs to them (referring to those who broke their covenant). The ِ before هِمْ connects these parts together.
Basic negative particle that negates the verb يُؤْمِنُونَ (they believe) in this ayah
Added فَ to show this is a result or consequence of what was mentioned before. In this ayah, their disbelief is a result of their previous actions.
Changed from root letters to past tense 'he' form by adding vowels
Added a hamza at the start changes the meaning from 'he trusted' to 'he made someone trust/believe' - like making something happen
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start. The أ changed to ؤ because it comes after a damma.

steps.m: they believe (referring to those who rejected faith)
steps.n: Added ون at the end to show multiple people doing the action. This refers back to those who broke their covenant mentioned earlier in the verse.
Basic exception particle that introduces something excluded from what came before
Added alif at the end to complete the particle. This word excludes قَلِيلًا (a few/little) from what was mentioned before.

steps.m: to be little/small in amount or number

steps.n: Added ي between the second and third root letters to show this is a quality or state of being little/few
Added tanween alif (ًا) at the end to show this describes how they believe - only a little bit

steps.m: to reject or cover up truth

steps.n: Changed the verb into a noun that means the act or state of rejecting truth. The ُ (damma) and ْ (sukun) pattern creates a noun showing the concept itself.
Added بِ at the beginning to show reason or cause. The ِ (kasra) at the end shows this word follows بِ. This explains why Allah cursed them (mentioned in عَلَيْهِمْ earlier).
Added هِمْ at the end to show this disbelief belongs to them (referring to those who rejected Jesus)
Added وَ (and) to connect this to the previous reasons for Allah's curse: their breaking of promises and rejection of Allah's signs
Changed the root into a noun that means the thing that is said
The kasra shows this word is connected to the previous phrase 'and by their disbelief'
Adds possessive pronoun suffix meaning 'their'
The conjunction وَ connects this phrase to what came before
This is a basic preposition that shows something is directed towards or against something else. In this ayah, it shows their false accusations were directed against Maryam.
The small alif (ىٰ) at the end shows this is the end of the word. It doesn't change the meaning, it just helps with pronunciation.
This is a proper name of Hebrew origin meaning 'exalted' or 'beloved lady', referring to Mary the mother of Jesus

steps.m: Mary (as the object being spoken about)
steps.n: The fatha at the end shows Mary is the object receiving the action in this sentence
Added damma and sukun to make it into a noun meaning the act of false accusation
Added ان to intensify the meaning, showing this is a serious or major false accusation
Added an -an ending (tanween) to show this is receiving the action, and wrote it with a special long alif
Added vowels to make it a past tense verb showing something becoming great
Changed from a verb (became great) to a descriptive word (very great) by adding ي in the middle
Added ًا at the end to show this describes the slander (بُهْتٰنًا) mentioned before it. Both words end in ًا because adjectives match the words they describe.
Changed from verb 'to say' into the noun 'a saying' by adding vowel marks
Added kasra at the end to show this word belongs to something
Adding attached pronoun to show possession (their)
Adding conjunction prefix to connect with previous statement
This is a special word that makes what comes after it stronger or more certain, like adding 'definitely' in English
Added 'ا' at the end to show 'we' are doing the action. This connects to the next word قَتَلْنَا (we killed) where 'we' is the subject doing the action.

steps.m: to end life violently, to kill

steps.n: Adding vowel marks to show this is a basic past tense verb
Adding نا changes the subject to 'we' (first person plural). This word comes after إنّ (indeed/verily) which emphasizes the statement.

steps.m: one who is anointed (title of Jesus)
steps.n: Added ي between second and third root letters and vowels to create a special title. This pattern shows someone who has been affected by the action.
Added ٱلْ at the beginning to make it specific to Jesus, like saying 'the' in English

steps.m: the Messiah (object of قَتَلْنَا - we killed)
steps.n: Added َ at the end to show this is what the action is being done to. In this verse, it's what they claimed to have killed.

steps.m: Jesus (the Messiah mentioned in قَتَلْنَا ٱلْمَسِيحَ)
steps.n: This is a proper name with no Arabic root - it comes from the Hebrew/Aramaic name Yeshua/Isa. In this ayah, it refers to Jesus the Messiah who the speakers falsely claimed they killed.

steps.m: to build or establish a family

steps.n: Root 'build/establish' became 'son' by adding alif at start - common pattern for family words
Added fatha at the end to show this describes Jesus (عِيسَى) in the phrase 'Jesus son of Mary'
This is a proper name for the mother of Jesus/Isa, originally from Hebrew. Since it's a foreign name adopted into Arabic, it doesn't come from an Arabic root.
The fatha at the end shows this is the object of the verb قَتَلْنَا (we killed). The Jews were claiming they killed Jesus son of Mary.
The و makes it mean someone who does this action as their main role
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb 'we killed' in the phrase 'we killed the messenger'
Added shadda to emphasize the L sound

steps.m: of Allah (possessed by قَوْلِهِمْ - their saying)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show possession, and changed alif to wasl-alif since it comes after another word
This is a basic negative particle that makes something not true

steps.m: and not (they did not kill him)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it. This connects to قَتَلُوهُ (they killed him) to say 'and they did not kill him'
Added fatha marks to connect the root letters and make it past tense singular
و suffix changes subject from singular to plural masculine (they)
Added هُ to show who was killed. This refers back to Jesus (عيسى) mentioned earlier in the verse.
This is a particle that negates the verb that comes after it. Here it negates قَتَلُوهُ (they killed him), saying that they did not kill him.

steps.m: and not (negates قَتَلُوهُ - they killed him)
steps.n: The و connects this phrase to what came before it, joining two related ideas
Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past

steps.m: they crucified (referring to those who claimed to kill Jesus)
steps.n: Added و with damma (ُ) to show a group did this action
Added هُ at the end to show Jesus was the one they claimed to crucify

This is a basic particle used to show contrast between ideas. In this ayah, it contrasts with the previous statement about killing and crucifying Jesus, showing that something different actually happened.
The و connects this to the previous statement. It shows that this new information is being added to what came before, while still contrasting with it.

steps.m: to look like or resemble something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb

steps.m: it was made to appear similar (to them)
steps.n: Changed to passive form by adding damma at start and kasra with shadda on middle letter. This shows that something was done to the subject rather than the subject doing the action.

steps.m: to/for (connects to قَتَلُوهُ - they killed him)
steps.n: This is a particle that shows direction, purpose, or ownership. Here it connects to the verb قَتَلُوهُ (they killed him) to show who the action relates to.

steps.m: to/for them (refers to those who differed about عِيسَى)
steps.n: Adding هُمْ shows this refers to a group (specifically those who differed about Jesus). This connects to the earlier part of the ayah talking about their claims.
Added fatha to the و to show it's a conjunction
Added إِ (indeed) to make it stronger and more emphatic
Added نَّ with shadda to complete the emphatic particle إِنَّ. This word connects to ٱلَّذِينَ (those who) in the next part of the verse.
Added ال to make it definite and doubled the ذ letter to connect them smoothly
Added ين at the end to show we're talking about multiple people. This word connects to ٱخْتَلَفُوا۟ (they differed) which comes right after it in the ayah.
Added alif at start and ta (ت) in middle shows people doing something with each other - like disagreeing together
The ا at the start helps pronounce the word and shows it's Form VIII
This is a basic preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. Since it's a particle, we start with its basic form rather than a root.

steps.m: in it (referring to the doubt/confusion about Jesus mentioned in the ayah)
steps.n: Adding هِ makes it refer back to their confusion about what happened to Jesus (عليه السلام). The هِ refers to their state of disagreement mentioned earlier in the verse.
This particle adds emphasis to what comes after it. It's like adding 'definitely' or 'surely' in English.
Adds the preposition 'in/within'. In this ayah, it connects to the word شَكٍّ (doubt) that comes right after it, showing they are 'in doubt'.
The doubled middle letter makes the meaning stronger

steps.m: any doubt (in لَفِى شَكٍّ - surely in doubt)
steps.n: Added the tanween kasra (ٍ) to show this is indefinite (any doubt) and is being affected by the word فِى (in) that comes before it
The shadda (ّ) on م makes the sound stronger, which adds emphasis to the word
The هُ at the end means 'him' - it refers back to Jesus (عِيسَى) who was mentioned earlier in the ayah. The ْ (sukun) is added before هُ to help with pronunciation.
In this ayah, مَا is used as a negative particle that denies the action of killing. It's working with the word قَتَلُوهُ (they killed him) to say 'they did not kill him'

steps.m: to/for (connects to شُبِّهَ - was made to appear)
steps.n: This is a prefix that shows direction or purpose. Here it connects to the word شُبِّهَ (was made to appear) to show who something was made to appear to.

steps.m: to/for them (males) (connects to شُبِّهَ - was made to appear)
steps.n: Added هُم which means 'them' when talking about a group of males or a mixed group. In this ayah, it refers to those who claimed they killed Jesus.
This is a basic preposition that shows how or with what something is done
The هِ with the small ۦ mark shows this means 'him' or 'it' when referring to one male person or thing. Here it refers back to عِلْمٍ (knowledge).
This is a basic preposition showing that their doubt comes from their lack of knowledge
These are the basic letters that give us the idea of knowing something
Added the vowels ِ and ْ to turn the action of knowing into the thing 'knowledge'
Added ٍ at the end because this word comes after مِنْ (from/of). This ending also shows we're talking about any knowledge, not specific knowledge.

steps.m: except (restricts following word from previous statement)
steps.n: Added shadda to strengthen the meaning of exclusion. This word restricts what follows from what came before.

steps.m: except (restricts اتِّبَاعَ ٱلظَّنِّ/following of assumption from previous statement)
steps.n: Added alif at the end to complete the word. In this ayah, it excludes 'following of assumption' from what came before.

steps.m: to follow or go after something
steps.n: These are the basic letters that give us the idea of following
Added vowels to make it a past tense verb
Added ا at start and doubled the ت to show doing something with extra effort or purpose
Changed the verb into a noun by adding ا after second letter and changing vowels

steps.m: act of following closely (object of إِلَّا)
steps.n: Changed the first letter to a special alif and added a mark at the end because it's being acted upon by the word 'except'
This is the basic concept of having a thought or assumption
Adding the vowel marks makes it a complete past tense verb meaning 'he thought'
Changed from a verb (he thought) to a noun (assumption) and added kasra to connect it to the previous word
Added ال at beginning to make it specific. This word is contrasted with يَقِينًۢا (certainty) in the ayah to show they only followed assumptions, not certain knowledge.
This is a basic negative or questioning particle in Arabic
Added و at the beginning to connect this negative statement with what came before. In this ayah, it's specifically negating that they killed Jesus.
Basic meaning of taking life or causing death
Added vowels to make the basic past tense verb - this shows one person did the action in the past
Changed the end to show many people did the action instead of just one person
Added هُ at the end to show who received the action (him). This word comes after مَا which means 'not', so together they mean 'they did not kill him'
Basic meaning of absolute certainty
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a past tense verb
Added ي to turn the verb (being certain) into a noun meaning complete certainty
Added ًا at the end to show this describes how something was done - in this case, describing how certain they are that they did not kill Jesus (peace be upon him)
This is a particle that helps switch from one idea to another, like saying 'Actually, let me correct that'

steps.m: but rather (introduces what Allah raised him)
steps.n: In this ayah, بَل introduces what really happened - Allah raised Jesus to Himself, correcting a previous wrong belief. It connects to رَّفَعَهُ (raised him) to show the true story.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make past tense. Allah (اللَّهُ) is doing this action.
Added shadda on first letter to show emphasis - this makes the lifting more definite and powerful
Added هُ at the end to show who was lifted (referring to Jesus in this context)
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
Added ال (the) and changed the word to become the proper name of the One True God. The ُ at the end shows this is the subject doing something in the sentence.
This is a basic preposition showing direction or destination. It's one of the most common Arabic prepositions.
The ى changes to ي with a sukun (ْ) to prepare for connecting with the next part. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning.

steps.m: to/toward him (referring to Allah raising Jesus to Himself)
steps.n: Added هِ at the end which means 'him'. In this ayah, it refers to Allah raising Jesus to Himself.

steps.m: to exist or be in a state

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense
Added alif after ك to make it a hollow verb (has weak middle letter)

steps.m: and he was (connected to عَزِيزًا حَكِيمًا - mighty and wise)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to mean 'and'. This connects 'he was' to the description that follows in the ayah (mighty and wise)
Changed the root letters into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding vowel marks
Added 'the' to make it specific/definite
Added damma to show this word is the subject
Adding vowels and ي in the middle makes this describe someone with great strength and honor. This word describes Allah in this ayah.
Adding ًا shows this is describing Allah's attribute in this sentence structure

steps.m: to judge wisely or make wise decisions

steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters into a basic past-tense verb
Changed from 'he judged' to a special form meaning 'one who is very wise' - like changing 'teach' to 'teacher'
Added -an ending to show this word is describing Allah in this part of the sentence
This is a combination of two particles: و (and) + إن (if)
This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or what group it belongs to. Here it connects to the word أَهْلِ (people) to show which people are being talked about.
Added a shadda (doubling mark) on the م because it comes after the word وَإِن. This is just for pronunciation and doesn't change the meaning.
Added vowel marks to make the basic noun meaning 'people' or 'family'
Added kasra at the end to show this word owns or belongs to what comes after it. Here it forms the important phrase 'people of the Book' (أَهْلِ الكِتٰبِ), which refers to Jews and Christians.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changes verb into noun meaning the result of writing. The kasra at the start and alif in the middle create this noun pattern.
Adding ال makes it specific - this refers to a particular book (scripture)
The kasra at the end shows this word is possessed by أهل (people) in the phrase أهل الكتاب (people of the book)

This is a basic exceptive particle that shows something or someone is excluded from what was mentioned before. In this ayah, it shows that despite what was said before, the People of the Book will believe in him.
Added vowels to connect root letters into past tense verb
Changed to present/future tense by adding يُ prefix and changing vowels. The hamza gets a sukun.
Added لَ at beginning to show emphasis/certainty

steps.m: surely he will believe (with strong emphasis)
steps.n: Added نَّ at the end for extra emphasis, showing this is absolutely certain to happen
Third person masculine singular pronoun. In this ayah, it refers back to Jesus who was mentioned earlier.

steps.m: in him (referring to belief in Jesus)
steps.n: Adds the preposition بِ (in) to show what they will believe in

steps.m: in him (referring to belief in Jesus)
steps.n: Adds a small alif (ۦ) after هِ to help with pronunciation when continuing to the next word

steps.m: to face or be in front of

steps.n: Changes verb 'to face' into noun meaning 'before'
Added fatha at the end to show this word is possessed by 'his death' (مَوْتِهِۦ) in the ayah
Changes the verb 'to die' into the noun 'death' by adding the vowels 'a' and silent 'w'

steps.m: death (ready to be connected to something)
steps.n: Adds kasra (ِ) at the end to show this word will be connected to something that comes after it
Adds هِ meaning 'his' at the end. This whole word comes after قَبْلَ (before) in the ayah, making the phrase 'before his death'
Connected the letters together with vowel marks to make the basic word for 'day'

steps.m: on the day (time when يَكُونُ happens)
steps.n: Added a fatha at the end to show this word tells us when something happens. It's connected to the verb يَكُونُ (he will be) in this ayah.

steps.m: and on the day (time when يَكُونُ happens)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this to what came before it
Changed from basic root meaning 'rise/stand' to mean 'a people/folk' (a group that stands together)
Changed from 'standing' to 'the act of standing up' by changing the middle letter and adding an alif
Added ة to make it refer to the specific event of resurrection
Added ال to show we're talking about THE resurrection (the Day of Judgment)
Changed to show it belongs to something else (by adding ِ at the end), with a small spelling change in the middle
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense form
Changed to present/future tense by adding يَ at start and ُ marks. This word describes what will happen with لَيُؤْمِنَنَّ (they will believe).
This is the basic preposition meaning 'upon' or 'over'. It's one of the most common Arabic prepositions.
When adding a pronoun after عَلَى, the ى changes to يْ to make it easier to connect
Added هِمْ at the end to show who is being referred to (them)

steps.m: to witness or testify to something

steps.n: Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb
Changed from past tense verb 'witnessed' to noun 'witness' by adding ي in the middle

steps.m: a witness (being acted upon by يَكُونُ)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this word is being acted upon by the verb يَكُونُ (he/it is) in this ayah
Changes the action into a noun meaning the act itself or its result

steps.m: by/because of wrongdoing (connected to حَرَّمْنَا - we forbade)
steps.n: Adds بِ to show this wrongdoing was the reason for the forbidding mentioned later in حَرَّمْنَا (we forbade)
Adds tanween (double kasra) to show this is referring to their wrongdoing in general, not a specific instance

steps.m: so because of a wrongdoing (reason for حَرَّمْنَا - we forbade)
steps.n: Adds فَ to show this explains the reason for what follows - that Allah forbade certain good things to them because of their wrongdoing
This is the basic form of the preposition meaning 'from'
The shadda emphasizes the 'م' sound, making the word stronger
The fatha at the end shows this connects to the next word الَّذِينَ (those who)
Added ين at the end to make it refer to multiple people
Added ٱلْ at the beginning with a shadda on the following letter, making it specific. This word describes the people who did هَادُوا۟ (became Jews).
Connected the root letters and added vowels. The middle root letter و changes to ا because it's a weak letter.
Added وا at the end to show multiple people did the action. This word is connected to حَرَّمْنَا (we forbade) in the ayah, showing who was affected by the forbidding.

steps.m: to forbid, prohibit, or make something unlawful

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Adding shadda (doubling mark) on the middle letter makes the meaning stronger and more emphatic
Changed the final vowel to sukun to prepare for adding the 'we' suffix
Added نَا at the end to show that 'we' did the action
This is the basic preposition meaning 'on' or 'upon'
The ى changes to يْ when adding pronouns at the end
Added هِمْ at the end to show who is being acted upon (them)
Root 'good' got doubled middle letter (shadda) to mean 'very good/pure', like adding 'very' in English
Added feminine plural ending ٰت to make it refer to multiple good things

steps.m: good/pure things (that were made forbidden) (feminine plural)
steps.n: Added tanween kasra to show this word is affected by حَرَّمْنَا (we made forbidden) in this ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha to create basic past tense verb
Changed from active ('he made lawful') to passive ('it was made lawful') by adding أُ at start and changing middle vowel to ِ, with doubled middle letter

steps.m: it (feminine) was made lawful (referring to طَيِّبٰتٍ - good things)
steps.n: Added feminine marker ت with sukun to show this refers to the feminine word طَيِّبٰتٍ (good things) mentioned in the ayah
Added fatha to help with pronunciation

steps.m: for them (connected to حَرَّمْنَا - we forbade)
steps.n: Added the pronoun هم meaning 'them' to show who the preposition is directed to. This combines with حَرَّمْنَا (we forbade) to show what was forbidden for them.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Changed verb into a noun by doubling middle letter
Added هِمْ to show it belongs to them

steps.m: by/because of their prevention (of سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ)
steps.n: Added بِ to show reason or cause. This prevention refers to them blocking Allah's path (سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ) mentioned in the next part of the ayah
Adds conjunction meaning 'and'

This is a preposition that shows movement or turning away from something. In this ayah, it connects 'their turning away' (صَدِّهِمْ) with 'the path' (سَبِيلِ) to show they turned away from Allah's path.
Added ي between second and third letters to make it a noun meaning 'path'
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to Allah's name after it, like saying 'path of Allah'
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'
Added genitive case ending to show possession/belonging
Added vowels to show this describes something increasing or growing
Adding ي makes it describe a strong or lasting quality

steps.m: much (describing how much they prevented others from Allah's path)
steps.n: The ًا ending makes this word describe 'how much' - like adding 'ly' in English (they prevented greatly/extensively)
Changed from basic root meaning 'take' into noun meaning 'taking' by adding vowel marks
Added و at beginning to mean 'and'
Added kasra to show possession
Added هِمُ at the end to show 'their' and connect to the next word

steps.m: to increase or grow beyond bounds

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb
Changed vowel on first letter from fatha to kasra to make it a noun
Added 'the' (ال) at start and doubled the middle letter with shadda
Added و with sukun at the end to show nominative case
Added alif at end to emphasize the noun meaning
This is a particle that adds emphasis or certainty

steps.m: and indeed (connects to أَخْذِهِمُ - their taking)
steps.n: Added و with fatha to connect this phrase with the previous one about their taking of usury

steps.m: to stop or prevent from doing something

steps.n: Connected the root letters together with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed vowels to show this happened to someone rather than someone doing it
Added وا to show this happened to a group of people
This is a preposition showing distance or separation. The sukun (ْ) shows where the word stops.
Added هُ ('hu') at the end to mean 'from it', connecting back to usury. Like adding 'it' to show what we're talking about.
Root letters أ-ك-ل became the noun 'eating' by adding the vowel marks َ (fatha) and ْ (sukoon)
Added و at the beginning to connect this to the previous phrase about taking usury
Added هِم to show this is their action of eating, referring back to those who took usury
Root letters م-و-ل become the noun مال (wealth) by adding vowels and changing و to ا
Changes the word to show many types of wealth/possessions
Shows this word is being acted upon - specifically being consumed wrongfully as mentioned in the ayah

steps.m: to be social or friendly with others

steps.n: Root 'people' became 'the people' and got kasra ending because it follows a word that affects it
Added 'the' at the beginning. The ن gets doubled (shadda) because it comes right after ال
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed from verb 'became false' to noun meaning 'false thing' by adding alif
Added بِ at the beginning to show how something is done
Added ال to point to a specific false thing, and kasra at the end because it follows the word بِ
Changed the alif to a long alif (ٰ) - this is just a writing variation that doesn't change the meaning
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form basic past tense verb
Added 'أ' at start makes it mean 'to make something/someone do the action'
Adding نا at the end changes it from 'he' to 'we'
The و (and) was added to connect this verb to what came before it, showing we prepared something for الْكٰفِرِينَ (the disbelievers)

steps.m: to reject or cover up truth

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Changed to describe someone who does the action
Makes the word plural to refer to multiple people

steps.m: the ones who reject/cover up truth
steps.n: Makes it specific by adding 'the'

steps.m: for the ones who reject/cover up truth
steps.n: Adds 'for/to' meaning and shows this word is being acted upon in the sentence
This is a preposition showing source or origin. The ِ and ْ marks help with pronunciation.

steps.m: from them (referring to those who took usury)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show this applies to 'them' (the people mentioned before who took usury)
Added vowels to connect the letters together and show this happened in the past

Long ا after second root letter makes this a noun meaning the result of punishment. This word is in accusative case because it is the object (what is being prepared) of the verb أعتدنا (we have prepared).

steps.m: a punishment (object of أَعْتَدْنَا/we have prepared)
steps.n: Adds accusative case marking to show this noun is the object of the verb
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
This form shows something that has a very strong or intense quality
The tanween alif shows this word is describing the punishment (عَذَابًا) mentioned in the ayah
Added vowel marks to show how the word is pronounced. The kasra shows connection to what follows.
Added kasra at the end to connect smoothly with the next word in the sentence.
Added shadda on first letter for emphasis and stronger pronunciation.
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
Changed pattern to show who does the action

steps.m: the one who is firmly established
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific and doubled the ر with shadda

steps.m: those who are firmly established (subject of يُؤْمِنُونَ)
steps.n: Added ون to show it's talking about multiple people. This word is the subject of يُؤْمِنُونَ (they believe) in this ayah.
Basic preposition showing location or position. Here it connects 'those firmly rooted' with 'knowledge', showing who has deep understanding.
The ي at the end is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Changed the verb into a noun by adding kasra and sukun marks. This pattern is used to turn actions into concepts.
Added ال to make it specific knowledge and kasra at the end because it comes after the word فِى (in/within). This word is being described by الراسخون (those who are firmly grounded).
This is a basic preposition showing where something comes from or starts. The kasra and sukun help connect the letters.

steps.m: from them (referring to الرَّاسِخُونَ - those firmly grounded)
steps.n: Added هُمْ to show this refers to 'them' - specifically referring back to those who are firmly grounded in knowledge (الرَّاسِخُونَ) mentioned earlier in the verse.

steps.m: to trust, feel secure, believe in

steps.n: Added مُ at start to show 'one who' and changed middle letter to ؤ to make it mean 'believer'
Regular masculine plural suffix added
Adding definite article makes the noun definite
Added و at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action
This is the base relative pronoun that introduces what was sent down. The alif is elongated for emphasis.

steps.m: in what/in that which (connects to يُؤْمِنُونَ)
steps.n: Added بِ at the beginning to show this is the object of belief - they believe IN what was sent down. This connects to the verb يُؤْمِنُونَ (they believe).
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Added أ at beginning to show someone is causing the action to happen
Changed vowels to show this happened to something rather than something doing it
This is the basic preposition meaning 'to' or 'towards'. It's one of the essential Arabic prepositions that shows direction or destination.
The ى changes to ي with sukun when connecting to a pronoun. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning.

steps.m: to/towards you (connected to أُنزِلَ - was sent down)
steps.n: Added the pronoun ك at the end to show who the preposition is directed to. This word connects to أُنزِلَ (was sent down) in the ayah, showing what was sent down to you.
