steps.m: those who give good news (describing رُّسُلًا/messengers)
steps.n: Added plural ending to show it describes multiple messengers

steps.m: to warn or alert of danger

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Adding مُ at start and changing vowels makes this describe someone who does the warning
Adding ين makes this refer to multiple people who warn

steps.m: and warners (connected to مُّبَشِّرِينَ - bearers of good news)
steps.n: Adding وَ connects this word to مُّبَشِّرِينَ (bearers of good news) mentioned before it in the ayah
Added kasra to show this introduces a purpose or reason
Added hamza with fatha to combine with أنْ (that)
Added لَّ (which comes from لا) with shadda to show negation
Added alif at the end to complete the negative particle لا
Added basic vowels to connect the letters
Added يَ at start to show present tense, and changed middle vowel to ُ because it's a hollow verb
Added fatha at end to show subjunctive mood

steps.m: people (those who are social/familiar with each other)
steps.n: Root meaning 'being social' became a word for 'people' who are social with each other
Added ال to make it definite. The ن gets a shadda because it's a sun letter.
Added لِ meaning 'for/to' and kasra at the end because it follows لِ. This word shows who the message is for in relation to يَكُونَ (there might be).
This is a basic preposition that shows something is on top of, against, or upon something else. Here it connects حُجَّةٌ (argument/proof) with what comes after it.
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word in Arabic that always refers to The One True God.
Added kasra at the end to show possession or connection, changed alif to wasl-alif, and added shadda to emphasize the laam

steps.m: to prove or argue with evidence

steps.n: Adding fathas creates the basic past tense verb
Changed from a verb (to argue) into a noun (one proof) by adding shadda and feminine ending
Added tanween (double damma) to show this is indefinite - referring to any proof. In this ayah, it refers to a proof against people who might argue with Allah after the messengers were sent.
Connected the root letters together with fatha and sukun to form the basic noun meaning 'after'
Added fatha at the end to show this word is in the accusative case
The و makes it mean 'someone who does this action a lot'
Changes the word to show many messengers instead of just one
Adds 'the' to show we're talking about specific messengers, and doubles the ر because it comes after ال
Adds kasra at the end to show this word follows another word in the sentence

steps.m: to exist or be in a state

steps.n: The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak letter between two fathas

steps.m: and he was (connected to عَزِيزًا حَكِيمًا - mighty and wise)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this word with what comes after it, showing that Allah was both mighty and wise
Added ال to specify The One True God, and the initial hamza merged with the lam creating a doubled letter
Added the shadda to show doubled lam, changed initial alif to wasl form, and added damma to show this is the subject being described as Mighty and Wise
Added short vowels (fat-ha and kasra) to make it past tense - 'he was strong'
Adding ي makes it an intensive adjective showing Allah's perfect attribute of power
The ًا ending shows this is describing Allah in the sentence وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ عَزِيزًا (And Allah is All-Powerful)

steps.m: to judge wisely or make wise decisions
one who is full of wisdom (describing اللَّهُ)
steps.n: Added vowels and ي to create a word describing someone characterized by wisdom. This word describes Allah in this ayah.

steps.m: All-Wise (describing Allah as the object of كَانَ)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this describes Allah as the object of كَانَ (was). This is part of the phrase وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ عَزِيزًا حَكِيمًا (and Allah is Ever-Mighty, All-Wise).
This word combines لا (no/not) with كن (but) to make a stronger word for 'but'. The parts join together to make one word.
Added kasra vowel marks to help with pronunciation and show this is a complete word.
Added shadda on first letter to emphasize the word, making it stronger when spoken.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding vowel marks

steps.m: Allah (The One True God who testifies in this ayah)
steps.n: Changed to the unique name of The One True God by adding ال with special spelling (ٱ instead of أ), doubling the ل with shadda, and adding ُ to show this is the subject doing the testifying in this ayah
Connected the root letters with vowels to make past tense verb

steps.m: he witnesses (subject of Allah يَشْهَدُ - Allah witnesses)
steps.n: Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. This shows Allah's continuous witnessing of what was sent down.
Added fatha to show the basic pronunciation of the word ما which means 'what'
Added the elongated alif (آ) to show the long 'aa' sound. This is just a spelling variation that doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: with/by what (connects to يَشْهَدُ - He testifies)
steps.n: Added the preposition بِ (with/by) at the beginning. In this ayah, it connects to the verb يَشْهَدُ (He testifies) to show what Allah is testifying about.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Added أَ at the beginning to show that someone is causing the action to happen. This word is what Allah (the subject of يَشْهَدُ - he testifies) sent down.
Basic preposition meaning 'to' or 'towards'
Changed ى to ي with sukun to prepare for attachment

steps.m: to you (object of أَنزَلَ - He sent down)
steps.n: Added ك at the end to show this is directed 'to you'. This word shows who received what was sent down (أَنزَلَ) in this ayah.
Added basic vowels to connect the letters and make it a past tense verb
The أَ at the start makes this mean 'caused to descend/sent down'. This is connected to يَشْهَدُ (he testifies) in the ayah.

steps.m: he sent it down (object of يَشْهَدُ)
steps.n: The ه (hu) at the end means 'it' - showing what was sent down. Like saying 'sent down' vs 'sent it down'
Root meaning 'know' becomes the noun 'knowledge' by adding vowels inside the word
Added بِ at the beginning to mean 'with' and ِ at the end because it's connected to the next word

steps.m: with His knowledge (referring to Allah's knowledge)
steps.n: Added هِۦ at the end to show it's Allah's knowledge. The ۦ mark shows this is a special pronunciation in the Quran.

steps.m: to have power or control over something

steps.n: This is not correct - 'malaa'ik' (angels) comes from 'malak' (angel), not from 'malaka' (to possess). They are different words that happen to share letters.
Adding ة shows this is a complete group
Adding ال makes it refer to all angels as a specific group
Adding و connects this word to what came before it

steps.m: and the angels (as the subject doing يَشْهَدُونَ - they bear witness)
steps.n: Adding ُ shows the angels are doing the action in the verse
Basic past tense verb form meaning 'he did the action'
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added ون at the end to show multiple people (men or mixed group) are doing the action

steps.m: he/it was sufficient (verb in وَكَفَىٰ بِٱللَّهِ شَهِيدًا - 'and Allah is sufficient as witness')
steps.n: Added fathas to make it past tense. This verb is part of a common Arabic phrase meaning 'Allah is sufficient'
Adding و as a conjunction to connect this clause
Added alif maqsura (ى with two dots) at the end because this word ends with a weak letter ي
This is the unique proper name of God in Arabic. It's related to the root meaning of divinity but is a distinct proper noun.
Added shadda on ل to show it's pronounced strongly
Added kasra at the end to show possession or connection

steps.m: by/with Allah (object of يَشْهَدُ - He testifies)
steps.n: Added بِ at beginning to show how Allah is involved in the action. In this ayah, Allah is the one by whom testimony is given.
Basic past tense verb form meaning 'he did the action'
Changed from a past tense verb (he witnessed) to a noun meaning 'witness' by adding ي in the middle
Added tanween alif to show this word is being acted upon by the verb كَفَىٰ (is sufficient)
This is a special word that emphasizes what comes after it. It makes the next word (ٱلَّذِينَ) change its ending to show it's being emphasized.
This is the basic form that points to someone or something. It's like saying 'who' or 'which' in English.
Adding ال at the beginning makes it point to specific people or things we're talking about, not just any person or thing.
Adding ين at the end makes it talk about many people instead of just one. This word connects to كَفَرُوا۟ (they disbelieved) in the ayah, showing who did the disbelieving.
Added fathas to connect the root letters and make a past tense verb
The وا shows multiple men did the action
Connected the letters and doubled the middle letter to show strong prevention
Added وَ (and) to connect this action with the previous word كَفَرُوا۟ (they disbelieved)

steps.m: and they blocked/prevented (others from Allah's path)
steps.n: Changed from one person to many people doing the action. This connects to عَن سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ (from Allah's path) which comes right after.
This is a basic preposition that shows separation or moving away from something. In this ayah, it shows people turning away from Allah's path (سَبِيلِ).
The fatha (َ) on ع shows this word is fixed and doesn't change its form regardless of its position in the sentence

steps.m: to make a way or path

steps.n: Adding ي and vowels changes it from a verb into a noun meaning 'path'
Added kasra at the end to show this is connected to the word Allah (اللَّهِ) that follows it, making it mean 'path of Allah'
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding specific vowel patterns
Added ال (the) and changed the word to become the proper name of God. The ِ at the end shows this word is connected to what comes after it in the sentence.
This is the basic form of the word that shows something definitely happened
Added fatha on ق and sukun on د to show how it's pronounced. This word emphasizes that they definitely went astray in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense form
When two same letters come together at the end, they combine with a shadda
Added وا to show many people did this action
Added alif after second letter to make it into a noun showing the action itself. This word comes after ضَلُّوا (they went astray) to emphasize how lost they are.

steps.m: a complete losing of the way
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this describes how completely they went astray. This connects to the word بَعِيدًا (far) that comes next in the ayah to show they are very far from the right path.
Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb

steps.m: far, distant (describes something that is far away)
steps.n: Forms an adjective showing the quality of being distant or far away

steps.m: far away (describes how they went astray in ضَلُّوا۟)
steps.n: Adds ًا at the end to show this word describes how they went astray (ضَلُّوا۟) in this ayah
This is a special word that doesn't come from a root. It's used at the start to make what follows stronger or more certain.

Added the kasra (ِ) under the إ and shadda (ّ) on ن to show this is a strong emphasis. This word connects to 'those who disbelieved' (ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟) in this ayah to emphasize what will be said about them.
This is the basic form of the word that points to someone or something. It's like saying 'who' or 'which' in English.
Adding ٱلّ at the beginning makes it more specific, like saying 'the one who' instead of just 'who'.

steps.m: those who (referring to people who كَفَرُوا۟ وَظَلَمُوا۟)
steps.n: Adding ن at the end makes it plural, changing it from 'the one who' to 'those who'. In this ayah, it refers to the people who disbelieved and did wrong.

steps.m: to disbelieve or cover up the truth

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create past tense verb. This word is part of the subject that لَمْ يَكُنِ (was not) is describing.
Added وا to show multiple people did the action. This refers back to ٱلَّذِينَ (those who) mentioned earlier in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to show this is a completed action in the past
The suffix وا shows this was done by multiple people - specifically referring back to 'those who disbelieved' (الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟) mentioned earlier in the ayah
Adds و to connect this action with their previous action of disbelieving (كَفَرُوا۟), showing they both disbelieved AND wronged
This is a special word that makes the verb after it (يَكُنِ) mean the opposite - that something did not happen in the past. It's like adding 'not' to 'did' in English.
Adds vowel marks to make the past tense 'he' form of the word meaning 'to be'
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
The word follows لَمْ which requires the jussive mood, causing the و to drop and the final vowel to become sukun

steps.m: he is (in jussive mood, connected to next word)
steps.n: Added kasra to connect smoothly to the next word ٱللَّهُ
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding specific vowel patterns
Added the U mark at the end to show this is the subject/doer
Added 'the' at the start to make it specific/definite
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: so that he forgives (purpose of لَمْ يَكُنِ)
steps.n: Added لِ to show purpose and changed the final vowel to fatha. This shows why Allah won't do something mentioned in لَمْ يَكُنِ (was not).
Added fatha to help with pronunciation when connecting to next part
Added هُمْ (them) to show who the action is for. This word connects to يَغْفِرَ (forgive) in the ayah, showing who won't be forgiven.
This is a negative particle that makes the following verb لِيَهْدِيَهُمْ (to guide them) into 'not guide them'
Adding وَ connects this negative statement to the previous phrase, showing these are two related ideas: Allah will not forgive them AND will not guide them
Connected the root letters with fatha marks. The final ي changes to ى because it's a weak letter at the end.
Changes from past tense (he guided) to present tense (he guides) by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowel

steps.m: so that he guides them (purpose of لَمْ يَكُنِ)
steps.n: The لِ at the start shows this action is happening for a reason - to explain why Allah did something mentioned before

steps.m: to make a path or way

steps.n: Adding ي in the middle changes it from a verb into a noun meaning 'path'

steps.m: a path (object of لِيَهْدِيَهُمْ - to guide them)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the object being acted upon by the verb 'to guide' in the ayah
This is a basic exception particle that cannot be broken down further. In this ayah, it introduces an exception related to the path to Hell (جَهَنَّمَ).

steps.m: to make a path or way

steps.n: Changes from basic root into a noun meaning 'path' by adding ي in the middle
Added a fatha mark at the end to show this is the object (the path) that is being talked about
This is a proper noun borrowed from Hebrew 'Gehenna', originally referring to a deep valley. Connected the letters with basic vowels.
The shadda (doubled ن) emphasizes the depth and intensity of Hell

steps.m: Hell (as the object being referred to)
steps.n: The fatha at the end shows this is the object being referred to in the phrase 'except the path of Hell'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Active participle form describes one who performs the action

steps.m: those who stay forever (describing people in جَهَنَّمَ)
steps.n: Changed from singular (one person) to plural (many people) who stay forever, and matches with جَهَنَّمَ to show who will stay there
Added kasra under ف to show it's a preposition meaning 'in'
Added ها at the end to show something feminine is being referred to. Here it refers to جَهَنَّمَ (Hell) which is feminine in Arabic.
Changed the final alif to an elongated form (آ) for emphasis and proper pronunciation at the end of a phrase
Connected the root letters together with fatha on first letter

steps.m: forever (describing how long they will stay in جَهَنَّمَ)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to make it work as 'forever' - like saying 'eternally' in English
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two vowels in a past tense verb

steps.m: and he was (connected to خٰلِدِينَ - eternally abiding)
steps.n: Adding و at the beginning connects this to خٰلِدِينَ (eternally abiding) that came before
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun for pointing to something nearby
The alif is replaced with a special mark (alif khanjariya) that shows this is a demonstrative
Adding لِ changes it from pointing to something near to pointing to something far away
Adding كَ makes it a complete demonstrative pronoun meaning 'that' (pointing to something far away)
This is a preposition that shows something is on top of, over, or upon something else. In this ayah, it connects يَسِيرًا (easy) with اللَّهِ (Allah) to show that something is easy for Allah.
Added ال and merged it with the word to create the proper name Allah. The hamza from إِلَه is dropped in this special case.
Added kasra at the end to show possession, shadda on lam for emphasis, and changed alif to wasl-alif since it comes in the middle of speech. This word is possessed by يَسِيرًا (easy) in this ayah.
Forms an adjective describing 'that' (ذٰلِكَ) in the ayah, showing how easy it is for Allah

steps.m: easy (describing how something is for Allah)
steps.n: Adds tanween fatha to show this describes how something is for Allah (عَلَى ٱللَّهِ)
The middle letter is doubled with shadda to create a word used for calling attention
Added an alif at the end to show this is in the accusative case

steps.m: O you (as a particle of address)
steps.n: Added ها to make it a complete vocative particle for addressing people
Adds يٰ with a long vowel mark and hamza to show this is specifically calling out to people

steps.m: to be social, friendly with others

steps.n: Changes from verb 'to be social' into noun meaning 'people'
Added ال to specify these particular people being addressed in يٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّاسُ (O people!)
Added damma to show this word is the subject being addressed

steps.m: indeed/certainly (emphasizes جَآءَكُمُ - has come to you)
steps.n: This is a particle that adds emphasis to the past tense verb that follows it (جَآءَكُمُ). It makes the meaning stronger and more certain, like saying 'has definitely come' instead of just 'came'.
Added vowels and the weak letter changed from ي to ا because it's between two fathas. This word is the main verb of the sentence.
Added an extra alif to emphasize the action of coming. This makes the word stronger.

steps.m: he came to you all (addressing the people)
steps.n: Added كُمُ at the end to show who is being addressed - in this case, all the people (ٱلنَّاسُ) mentioned at the start of the verse
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed from a verb (sent) to a noun (messenger) by adding و
Added tanween (double dammah) to show this is any messenger, not a specific one
Added 'Al' (the) at the beginning to make it 'the Messenger' instead of just 'a messenger'
Connected the letters and doubled the middle letter to make a noun meaning 'truth'
Added 'the' to make it specific

steps.m: with the truth (describing how the messenger came)
steps.n: Added 'with' at the start and a kasra at the end to show how this truth relates to the messenger's coming

steps.m: from (connects رَّبِّكُمْ to what came before)
steps.n: This is a basic word that shows where something comes from or starts. Here it connects 'your Lord' (رَّبِّكُمْ) to what was mentioned before, showing that the truth comes from your Lord.

steps.m: to care for, nurture, develop something to completion
Lord (one who nurtures and sustains completely)
steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows intensity, making it mean 'the ultimate nurturer/sustainer'
Added kasra to connect with the following possessive pronoun
Added the plural 'your' suffix to show who the Lord belongs to
No actual change since shadda was already present from previous step
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb about feeling safe
Changed from basic 'feeling safe' to religious 'believing' by adding alif at start - this is a common pattern to make new meanings in Arabic
Added فَ to show this belief is a response to what came before in the verse
Changed from statement to command for multiple people by adding وا at the end
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make it a noun meaning a good thing or benefit
Added tanween alif to show this describes how something is better for someone. This connects to لَّكُمْ (for you) in the next word.
Added fatha to show basic pronunciation of the preposition
Added shadda to emphasize the meaning, making it stronger
Added كُمْ to show this applies to multiple people being spoken to
This is a basic conditional particle that introduces a possible situation. In this ayah, it introduces what might happen if people disbelieve.
Added و at the beginning to connect this condition with the previous statement about believing being better for people

steps.m: to reject or deny truth by covering it up

steps.n: Added تَ at the beginning to show 'you' are doing the action, and added ْ (sukun) and ُ (damma) to connect the letters properly

steps.m: you all reject/disbelieve (referring to those who might reject the messenger mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to show this is addressing multiple people. This connects to the earlier part of the verse where it addresses 'O people' (يٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّاسُ)
This is a basic emphasis word that makes statements stronger. It's like adding 'indeed' or 'surely' in English.
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from the previous statement. In this ayah, it connects to the previous statement about believing or disbelieving.
This is the base word Allah, which is a proper name and doesn't come from a standard root. The shadda shows emphasis on the ل.

steps.m: to/for Allah (belongs what is mentioned after)
steps.n: Added لِ at the beginning to show ownership/belonging and ِ at the end to connect it with what comes after. This shows that what is mentioned next (what is in the heavens and earth) belongs to Allah.
This is a relative pronoun that connects to what comes after it. In this ayah, it refers to everything that exists in the heavens and earth.
Basic particle showing location or position inside something
The ى at the end makes it able to connect with what comes after it, showing what thing you are 'in'

steps.m: to rise up, be elevated, be high

steps.n: Adds اء ending to make it a noun referring to something high/elevated
Changes the word to mean multiple heavens/skies by adding ات and changing the middle part to او
Makes the word 'heavens' specific by adding 'the' to it
Adding kasra for genitive case. This word is in the genitive case because it is possessed by Allah (لله) - meaning everything in the heavens belongs to Allah.
Added short vowels (fatha and sukun) to make the word readable
Added 'the' to specify we're talking about THE earth
Added 'and' to connect this with the previous word (السماوات)
Added kasra at the end to show this is something possessed (by Allah in this context)

steps.m: to exist or be in a state

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two vowels in a past tense verb

steps.m: and he was (connected to فَإِنَّ before it)
steps.n: Adding و at the beginning connects this to the previous statement about Allah owning what is in the heavens and earth
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
Added the U mark at the end to show this is the subject
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changed the pattern to show someone who has this quality very strongly
Added tanween (two dashes) with alif at the end to show this word is describing something in the sentence
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: wise, full of wisdom (describes اللَّهُ - Allah)
steps.n: Changed from 'to judge' into a word meaning 'someone who is very wise' - like changing 'teach' to 'teacher'

steps.m: wise, full of wisdom (describes how Allah is)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this describes a quality (how something is)

steps.m: to settle, to be familiar with a place

steps.n: The root becomes a noun meaning the people who are familiar or settled together
The word gets a fatHa (َ) at the end when someone is being called or addressed
The يٰٓ (yaa) at the beginning shows we are calling out to these people directly
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
Changed vowel pattern to create a noun meaning the result of writing
Added ال to make it specific and ِ to show it's possessed by the word أَهْلَ (people) before it

steps.m: do not (negates تَغْلُوا۟ - go to extremes)
steps.n: This is a negative particle that tells people not to do something. In this ayah, it's telling people not to go to extremes in their religion.
Basic root letters got vowels (fat-ha) to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he went too far'
Added ت at start to show 'you' and changed vowels to show present tense
Added ا۟ at the end to show this refers to a group of people
Basic preposition showing location or containment
Added ى at the end to complete the preposition. This word connects to دِينِكُمْ (your religion) to show where the action happens.

steps.m: a way of life with rules to follow
steps.n: Adding the kasra (ِ) and long ي sound turns the basic idea of following into a complete system of rules
Adding kasra at the end shows this word is connected to something after it

steps.m: your way of life/religion (addressing multiple people)
steps.n: Adding كُمْ at the end shows this belongs to multiple people being spoken to
Basic negation particle that negates the verb تَقُولُوا۟ (you say) that follows it
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it
Added vowels to make the past tense base form
Changed to present tense with 'you' prefix
Added plural marker to show multiple people are being addressed. This word comes after لَا (not/don't) in the ayah, making it a prohibition: 'do not say'
This is a helper word that shows something is being done upon or against something else. In this ayah, it connects 'say' (تَقُولُوا۟) with 'Allah' (ٱللَّهِ), meaning 'say against Allah'
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' or 'something that is worshipped'
Adding 'al' makes it refer to a specific deity rather than any deity

steps.m: of the deity, of the god
steps.n: The kasra at the end shows this word is being described by another word
This is the basic form of the exception particle that excludes what comes after it from what came before. In this ayah, it excludes 'the truth' from what should not be said about Allah.
Added alif at the end to make the word flow better with what follows. This word introduces 'the truth' (ٱلْحَقَّ) as the exception to what should not be said about Allah.

steps.m: to be true, to be real, to be right

steps.n: Adding fatha (َ) makes it a past tense verb
The root حَقق becomes حَقَّ (truth) - the doubled letter in the middle shows it's a noun meaning 'truth' rather than the verb 'to be true'
Adding ال makes it refer to the specific truth about Allah that should be said, not just any truth
This is an emphasis particle that makes statements stronger, like adding 'indeed' or 'verily' in English
Adding ما changes the meaning from simple emphasis to restriction, meaning 'only'. This word is connected to ٱللَّهُ (Allah) in this ayah, emphasizing that ONLY Allah is being described.
Added ي between س and ح and vowels to form the noun meaning 'one who is anointed'
Added ُ at the end to show this is the subject of إِنَّمَا (indeed/truly)
Added ٱلْ (the) at the beginning to make this a specific title referring to Jesus (عِيسَى) who is mentioned right after this word in the ayah

steps.m: Jesus (the subject of what follows in the ayah)
steps.n: This is a proper name that refers to Prophet Jesus (peace be upon him). In this ayah, he is being described as the son of Mary and a messenger of Allah.

steps.m: to build, establish, or create offspring

steps.n: Root letters 'build/create' became the simple word 'son' by adding a stop mark (sukun) after first letter
Added helper alif at start to make it easier to pronounce the word
Added damma (ُ) at the end to show this is the subject of the sentence, describing who Jesus is
This is a proper name that comes from Hebrew/Aramaic meaning 'the exalted one' or 'servant of God'. In this ayah, she is mentioned as the mother of Jesus (عِيسَى).

steps.m: Mary (the one to whom Allah sent His word)
steps.n: The word stays the same but shows Mary is receiving something from Allah, like when you receive a gift - the ending mark (fatha) tells us she's the receiver
The و makes it mean someone who does this action as their main role
The ُ ending shows this word is the first part of a possessive phrase with اللَّهِ (Allah), meaning "messenger of Allah"
Changed first letter to ل and added another ل with shadda to make it specifically refer to Allah, the One True God
Added kasra at the end to show this describes what is being spoken about (after the word عَلَى meaning 'about/concerning'). Also changed initial alif to wasl form since it comes in middle of speech.

steps.m: to speak or express with words

steps.n: Changes from 'speaking' into 'one word' - like changing from the action to the thing
Adds conjunction meaning 'and'
Added damma to show this word is doing the action in the sentence. It's the subject of the verb أَلْقَىٰهَآ (He cast it).
Added pronoun 'his' to show possession by Allah
Added pronunciation marks that don't change meaning
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Adding أَ at the start changes the basic verb 'throw' into 'cast/convey' - making it more purposeful
Added alif maksura (ى with small alif above) at the end to show proper spelling of weak letter ending

steps.m: he cast/conveyed it/her (referring to كَلِمَتُهُ - His word)
steps.n: Added هَا with long alif to show the object (referring back to كَلِمَتُهُ - His word) that was cast/conveyed
Basic preposition showing direction or destination. In this ayah, it connects مَرْيَمَ (Maryam) to أَلْقَىٰهَآ (cast it), showing that Allah directed His word towards Maryam.
Added alif maksura (ىٰ) at the end which is just a writing variation of regular alif - it doesn't change the meaning

This is a proper name borrowed from Hebrew/Aramaic, specifically referring to Mary, the mother of Jesus (عِيسَى). The fatha at the end shows this word is being acted upon in the sentence - here it shows Mary is being described as the mother.
Changed the action into a noun meaning the essence or force of life
Added tanween to show this is indefinite (like saying 'a' instead of 'the')

steps.m: and a spirit (connected to كَلِمَتُهُ - His Word)
steps.n: Added وَ to connect this word to the previous description of Jesus as 'His Word'
This is a basic preposition showing source or origin
Added هُ at the end to show this is happening to or coming from him
Added shadda on م for pronunciation because it comes after a word ending in a similar sound
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb about feeling secure
Changed from 'he felt secure' to 'he made himself secure through faith', which is why it means 'believe'
Added وا to show multiple people (men) did the action
Added فَ (meaning 'so/then') at the start + changed from past tense 'they believed' to a command 'believe!' for multiple people
Connected the root letters to form the basic word for deity/god
Added a double L sound (shadda) to change from 'any god' to 'Allah' - the one and only God
Added fatha on the shadda for pronunciation
Added kasra at the end to show possession or connection
Added بِ at beginning to show how something is done or the means by which it happens. Also added hamza-wasl (ٱ) which helps pronunciation when connecting words.
This pattern is used for someone who does something a lot - in this case, someone who delivers messages
Changes from 'rasool' (one messenger) to 'rusul' (many messengers) - a special plural form common for words about people
The word gets a kasra (i) sound at the end because it's connected to 'His' (هِ) showing possession - like saying 'his messengers'
Adding هِ at the end makes it mean 'his messengers' - like adding 's to show something belongs to someone
Adding وَ at the start connects this word to the previous one, like saying 'and'
Added fatha to show correct pronunciation. This is a negative particle that negates the verb تَقُولُوا۟ (say) that comes after it.
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it. This connects two negative commands: 'do not exceed' and 'do not say'.
ت at start shows it's either 'you' or 'she' doing the action
The ُ at the end shows this is a normal present tense statement (not a command or wish)
Adding plural masculine subject marker
Adds a small mark to help with pronunciation
Adding basic vowel marks to make the word readable
Adding alif to make it a number word
Added ة to show we're talking about one specific group of three
Added tanween dammah to make it an indefinite noun
Added vowels to connect the letters and make a past tense verb
Added اِنْتَ to show the person did the stopping by themselves (like saying 'he stopped himself' instead of 'he stopped something else')
Added وا at the end to show multiple people did the action
Added ا to help pronounce the word since it starts with a silent letter
Connected the root letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter to form the basic noun
Added tanween alif to show this word is receiving the action in the sentence
This is a preposition showing direction or purpose
The shadda adds emphasis to show importance
Added كُمْ to show this is directed to a group of people (you all)
This is an emphatic particle that strengthens the meaning of what follows

steps.m: only/just (restricts الْمَسِيحُ عِيسَى to being رَسُولُ ٱللَّهِ)
steps.n: Adding ما changes إِنَّ from emphasis to restriction. In this ayah, it restricts Jesus to being just a messenger of Allah, not divine.

steps.m: a deity, god, or object of worship
steps.n: Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding specific vowel patterns

This is a unique word that underwent special changes to become the proper name of The One True God. The ال was merged with the word and the first letter changed to show this is a unique, definite being, not just any deity.

steps.m: to be worshipped, to be taken as a deity

steps.n: Changes from verb root to noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'
The small alif makes the 'aa' sound longer

steps.m: a deity, a god (predicate of إِنَّمَا ٱللَّهُ)
steps.n: Adding tanween (ٌ) makes it indefinite - 'a god' rather than 'the god'. This word describes Allah in the phrase 'Indeed, Allah is One God'
Added short 'a' vowels (fatha) to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he became one'
Changed from a verb (doing) to a describing word meaning 'one'

1. Changed the alif to a long alif (ٰ) which is common in Quranic writing
2. Added tanween damma to show this is a general description

steps.m: to declare perfection, to glorify, to praise as perfect

steps.n: Changed to فُعْلان pattern to express the complete and absolute nature of Allah's perfection
The fatha shows this is the object of an implied verb meaning 'I/We declare' or 'I/We glorify'

steps.m: (I/We declare) His absolute perfection (referring to اللَّهِ)
steps.n: Adds possessive pronoun 'His' referring back to Allah mentioned earlier in the verse. The word serves as a declaration of Allah's perfection and freedom from having any partners or children.

steps.m: that (introduces what is said about اللَّهُ)
steps.n: This is a particle that introduces what comes after it. Here it connects to يَكُونَ (to be) to show what is being said about Allah.
Added يَ at the beginning to show 'he' is doing the action in the present/future. The ُ (damma) after ك shows this is happening now.
Added َ (fatha) at the end to show this is in the subjunctive mood, often used after 'that' or 'to'
Basic preposition showing direction or ownership
Added pronoun ه with damma to show it means 'him'
Added small و mark (ۥ) after ه which is just a pronunciation helper, doesn't change meaning
Added fathas to create the noun form meaning the result of birth - a child
Added tanween damma to show this is any child in general, not a specific one. In this ayah, it's stating that Allah cannot have a child/offspring.
Starting with the basic preposition ل which shows direction or purpose
The shadda appears because this word comes after إِنَّ, causing the ن to merge with the ل
Added هُ to show who the preposition is directed to

steps.m: for him/to him (referring to having a child)
steps.n: Added the small و mark (ۥ) which is just a pronunciation helper that doesn't change the meaning. In this ayah, it's referring to the concept of Allah having a child, which is being denied.
This is a relative pronoun that refers to things (not people). In this ayah, it's used to refer to everything that exists in a place.
Basic preposition that connects words by showing where something happens. Here it connects the verb تَغْلُوا۟ (go to extremes) with دِينِكُمْ (your religion) to say where the action happens.
Adds اء to make it a noun meaning sky/heaven
Added feminine plural ending to show multiple heavens
Added 'the' (ال) at beginning and changed ending to show possession/location
This is a relative pronoun that refers to things mentioned later in the ayah (things in the heavens)

steps.m: and what/whatever (refers to things in السَّمٰوٰتِ)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it

This is a basic preposition that connects the verb تَغْلُوا۟ (go to extremes) with دِينِكُمْ (your religion) to show where the action happens - 'go to extremes in your religion'. It's one of the most common words in Arabic.
Connected the root letters together and added vowel marks to make it pronounceable
Adding ٱلْ makes it refer to the specific earth rather than any earth

steps.m: of the earth (after فِى in this ayah)
steps.n: The kasra at the end shows this word comes after the word فِى (in/on) in this ayah
Added fathas to make past tense verb
Added و to connect this clause with what came before
Added alif maksura at end to show this is a weak-ending verb. This word connects to بِٱللَّهِ وَكِيلًا ("Allah as Disposer of affairs") to complete the phrase.
This is the basic word for Allah, with shadda on the ل to show emphasis
Added kasra at the end to show possession or connection

Added بِ at beginning to show how to believe (with/in) and ٱ is just a different way to write ال. This word is what we should believe in, connected to the command 'believe!' (فَـَٔامِنُوا۟) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create basic past tense verb
Added ي between second and third root letters and changed vowels to create noun meaning 'one who does the action'

steps.m: a caretaker, a guardian (object of كَفَىٰ - sufficient)
steps.n: Added tanween fatHa to show this word is receiving the action of كَفَىٰ (is sufficient)
This is a special word that makes the following verb negative in the future. It's stronger than just saying 'not' - it means something definitely won't happen.
The ل gets emphasized with a shadda (ّ) because it comes after another ل in the previous word. This is just a pronunciation change and doesn't affect the meaning.

steps.m: to reject or turn away from

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Adding است at the beginning shows someone is actively considering or judging something as beneath them

steps.m: he would consider beneath himself (after لَّن)
steps.n: Changed to present tense with يَ prefix and added َ at the end because it comes after لَّن (never)

steps.m: to anoint, to wipe/touch with hand (especially with oil)

steps.n: Forms the noun 'Messiah' - someone who has been anointed (rubbed with holy oil). The ي makes it mean someone who has this quality.
Adding ال makes it refer specifically to Jesus as the Messiah

steps.m: the Messiah (as the subject who will not disdain)
steps.n: The ُ shows this is the subject (doer) of the verb يَسْتَنكِفَ (will disdain) in this ayah
This word connects two ideas, showing that the first leads to or affects the second. Here it connects 'will not disdain' with 'to be a servant'
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowel to ُ
Changed ending vowel to fatha to show subjunctive mood
Changed the root into a noun by adding fatha and sukun. This word describes what the Messiah (المسيح) would be in relation to Allah.
Added tanwin alif (ًا) to show this is indefinite (any servant) and is affected by يَكُونَ (to be).
This is the base word referring to God in Arabic. The shadda (ّ) shows emphasis on the ل.
Added ل at the beginning which means 'for' or 'to', and the ا from الله is dropped when combined with ل
Added kasra (ِ) under the first ل to help with pronunciation
Added kasra (ِ) at the end to show this word is being acted upon by something else in the sentence
Added shadda (ّ) on the first ل to add emphasis, showing this is a strong statement about belonging to Allah
Added fatha to help with pronunciation. This word negates what comes after it.

steps.m: and not (connects to يَسْتَنكِفَ - will disdain)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this negative statement with the previous one. It connects to the earlier statement about the Messiah not disdaining to be a servant of Allah.
Adding the vowel marks makes it a complete past tense verb

This is not correct - مَلَائِك (angels) comes from مَلَك (angel), not from مَلَكَ (to possess). It's the broken plural form of مَلَك where an alif is added after the first letter. The word means angels and is the subject showing who won't refuse.
Feminine ending common in plural forms
Adding ال makes the word specific to these particular angels being discussed in the verse

steps.m: the angels (subject of يَسْتَنكِفَ - will disdain)
steps.n: The ُ shows these angels are the subject (doer) of the verb يَسْتَنكِفَ (will disdain) in the negative statement لَّن يَسْتَنكِفَ (will never disdain)

steps.m: to draw near, to become close
to bring close, to cause to draw near
steps.n: Doubling the ر makes it mean 'to make something close' instead of just 'being close'
Adding مُ changes it from a verb (to bring close) to describing someone who is brought close

steps.m: the one who is brought close
steps.n: Adding the definite article makes it specific/definite

steps.m: the one who is brought close (as subject)
steps.n: Adding damma for nominative case marking

steps.m: the ones who are brought close (as subjects)
steps.n: Adding masculine plural nominative ending
This is a question word that became a general word for 'whoever' or 'anyone who'. It's used when we don't know exactly who we're talking about.
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this with the previous statement. This word is the subject (doer) of the verb يَسْتَنكِفْ (refuses/disdains) in this ayah.
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters
Adding 'است' at the start shows someone is seeking to do the action for themselves

steps.m: he considers himself above (subject of لَّن)
steps.n: Changed from past to present tense and got a silent ending because it comes after لَّن (meaning 'will never')

This is a basic preposition that shows either physical or conceptual distance/separation from something (like 'from') or what something is about (like 'about' in 'talk about'). In this ayah, it's used with عِبَادَتِهِۦ (His worship) to show turning away from worship.
Added vowel marks to show how the word is pronounced: fatha (َ) on ع and sukun (ْ) on ن. These don't change the meaning.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create basic past tense verb
Changes the verb into a noun meaning the act of worship
Adds 'i' sound at the end to show it connects to the next word (like saying 'worship of')
Adds pronoun meaning 'his' at the end
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Transforms from basic root to Form X verb (استفعل pattern) indicating seeking/considering oneself to have the root quality

steps.m: he seeks to be great/acts arrogantly
steps.n: Adding ي makes this a present tense verb (3rd person masculine singular)

steps.m: and he seeks to be great/acts arrogantly
steps.n: Added 'and' (wa) at the beginning
Added vowels to make the basic past tense form
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added فَ to show this happens after something else
Added سَ to show future tense
Added هُمْ to show who is being gathered
This is a basic preposition showing movement or direction towards something
The ى changes to ي before adding pronouns

steps.m: to him (referring back to Allah in عِبَادَتِهِۦ)
steps.n: Added هِ at the end to show the direction is towards him. This refers back to Allah mentioned in the phrase 'His worship' (عِبَادَتِهِۦ) earlier in the verse.

steps.m: to join or bring things together

steps.n: Added ي between second and third root letters with vowels to show completeness

steps.m: all together (describes how something happens in فَسَيَحْشُرُهُمْ/He will gather them)
steps.n: Added -an ending to make it describe how Allah will gather them all together (works with the verb فَسَيَحْشُرُهُمْ)
This is a special word that comes from the idea of heading towards a topic. The shadda makes it stronger.
Added alif at the end to make it work as a special word that introduces conditions

steps.m: and as for (introduces the first part of ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟)
steps.n: Added fa at the beginning to connect it with what came before. This word introduces the first group being discussed - those who believe.
Added ن at the end to show we're talking about multiple people

steps.m: those who (referring to people who ءَامَنُوا۟ - believed)
steps.n: Added ٱلّ at the beginning to point to specific people. This word connects to ءَامَنُوا۟ (believed) which comes after it, describing who these people are.
This is the basic meaning of feeling secure or having faith
Adding alif makes it mean 'to actively put trust in something'
Added وا at the end to show that a group of people did this action. This word is connected to فَيُوَفِّيهِمْ (then He will give them in full) in the ayah.
This is the basic meaning showing the action of working or doing something
Adding vowels makes this a past tense verb meaning 'he worked'
Added وا to show that a group of people did this action
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this word with what came before it

steps.m: to make something good or right

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed the word pattern to show who does the action or has this quality
Added ات to make it refer to multiple feminine things or people
Added ال to point to specific righteous ones. The ص gets a shadda because it comes after ال

steps.m: the righteous ones (feminine plural) (object of عَمِلُوا)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end because this word is being acted upon by the verb عَمِلُوا (they did/performed)
Added a double letter (shadda) to show he did it fully and completely
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Added فَ to show this happens as a result of what came before

steps.m: so he fulfills completely for them (their rewards)
steps.n: Added هِمْ to show who receives the action (referring to the believers mentioned before)
Changed from root letters to a noun meaning 'payment' by adding vowel marks
Changed to broken plural form by adding و and changing vowels to show multiple payments
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object receiving the action of being given in full (from يُوَفِّيهِمْ)

steps.m: their payments (being given in full to them)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show these payments belong to them. This word is receiving the action of يُوَفِّيهِمْ (he gives them in full)
Root letters got vowels to make it mean 'increasing' instead of just 'increase'
Added يَ at start to show who does the action and when it happens, and ُ at end to show it's happening now or in future

steps.m: and he increases (connected to فَيُوَفِّيهِمْ - then He will give them in full)
steps.n: Added وَ to connect this action with the previous one (giving in full) in the verse

steps.m: and He will increase for them (referring to the believers mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Added هُم to show who receives more (the believers mentioned earlier in the verse)
This is a basic particle that shows something comes from somewhere or belongs to something
Added shadda to show stronger pronunciation. This word connects 'His bounty' to the verb 'He will increase them', showing where the increase comes from.
Root meaning 'favor' becomes a noun by adding marks (fatha-sukun) to make 'bounty'
Added kasra at the end because it comes after the word مِن (from/of)
Added هِۦ at the end to show it belongs to Him (Allah)
This is a basic particle used to introduce a new topic or contrast with what came before. It's like saying 'Now, regarding...' in English

steps.m: and as for (contrasts with فَأَمَّا in previous verse)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this with the previous statement. This creates a contrast with فَأَمَّا from the previous verse, showing two different groups of people and what happens to them
This is the basic form meaning 'who' or 'which' for singular things or people
Adding ال makes it more specific, like saying 'the one' instead of just 'one'
Adding ين at the end makes it refer to many people instead of just one person
The alif at the start is written with a special mark (waslah) because it connects with words before it in pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb

steps.m: he considered himself too good (to accept something)
steps.n: Adds reflexive meaning of 'considering oneself' to the base meaning of 'abstaining'

steps.m: they considered themselves too good (to accept something)
steps.n: Adds masculine plural past tense marker to indicate 'they'
The ُ (damma) in the middle shows this is about having a natural quality
Added است at the beginning to show someone trying to make themselves seem great
Added وا at the end to show multiple people did this action
Added و at beginning to connect with previous statement
Added shadda to make the meaning stronger and show intense punishment
Changed to present/future tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Added فَ to show this happens as a result of something before it

steps.m: so he will punish them severely
steps.n: Added هُمْ to show who receives the punishment (them)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Long ا after second root letter makes this a noun meaning the result of punishment. This word is the direct object (accusative case) of the verb يُعَذِّبُ meaning 'he punishes'.
Adds accusative case ending to make the noun عذاب into the object of a verb
Added vowels and ي to make an adjective meaning 'full of pain'. This word describes عَذَابًا (punishment) in this ayah.
Added ًا at the end to match the case of عَذَابًا (punishment) that it describes
This is a basic negation particle that means 'not' or 'no'
Added و at the beginning to connect this negation to the previous statement. This word negates the verb يَجِدُونَ (they find) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: they find (referring to those who rejected faith)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action. This refers back to those who rejected faith mentioned earlier in the verse.
Added fatha to show the basic meaning of direction or purpose
Added هُم at the end to show this applies to a group of people (them)
This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it shows that extra reward comes from Allah's favor.
The letter م is doubled (has shadda) to add emphasis, showing the importance of where the favor comes from

steps.m: to be below or besides something
steps.n: Basic concept of being lower than or besides something
Adding damma makes it into a word meaning 'below' or 'besides'
Added kasra at the end to show connection to the word Allah that follows it, making it mean 'besides Allah' or 'other than Allah'
Added ال to make it specific and unique. The hamza was dropped due to frequent use. This word specifically refers to the One True God in Islam.
Added kasra at the end to show possession, doubled the ل with shadda, and changed initial alif to wasl form. This word shows who the favor (فَضْلِهِۦ) belongs to.

steps.m: to protect or be close to someone/something
steps.n: The ي is added between the second and third root letters to create a noun meaning 'one who does the action'
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make a basic past-tense verb
Adding shadda turns the verb into an intensive noun meaning 'one who strongly protects'

steps.m: a protector/guardian (object of يَجِدُونَ - they find)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this word is receiving the action of 'finding' in the phrase 'they do not find for themselves besides Allah a protector'
This is a negative particle that negates the verb يَجِدُونَ (they find), showing they will not find something
The و connects this negative statement to what came before it in the verse, showing this is an additional thing they won't find
Changes the verb into a noun meaning the act itself
Adds ي between second and third root letters with vowels to show someone who does this action strongly

steps.m: a helper/supporter (object of يَجِدُونَ - they find)
steps.n: Adds ًا at the end to show this word is receiving the action of 'finding' in the phrase 'they do not find a helper'
Changes from a root meaning 'to point' into a question word asking 'which one?' or 'what kind?'
Adding يا at the start makes this word call out to someone
Adding a small 'u' sound at the end to show this is the start of calling someone
Adding ها to make it a proper way to call out to someone

steps.m: to be social, friendly with others

steps.n: Changes from verb 'to be social' into noun meaning 'people'
Adds emphasis by doubling the ن
Adds ال to show we're talking about specific people, not just any people

steps.m: the people (as the main subject being addressed)
steps.n: Shows these people are being directly addressed in يٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّاسُ (O people!)
This is a basic particle that adds emphasis or certainty to statements. It's often used before verbs to show something has definitely happened or will happen.
Added vowel marks to show pronunciation: fatha (َ) on ق and sukun (ْ) on د. In this ayah, it emphasizes that proof has definitely come from Allah.
The middle letter ي changes to ا because it's a hollow verb (has a weak middle letter). Added fatha vowels to show past tense.
The alif is written with a special mark (آ) to show it's pronounced longer, but this doesn't change the meaning
Added كُم at the end to show the action was directed towards multiple people being addressed
Added damma (ُ) on first letter and sukun (ْ) on second letter to make it into a noun meaning 'proof'
Changed the regular 'a' sound (َ) to a long 'aa' sound (ٰ) for emphasis

steps.m: a proof (that came to you from رَّبِّكُمْ)
steps.n: Added double damma (ٌ) at the end to show this is 'a proof' rather than 'the proof'. This proof is described as coming from 'your Lord' in the ayah.
This is a preposition meaning 'from'. It is a fixed helper word that does not derive from a root.
Added shadda (doubled م) because it comes after a pause in speech. This word connects to رَّبِّكُمْ to show something coming from your Lord.

steps.m: to care for, nurture, develop gradually to completion

steps.n: Doubling the middle letter makes it stronger and turns it into a noun meaning 'one who does this a lot'
Adding kasra (the i-sound) at the end shows this means 'my Lord' or 'Lord of'

steps.m: your (all) Lord (addressing the people mentioned as ٱلنَّاسُ)
steps.n: Adding the 2nd person plural masculine possessive pronoun suffix كُمْ to show the Lord belongs to all the people being addressed
Past tense verb meaning 'he sent down'. The root ن-ز-ل means 'to come down', but there is no hamza here - this is just the basic past tense form.

steps.m: he caused to come down/he sent down
steps.n: Adding أ at start changes meaning from 'came down' to 'caused to come down'
Adding نا changes 'he sent down' to 'we sent down'

steps.m: and we caused to send down
steps.n: Adds 'and' to connect this with previous phrase
This is a helper word that shows something is going towards something else
The ى changes to ي with sukun to prepare for attaching the next part

steps.m: to you all (connected to أَنزَلْنَآ - we sent down)
steps.n: Adding كُمْ shows this is directed towards multiple people being spoken to. This word connects to أَنزَلْنَآ (we sent down) to show what was sent down to them.
Added ُ on first letter and ْ on middle letter to change from action to thing

steps.m: a light (object of أَنزَلْنَآ - we sent down)
steps.n: Added ًا to show this is what's being sent down in the ayah - Allah says He sent down to us a light
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Added مُ at start to make it mean 'the one who' and changed inside vowels to show it's describing something that makes things clear
Added shadda to emphasize the meaning
Added tanween to show this describes how the light (نُورًا) appears or functions
This is a common particle used to introduce a new topic or focus attention on something specific. It's like saying 'speaking about' or 'concerning' in English.

steps.m: then as for (introduces what happens to الَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟)
steps.n: The فَ at the beginning shows this starts a new part that follows from something before. In this ayah, it introduces what will happen to 'those who believe'.
Starting with the basic demonstrative word that became the relative pronoun
Adding ال makes it specifically refer to someone or something
Adding ين makes it refer to many people instead of just one
Adding the correct pronunciation marks and connecting it to the next word ءَامَنُوا۟ (believed). Together they mean 'those who believed'.

steps.m: to cause to feel secure, to make someone believe
steps.n: Adding long alif at start changes meaning to 'make someone do the action'
Added وا at the end to show many people did this action. This word is connected to فَسَيُدْخِلُهُمْ (then He will admit them) in the ayah, showing what will happen to those who believe.
This is the proper name of God in Arabic. It's a unique word that doesn't come from a root like other Arabic words.
Added shadda on ل to show it's pronounced strongly
Changed alif to waslah-alif (ٱ) and added kasra at the end to show possession
Added ب with kasra at the beginning to show how someone relates to Allah in this context

steps.m: he held fast (to something for protection)
steps.n: Added اِعْتَ at the beginning to show someone actively seeking protection or holding firmly to something

steps.m: they held fast (to something for protection)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to show multiple people did this action

steps.m: and they held fast (to بِهِۦ - to Him)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) at the beginning. In this ayah, they are holding fast to Allah (بِهِۦ - to Him)
Starting with the preposition that shows how something is done
Added the pronoun ه to show who/what the preposition connects to. Here it refers back to Allah mentioned earlier in the ayah.
Added a small alif (ۦ) after the ه as a pronunciation guide to show it should be read clearly
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb
Added alif at beginning to show causative meaning
Changed to present tense form
Added pronoun suffix to show who receives the action
Added سَ at the start to show it will happen in the near future

steps.m: then he will make them enter
steps.n: Added connective prefix to show sequence
This is a basic preposition showing location or containment. In this ayah, it connects how Allah will enter them 'into' His mercy.
Added the kasra (ِ) mark under the ف to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'fi'
Changed the final ي to ى which is a common spelling variation that doesn't change the meaning
Root letters combined into a simple noun that means the feeling of mercy
Adding ة makes it refer to one specific instance of mercy rather than mercy in general

steps.m: a mercy (being acted upon by مِّنْهُ - from Him)
steps.n: The tanwin kasra (ٍ) shows this is indefinite (like 'a' in English) and is being acted upon by the word 'from' (مِّنْهُ) that follows it
Starting with the basic preposition meaning 'from'

steps.m: from him (referring to Allah in فَسَيُدْخِلُهُمْ)
steps.n: Added the pronoun هُ (him) to show who/what is being referred to. This connects to Allah mentioned earlier in the verse.
Added a shadda on the م to help with pronunciation when connecting to the previous word

steps.m: to give extra or bestow favor

steps.n: Changes from verb to noun meaning 'favor' by adding specific vowel marks
Added و with fatha to connect this word with رَحْمَةٍ (mercy) that came before it
Added tanwin kasra at the end because this word follows رَحْمَةٍ (mercy) in grammar, showing they're connected by 'and'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks. The final ي changes to ى because it's a weak letter at the end.
Changes to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowel to kasra. The final ى changes back to ي in present tense.

steps.m: he guides them (referring to those who believed in Allah and held firmly to Him)
steps.n: Adds هِمْ to show who is being guided. This refers back to the believers mentioned earlier in the verse.

steps.m: and he guides them (connected to فَسَيُدْخِلُهُمْ - He will admit them)
steps.n: Adds وَ (and) to connect this action with the previous phrase about Allah admitting them into His mercy
Basic preposition meaning 'to' or 'toward'

steps.m: to him/it (referring to the straight path mentioned after)
steps.n: Added the pronoun ه (him/it) at the end to show who/what is being referred to. The ى changes to ي when connecting with the pronoun.
Adding the vowels makes it a proper noun we can use in a sentence
The alif (ٰ) extends the sound but doesn't change the meaning
The tanween at the end shows this is the object being acted upon - what Allah is guiding people to
The root ق-و-م became مستقيم by adding مست at the start to mean 'seeking to be', and the shadda is not for emphasis but is part of the word pattern
Added tanween to show this word describes the path (صِرٰطًا) mentioned in the ayah
Added vowel marks (fatha) to make it mean 'he gave guidance' in the past tense
Added يَسْتَ at the beginning to change from 'giving guidance' to 'asking for guidance' - like adding 'seeks' in English
Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action

steps.m: they seek your ruling (followed by قُلِ)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show they are seeking guidance from 'you' (the Prophet ﷺ). This word is followed by قُلِ (say) in the ayah.
This is the basic concept of speaking or saying something
The root letters are shortened to just قُل and given a ُ (damma) mark to make it mean 'say!' as a command

steps.m: say! (as a command to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ)
steps.n: The ِ at the end connects this command to the next word (ٱللَّهُ) in the ayah, showing that Allah is telling the Prophet ﷺ to say something specific
Changed the root letters into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
Added the u-mark at the end to show this is the subject
Added 'the' at the start to make it specific
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form the past tense verb
Changed from past tense 'gave ruling' to present tense 'gives ruling' by adding يُ at the start. Like changing 'ran' to 'runs'.

steps.m: he (Allah) gives you all a ruling
steps.n: Added plural 'you' as object of the verb
Basic preposition showing location or topic. Here it introduces what the ruling is about (inheritance for those without parents or children).

steps.m: to be surrounded/encompassed from all sides
steps.n: Base meaning relates to surrounding or encircling
Adding the vowel marks makes it a complete past tense verb

steps.m: one who has no children or parents
steps.n: Adding ة changes it from verb to a special type of noun

steps.m: the state of having no parents or children (object of فِى)
steps.n: The kasra at the end shows this word comes after the word فِى (in/concerning) in the ayah
This is a basic conditional particle that introduces a hypothetical situation. In this ayah, it starts a condition about inheritance cases.
Added kasra at the end because it's followed by a word starting with a connecting hamza (ٱمْرُؤٌا۟). This is just for pronunciation and doesn't change the meaning.
Root letters م-ر-أ become the word for 'a man' with special ending ؤ
Double dammah marks (tanween) at the end shows this is 'a man' who is doing something in the sentence
Added alif and small circle to show how to pronounce the word when stopping at it
Added fathas to make it past tense. This word describes what happened to the person (ٱمْرُؤٌا۟) being discussed in the inheritance rules.
This is a special verb used only for negation. It has no root because it's a frozen form
Adding vowels makes it a complete past tense negation verb
Added fatha to show the vowel sound on the letter lam
Added the pronoun هُ (him) to show who the preposition is referring to

steps.m: for him/to him (referring to someone who died without children)
steps.n: Added a small و mark (ۥ) after هُ to show proper pronunciation. This is just a pronunciation helper and doesn't change the meaning. In this ayah, it refers to someone who died without children.
Basic noun form meaning 'child'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic noun form meaning 'child'
Added tanween damma to show this is indefinite (any child). This word is the subject of يَكُن (there is) in this ayah.
Added fatha to show this is a preposition meaning 'for' or 'to'
Added و with fatha at the beginning to mean 'and'
Added هُ at the end to show this means 'him'
Added elongation marks (ۥٓ) to help with pronunciation, but this doesn't change the meaning
This is a basic family relationship noun
Changes the masculine 'brother' word to mean 'sister' by adding feminine markers
The double damma (ٌ) shows this is the subject doing the action in هَلَكَ (died/passed away). It also shows we're talking about any sister, not a specific one.
Basic preposition meaning 'for/to'

steps.m: for her, to her (connected to نِصْفُ)
steps.n: Added 'ha' with fatha to show it means 'her'. This shows who gets the half (نِصْفُ) portion of inheritance.
Added 'fa' with fatha at start to show this is a result or consequence - in this case, showing what follows from the previous conditions about inheritance

steps.m: to divide into two equal parts
half (of ما تَرَكَ - what he left)
steps.n: Changed the verb into a noun that means one of two equal parts. In this ayah, it refers to half of what someone left behind after they died.

steps.m: half (of ما تَرَكَ - what he left)
steps.n: Added damma at the end to show this is the subject of فَلَهَا (then for her is)
This is a relative pronoun that connects to the verb تَرَكَ (he left behind). In this ayah, it's used to mean 'what was left behind' in the context of inheritance.
Added vowels to make a past tense verb. In this ayah, it refers to what someone left behind as inheritance after they died.
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to form the basic pronoun meaning 'he'

steps.m: and he (subject of يَرِثُهَآ - inherits her)
steps.n: Added conjunction و at the beginning to connect this pronoun to what came before. This pronoun is the subject of the verb يَرِثُهَآ (inherits her) in this ayah.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Present tense form showing ongoing action
Adds feminine object pronoun هَآ with long alif to show what is being inherited. This refers back to the sister's inheritance portion mentioned earlier in the verse.
This is a conditional particle that introduces a hypothetical situation. In this ayah, it introduces the condition 'if someone dies' before explaining what should happen with the inheritance.
Basic negative particle that changes present tense verbs to past tense and negates them. Here it negates يَكُن (there is) to mean 'there was not'.
When إِن comes before لَمْ, the ل gets doubled (with a shadda ّ) to make the word stronger, but the meaning stays the same
Added vowels (fatha) to make it past tense 'he was'
Added يَ at the start to make it mean 'he is' (present tense) instead of 'he was' (past tense), with vowel changes to match
Removed final vowel because it comes after لَمْ (not). When لَمْ comes before a present tense verb, it changes it to past tense negative meaning (did not)
Combined the preposition ل (for/to) with هَا (her/it) to show who receives something. This connects to what was left (تَرَكَ) in the inheritance.

steps.m: then surely for her/it (connected to تَرَكَ)
steps.n: Added emphasis by doubling the ل with a shadda. This happens because it follows the connecting word فَ (then/so), making it stronger to show certainty.
Changed from root letters to noun meaning 'child' by adding vowel marks
Added tanween damma to show this is the subject of لَيْسَ (is not) in the ayah, meaning 'he does not have a child'
This is a compound particle made up of فَ (then/so) and إِن (if). It connects conditions with their results.
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas in past tense. This makes it easier to pronounce.
Added ت with sukun to show a female did the action

steps.m: they both (f) were (referring to two sisters in the inheritance law)
steps.n: Changed the ending to show exactly two females did the action. This refers to two sisters mentioned in the inheritance rules being discussed.

steps.m: to bend, fold, or make into two

steps.n: Changed root letters into the number 'two' by adding vowel marks
Added alif prefix to make the word complete
Changed the word 'two' to mean 'two females' by adding تَ and the dual ending يْنِ
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from what came before

steps.m: then for those two (sisters mentioned before)
steps.n: Added هُمَا at the end to show we're talking about the two sisters mentioned earlier in the verse
Adding the vowel marks ُ (damma) turns it into the word for 'one third'
Added ان at the end to change 'one third' into 'two thirds' (dual form)

steps.m: the two-thirds (their share of what was left)
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific and a shadda on the ث because it comes after ال. The final ِ shows this is part of a longer sentence about inheritance shares.
Combined the two basic words مِن (from) and مَا (what) together

steps.m: from what (of تَرَكَ - he left behind)
steps.n: The ن changes to م and gets a shadda because it's easier to pronounce. This word connects to تَرَكَ (he left behind) to show what portion is being inherited.

steps.m: to leave behind or abandon something
he left behind (referring to inheritance mentioned in this ayah)
steps.n: Connected the letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. In this ayah, it's talking about what someone left behind as inheritance after they died.
This combines و (and) with إن (if) to connect conditions
The middle letter و becomes ا when making 'he was' from the basic root meaning 'to be'
Added وا at the end to show multiple people did the action
Added pronunciation marks that don't change the meaning
Changed the vowel pattern to make it plural, showing we're talking about multiple brothers or siblings
Added ة at the end to make it a collective noun meaning a group of brothers/siblings
Added tanween fatha to show it's indefinite and in the accusative case
Changes the singular 'man' into a plural form meaning 'men'
Adds alif to make the word plural
Makes the word accusative and indefinite
Changes the word into a broken plural form meaning 'women'
Adds و at the beginning to connect this word with رِّجَالًا (men) mentioned earlier
Adds tanween (double vowel mark) to make it 'women' as a direct object
Adding vowel marks creates the basic past tense verb form
These are different words - ذَّكَر means 'remembered' while ذَّكَرِ means 'male'. The kasra ending here shows it follows a preposition.
Adds the preposition لِ meaning 'for/to' at the start of the word
Adds the definite article ال to make it specifically 'the male'
Added فَ to show this is the result or consequence of the previous statement about inheritance

steps.m: to be like or similar to something

steps.n: Connected the letters together to form the base word that means similarity
Added vowels to make it a noun showing similarity

steps.m: to get a share or portion of something

steps.n: Root letters combined into a noun with shadda on ظ to show doubling, means 'a portion'

steps.m: of a share (connected to مِثْلُ meaning 'like/similar to')
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to the word مِثْلُ (like/similar to) in the phrase 'like/similar to a share'
Changed root letters into a simple noun meaning 'female' by adding the vowels u- and sukoon
Added feminine ending to specify a single female
Added dual ending to show exactly two females
Added definite article to specify these particular two females. The word is in the genitive case because it's possessed by حَظِّ (portion/share) in the ayah.
The doubled middle letter (shadda) shows doing something thoroughly or with emphasis
The يُ prefix changes it from 'made clear' (past) to 'makes clear' (present/future), showing Allah is doing this action
The ending changed from نَ to نُ to show this is a regular present tense verb, not a special type that would use نَ
Changed the root letters into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'

steps.m: Allah (the One True God) (subject of يُبَيِّنُ - He makes clear)
steps.n: Added ال (the) and changed the word to become the proper name of the One True God. The ل has a shadda showing emphasis. This word is the subject doing the action of making clear (يُبَيِّنُ) in this verse.
This is a basic particle showing purpose or direction

steps.m: for you all (connected to يُبَيِّنُ - He makes clear)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show this is directed at multiple people. This word connects to يُبَيِّنُ (He makes clear) in the ayah, showing Allah is making something clear for all of you.

steps.m: that (connects to تَضِلُّوا۟ - you go astray)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that introduces what Allah is explaining - that you might not go astray. It works with the verb تَضِلُّوا۟ (you go astray) to show the purpose of Allah's explanation.
Adding vowels makes it a past tense verb meaning 'he strayed'
Changes to present tense by adding تَ, meaning 'you' or 'she' goes astray

steps.m: you (all) lose the right path (so that you don't...)
steps.n: Added وا to show multiple people and ُ before it. This comes after أَن in the ayah to mean 'so that you don't go astray'
This is a unique proper name that doesn't come from a root. It's the personal name of God in Islam.
Added shadda on ل to show it's pronounced strongly

steps.m: Allah (as the doer of يُبَيِّنُ - explains)
steps.n: Added damma at the end to show Allah is doing the action of explaining in this ayah

steps.m: and Allah (as the doer of يُبَيِّنُ - explains)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this part to what came before it
Doubles the middle letter to show completeness or totality. Like saying 'completely' or 'totally' in English
Adds kasra at the end because it comes after the word 'with' (بِ). This word connects to شَىْءٍ (thing) to mean 'of every thing'
Adds بِ at the start to mean 'with'. The whole phrase بِكُلِّ شَىْءٍ means 'with every thing'
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter to make it into a noun. The hamza at the end is written above the line.
Added tanwin kasra at the end to show this word belongs to كُلِّ (every/all) in the phrase 'every thing'
Added ي between second and third root letters to show this describes someone who has this quality in the most complete way. When used for Allah, it means His knowledge is perfect and complete.
The ٌ shows this is a divine attribute of Allah, describing His perfect and complete knowledge of everything (predicate after وَٱللَّهُ/and Allah)
